summaries.00406A80: CRT initialization stub. Calls two sub-functions: one that chains to a library initialization routine, and one that registers an atexit handler.
summaries.00406A9A: Thunk that calls library initialization function at 0x0055339f.
summaries.00406AB4: Registers FUN_00406ad1 as an atexit handler to clean up a global MFC object.
summaries.00406AD1: atexit handler that destroys the global COleCntrFrameWnd object at DAT_0063c818.
summaries.00406AEB: Sets the minimap drawing lock flag to 1, preventing minimap redraws.
summaries.00406B02: Unlocks minimap drawing (sets flag to 0), calls citywin handler, then triggers a full minimap redraw.
summaries.00406B28: Locks minimap drawing and hides the minimap window.
summaries.00406B4C: Calculates the minimap viewport dimensions and tile sizes based on the minimap surface dimensions and the map dimensions. Computes scroll offsets for centering the view.
summaries.00406DB8: Converts a map tile coordinate (param_1=x, param_2=y) to minimap screen pixel coordinates, writing to *param_3 (screen X) and *param_4 (screen Y).
summaries.00406E61: Determines the minimap color index for a given map tile, considering visibility, ownership, and terrain type. Returns a palette index (e.g., 0x29 for cursor, 0x30 for unexplored, 0x5d for explored-but-not-visible).
summaries.0040701E: Draws a goto movement line on the minimap between two tile positions (start and destination), using filled rectangles in the cursor color (0x29).
summaries.004070F1: Redraws a circular radius of tiles on the minimap centered at (param_1, param_2) with radius param_3. Optionally invalidates the affected screen region if param_4 is set.
summaries.0040733C: Performs a complete minimap redraw: recalculates viewport, iterates over all visible map tiles, draws each tile's color, draws the goto line, and invalidates the display.
summaries.004074DC: Handles a left-click on the minimap. Converts the screen pixel position to a map tile, then centers the main map view on that tile via thunk_FUN_00410402.
summaries.00407658: Handles a right-click on the minimap. Similar to left-click but also searches for a free civ slot (checking DAT_0066ca84 array at stride 0x3f0 for the first slot with value 0), then centers the view and potentially switches to that civ's perspective.
summaries.0040785B: Calculates the bounding rectangle for the status panel, adjusting dimensions based on screen size, multiplayer mode, and map height.
summaries.0040795A: Invalidates the status panel region for repainting, passing &DAT_00655324 as the rect.
summaries.00407980: Identical to FUN_0040795a — invalidates the status panel region. Likely a duplicate entry point or different call context.
summaries.004079A6: Sets up the main game window: calculates status panel rect, creates the map view surface, and registers callback handlers for keyboard, mouse, resize, paint, and other events.
summaries.00407B31: Locks minimap drawing and saves the current minimap state.
summaries.00407F90: Returns the width of a RECT structure: right - left (param_1[2] - param_1[0]).
summaries.00407FC0: Returns the height of a RECT structure: bottom - top (*(param_1+0xc) - *(param_1+4)).
summaries.00407FF0: Flushes the display buffer by calling FUN_005bbbce.
summaries.00408010: Sets style flags on the window object. Reads the window handle from this+8, calls FUN_005bd05f with the handle and the style parameter.
summaries.00408050: Invalidates a window for repainting. Reads the window handle from this+8.
summaries.00408090: Calls manage_window_C636 to hide or manage the window associated with this+8.
summaries.004080C0: Returns the window width by calling FUN_005bc933 with the window handle from this+8.
summaries.004080F0: Invalidates a specific rect region of the window for repainting.
summaries.00408130: Sets the paint callback handler on the window object. Stores param_1 at this+0xc, returns the previous handler.
summaries.00408170: Sets the resize callback handler on the window object at this+0x18.
summaries.004081B0: MFC library function. Sets the dialog control ID on the control site object at this+0x28.
summaries.004081F0: C++ library function — sets the tied ostream. Here repurposed/reused to store a callback at offset 0x2c of the window object.
summaries.00408230: Sets a callback handler at this+0x30.
summaries.00408270: Sets a callback handler at this+0x34.
summaries.004082B0: Sets a callback handler at this+0x38.
summaries.004082F0: Sets a callback handler at this+0x40.
summaries.00408330: Sets a callback handler at this+0x44.
summaries.00408370: Sets horizontal and vertical scroll amounts on the window object.
summaries.004083B0: Saves the current rendering context and flushes display updates.
summaries.00408420: Swaps the device context handle. Reads the current DC from this+8, calls manage_window_C0AB to get a new one, stores it back.
summaries.00408460: Ends a paint operation by calling invalidate_region with a null rect (0 = invalidate all).
summaries.00408490: Invalidates a screen region. If param_1 is 0, uses the object's own rect (this+0x24 with clip to this+0x48); otherwise uses the specified rect.
summaries.00408580: Blits a rect region to the screen window. Calls FUN_005bc6bb with the window handle and the rect.
summaries.004085C0: MFC library function. Stores the parameter at this+0x110 (different offset from the other instance).
summaries.004085F0: Wrapper that calls thunk_FUN_00408620 to show the window.
summaries.00408620: Shows the window by calling manage_window then a follow-up display function.
summaries.00408650: Calls manage_window_C40A with the window handle from this+8.
summaries.00408680: Wrapper for Win32 SetRect with absolute coordinates.
summaries.004086C0: Wrapper for Win32 SetRect that takes x, y, width, height and converts to absolute right/bottom: SetRect(rect, x, y, x+w, y+h).
summaries.00408700: Fills a rectangle on the minimap surface with a given palette color index.
summaries.00408750: Sets the clear/background color for the minimap surface.
summaries.00408780: Fills a rectangle on the minimap surface with specified position, size, and palette color.
summaries.004087C0: Returns 1 if the tile coordinates (param_1=x, param_2=y) are within map bounds, 0 otherwise.
summaries.00408830: Fills a byte in every map tile record with a constant value. The tile record stride is 6 bytes. Iterates over DAT_006d1164 total tiles.
summaries.00408873: Fills a rectangular region of the map tile data with a byte value. Each tile record is 6 bytes.
summaries.00408903: Copies one byte from each tile record of the source array to the corresponding byte in the destination array. Stride is 6 bytes per tile.
summaries.0040894C: Flushes the display and optionally updates a progress dialog during map generation.
summaries.0040897F: Calculates fertility values for all map tiles and per-continent tile counts. For each land/grassland tile, sums up the yield potential of its 21-tile city radius (food*3 + shields + trade*2 with terrain bonuses), clamps to 1-15, and stores in the tile's fertility byte (offset 5 in the 6-byte tile re
summaries.00408D33: The main world map generation function. Creates terrain from scratch: places continents, fills terrain types based on latitude, runs climate smoothing passes, generates rivers, places polar ice, and calculates fertility. This is the core mapgen routine.
summaries.0040A54E: Cleans up the dynamic stack allocation from the mapgen function.
summaries.0040A564: SEH (Structured Exception Handling) epilog — restores the previous exception handler from the stack frame.
summaries.0040A572: Places a single continent or island group on the map. If param_1 == 0, places a large continent using either small or large land placement depending on the ocean setting. If param_1 == 1, places small islands.
summaries.0040A763: Places a small continent by random walking outward from a starting point, marking 3-tile groups at each step. Walk length is random(0..63), halved if starting on existing land.
summaries.0040A824: Checks whether a tile position is within the valid continent placement bounds. Returns 1 if valid, 0 if out of bounds.
summaries.0040A892: Marks a 3-tile diamond pattern (center + two diagonal neighbors) as land in the scratch layer.
summaries.0040A8DB: Marks a single tile as land in the scratch layer (byte 5 of the tile record) if it's within continent placement bounds.
summaries.0040A92F: Places a large continent by random walking with branching. Similar to place_land_small but additionally has a 25% chance of placing extra 3-tile marks 2 tiles away in each cardinal direction at each step, creating wider landmasses.
summaries.0040AAA4: Places a small island by random walking, marking only single tiles (not 3-tile groups). Walk length is rand(1..16).
summaries.0040AB41: Attempts to create an inland sea (lake) at the given position. Checks that the tile is valid, not on the map edge, and that all 4 adjacent (2 tiles away) cardinal neighbors are land. If so, sets the tile to ocean (terrain 10). Returns 1 on success, 0 on failure.
summaries.0040AC5A: Generates rivers on the map using a random walk algorithm. Each river starts from a random land tile, walks in a semi-random direction, and marks tiles with the river flag (0x80 OR'd into terrain type). Rivers terminate when they reach water or hit the map edge. Unsuccessful rivers are reverted.
summaries.0040BBB0: Begins a new text composition operation on the global text buffer at DAT_00679640.
summaries.0040BBE0: Appends a string to the global text buffer.
summaries.0040BC10: Appends a localized label (by ID) to the global text buffer.
summaries.0040BC40: Sets up a dialog/progress indicator for map generation with the given type parameter.
summaries.0040BC80: Selects a list item in the current dialog, with index 0 and param_1 as a flag.
summaries.0040BCB0: Determines if a grassland tile at (param_1, param_2) has the bonus grassland shield. Uses the formula: returns true if ((-(y + x >> 1) - x) & 2) == 0. This creates a checkerboard-like pattern.
summaries.0040BD10: Returns the maximum tax/luxury/science rate allowed for a civ based on its government type. Anarchy/Despotism: 6, Monarchy: 7, Communism/Fundamentalism: 8, Republic/Democracy: 10.
summaries.0040BDAC: Balances three tax rate sliders (tax, luxury, science) to sum to 10, respecting individual maximums and lock flags. Adjusts rates upward (luxury first, then science, then tax) when sum < 10, and downward (luxury first, then science, then tax) when sum > 10.
summaries.0040BED1: Wrapper that triggers a repaint of the tax rate dialog.
summaries.0040BEEC: Handles changing the tax slider value. Clamps to [0, max_rate], rebalances the three rates, updates the UI scrollbar positions, recalculates totals, and repaints.
summaries.0040C07F: Handles changing the luxury slider value. Same structure as taxrate_set_tax but for the luxury rate.
summaries.0040C212: Handles changing the science slider value. Same structure as taxrate_set_tax but for the science rate.
summaries.0040C3A5: Closes the tax rate dialog by setting DAT_0063cbb0 = 0 (dialog active flag) and invalidating the dialog object cache.
summaries.0040C3CD: Handles checkbox toggles in the tax rate dialog. When a checkbox is checked, unchecks the other two. The checkboxes correspond to locking tax, luxury, or science rate.
summaries.0040C480: Recalculates tax/luxury/science income totals for the tax rate dialog. Temporarily swaps the civ's tax/luxury rates with the dialog's proposed rates, iterates over all cities belonging to the civ, sums up trade income split by tax and luxury, and calculates building maintenance costs. Restores origi
summaries.0040C7D0: Paints the entire tax rate dialog UI. Draws the background, government icon, max rate indicator, tax/luxury/science sliders with percentage labels, income/expenses summary, and turns-to-advance calculation.
summaries.0040CD64: Creates and runs the tax rate adjustment dialog. Initializes the dialog state, adjusts rates to comply with government limits, creates UI controls (3 scrollbars, 3 checkboxes, 1 button), enters a modal event loop, and on exit applies the new rates to the civ's data.
summaries.0040DDA0: Cleanup function for the tax dialog's dynamic allocation.
summaries.0040DDB6: SEH epilog for the tax rate dialog function.
summaries.0040DDC6: Shows the tax rate dialog for a civ. First checks if the civ is active (bit set in DAT_00655b0b). In single-player or when it's the current player's turn, opens the local dialog; in multiplayer for other civs, sends a network message instead.
summaries.0040DEA8: Thunk that calls the dialog object destructor.
summaries.0040DECC: Checks if a civ's tax rates exceed the maximum allowed by their government and, if so, automatically shows the tax rate dialog to force adjustment. Called during government changes.
summaries.0040E017: Displays the "Find City" dialog that lists all known cities. Shows city names with owner names for foreign cities, and disorder indicators. When a city is selected, centers the map on it and optionally opens the city screen.
summaries.0040E38D: Cleans up the dynamic stack allocation from the find city dialog.
summaries.0040E3A3: SEH epilog for the find city dialog.
summaries.0040E3B1: Handles the player initiating a revolution. If the civ is in anarchy, shows the government selection dialog. Otherwise, confirms the revolution with a dialog, triggers civil disorder, sets government to anarchy, and recalculates cities.
summaries.0040EF50: Processes pending Windows messages (message pump). Called in modal dialog loops.
summaries.0040EF70: Returns the font height from the font object at this+4.
summaries.0040EFA0: C++ library destructor for _Timevec. Calls gdi_8514 with the object's first member. Here repurposed to clean up a font/GDI resource.
summaries.0040EFD0: Measures the height of text rendered with a given font, by calling measure_text_858E.
summaries.0040F010: Destroys a surface object. Calls cleanup, then conditionally frees the memory.
summaries.0040F060: Constructs a dialog object. Initializes 3 sub-objects via thunk_FUN_0040fb00, creates a vector of 3 checkbox/control objects, initializes the base control, and sets the vtable pointer to PTR_FUN_0061c054.
summaries.0040F1E0: Destructs a dialog object. Destroys controls in reverse order: the base control, 3 vector elements, and 3 scrollbar/checkbox sub-objects, then the frame window.
summaries.0040F25F: Destroys the base control by calling thunk_FUN_0040f570.
summaries.0040F26E: Destroys the vector of 3 control objects at offset +0x418 from the dialog base.
summaries.0040F2C6: SEH epilog for the dialog destructor.
summaries.0040F320: MFC library function. Stores the parameter at this+0xa4 (different offset from instance 1 at 0x4085C0).
summaries.0040F350: Sets an enabled/disabled flag on a dialog control at this+0xc4.
summaries.0040F380: Invalidates a UI control for repainting. Calls manage_window_8B2D and invalidate_8B00 on the control's window handle.
summaries.0040F3E0: Initializes the base control structure. Calls thunk_FUN_0040f480 for rect initialization, then sets initial values.
summaries.0040F480: Clears all fields of a UI control structure to default values (zeroes, empty rect).
summaries.0040F510: Destroys a UI control. If a bitmap is attached (this+8 != 0), deletes it. Clears the handle and calls manage_window_944B.
summaries.0040F570: Fully destroys a control: if it has a window (this+0x1c != 0), calls thunk_FUN_0040f610 first, then control_destroy.
summaries.0040F610: Detaches and destroys a window from a control object. Only acts if this+0x1c (window handle) is not null.
summaries.0040F680: Creates a text button control. Detaches any existing window, sets the button style, creates the button window, and stores the handle.
summaries.0040F730: Initializes UI control fields with the given parameters (ID, type, parent, rect).
summaries.0040F7D0: Sets the button's owner/parent reference. Gets the window object via thunk_FUN_0040f810, then stores in_ECX at offset +0xbc.
summaries.0040F810: Returns the window object pointer from this+8.
summaries.0040F840: Sets a handler callback on the button's window object at offset +0xc0.
summaries.0040F880: Sets the click callback function pointer for a button control.
summaries.0040F8B0: Constructs a single element of the control vector. Calls control_clear_fields.
summaries.0040F930: Destructs a single element of the control vector. If it has a window handle, destroys it.
summaries.0040F9D0: Creates a checkbox control. Measures the text to determine the checkbox size, detaches any existing window, initializes control fields, creates the checkbox window.
summaries.0040FAA0: Sets the checkbox callback function pointer.
summaries.0040FB00: Initializes a scrollbar sub-object. Calls control_clear_fields and sets initial values (type = 1, checkbox mode, inactive).
summaries.0040FBB0: Destroys a scrollbar sub-object. If it has a window, destroys the scrollbar window, then calls control_destroy.
summaries.0040FC50: Creates a scrollbar control. Detaches existing window, initializes control fields, registers the window class, creates the scrollbar window, sets initial range.
summaries.0040FCF0: Sets the scrollbar position value and updates the scrollbar control.
summaries.0040FDB0: Fills a rectangular region on a surface with a solid color, reading dimensions from a rect structure.

summaries.0040FED0: Ends italic text mode in the global text buffer.
summaries.0040FF00: Adds an improvement/government icon to the text buffer.
summaries.0040FF30: Adds a number to the global text buffer.
summaries.0040FF60: Stores a message string in the message buffer at the specified slot index.
summaries.0040FFA0: Opens a list dialog with the given title and flags.
summaries.0040FFE0: Opens a dialog with extended parameters, passing through to the dialog creation function.

summaries.004100A0: Wrapper that calls thunk_FUN_0056d289 with reordered parameters. Adds 2 to param_5 and passes 0 for two parameters.
summaries.004100CF: Displays a city information dialog for city index param_1. Shows city name, owner, and up to 3 trade route commodity names. Optionally shows embassy information if cheat/debug flags are set.
summaries.004102D5: Cleanup thunk that calls FUN_0059df8a to free a dialog string buffer.
summaries.004102E1: Cleanup thunk that calls FUN_005cde4d to destroy a dialog window.
summaries.004102F4: SEH frame cleanup — restores the previous exception handler from the stack.
summaries.00410302: Centers the map view on the current cursor position (DAT_0064b1b4, DAT_0064b1b0) for the current player (DAT_006d1da0).
summaries.0041033A: Iterates over all 8 map views and calls center_map_on_cursor for each active view.
summaries.004103AE: Calls city window setup, gets the current document, then refreshes the map display for the current player.
summaries.00410402: Sets the map scroll position to (param_1, param_2) on the current map view, temporarily disabling a rendering flag.
summaries.00410464: Checks if position (param_1, param_2) is near the edges of the visible map area and scrolls the map if necessary. Returns 1 if scrolling occurred.
summaries.004105F8: Iterates all 8 map views and scrolls each active view if the given position is near edges. Sets/clears a rendering lock flag.
summaries.004106FD: Converts pixel coordinates (param_1, param_2) to a direction index (0-7) relative to a tile position. Returns 8 if outside valid range.
summaries.00410835: Updates the mouse cursor shape based on the current unit's mode and the tile under the mouse. Supports move-to mode (directional arrows) and goto mode (goto/no-go cursors).
summaries.00410A64: Cancels the unit blink timer and restores the cursor state. If a blink was active and the cursor was in special mode (0x1fe), resets it to normal (0x201).
summaries.00410B23: Starts a unit blink animation by killing the blink timer and setting the cursor to blink mode (0x1fe).
summaries.00410BC3: Handles left mouse button down event on the map. Starts unit selection blink timer or immediately enters goto mode if Shift is held.
summaries.00410CFA: Handles left mouse button up on the map. Cancels the blink timer if still running and refreshes the cursor display.
summaries.00410D98: Centers the map on (param_1, param_2) if the tile is valid. Updates the cursor position globals and redraws.
summaries.00410E0A: Returns 1 if the first map view cursor is in goto mode (0x202 or 0x203), 0 otherwise.
summaries.00410E46: Sets all active map view cursors to goto mode (0x202).
summaries.00410ED8: If currently in goto mode, resets all view cursors back to normal (0x201).
summaries.00410F77: Main handler for map click events. Handles city opening, unit movement orders (goto, directional move), and unit selection depending on game mode (hotseat/multiplayer/single).
summaries.004116C1: Wrapper that calls map_window_click with param_3=0 (left click).
summaries.004116E3: Wrapper that calls map_window_click with param_3=1 (right click).
summaries.00411705: Handles double-click on the map. Opens the terrain improvement info popup for the tile under the double-click, based on terrain type and resource presence.
summaries.00411A13: Moves the map cursor in direction param_1 (0-7). Uses direction offset tables at DAT_00628350/DAT_00628360 to compute new position, then centers and scrolls.
summaries.00411A85: Handles keyboard input in spectator/observer mode (DAT_006d1da8 == 0). Processes Enter/Space for city dialog, 'C' for centering map.
summaries.00411BD7: Opens the cheat menu for the current player.
summaries.00411BF5: Handles keyboard input when a unit is active (DAT_006d1da8 == 1). Dispatches unit orders: Space=wait, B=build, C=center, E=explore, F=fortify/air, G=goto, H=hold, I=irrigate, K=stack, L=airlift, M=mine, O=convert, P=pillage/paradrop, Q=cheatmenu, R=railroad, S=sentry, U=unload, W=wake.
summaries.00411F91: Main keyboard character handler for the map window. Routes to city window shortcuts (if city view), map-level commands (C=center, D=disband, H=history, R=revolution, S=save, T=tax, X/Z=zoom), or unit commands.
summaries.004125C6: Main virtual key handler for the map window. Dispatches function keys (F1-F10 = advisor screens), menu accelerators (0x243-0x25A), cheat commands (0x1B0-0x3B7), arrow keys for unit movement, and city view navigation.
summaries.004131C0: Timer tick handler. In multiplayer mode, calls view update. Toggles cursor blink state if conditions are met.
summaries.0041325D: Toggles the resource icon display overlay on the map.
summaries.004132B7: Calculates and sets the main map view rectangle dimensions, accounting for minimap panel offset.
summaries.00413350: Checks if the current view is in primary mode (view+0x2d8 == 0). If so, calls setup. Returns true if in secondary mode.
summaries.004133C2: Sets the view's background bitmap to the appropriate entry in the view array (DAT_006552a4) based on the current view index.
summaries.0041341C: Identical to FUN_004133c2 — sets view bitmap from the view array. Likely a duplicate for a different callback context.
summaries.00413476: Sets up the map window status bar content: player name, language indicator, and map view filter options.
summaries.004135AB: Handles map view commands: 1=reset view, 2=zoom in, 3=zoom out, 4=cycle filter mode.
summaries.00413717: Full initialization of a map view window: sets initial zoom/filter, creates the view bitmap surface, registers all callback handlers (click, double-click, key, ascii), loads tile sprites, and performs initial layout.
summaries.00413A90: Initializes all 8 map views, sets up the animation timers, and starts the game UI loop.
summaries.00413BD1: Destroys all map views: kills both timers and destroys each active view.
summaries.00414B70: Sets the cursor icon to the specified resource ID on the current view's window handle.
summaries.00414BB0: Returns the height of the current view window.

summaries.00414C20: Sets callback function pointer at offset 4. Returns previous value.
summaries.00414C60: Sets callback function pointer at offset 8. Returns previous value.
summaries.00414CA0: Sets callback function pointer at offset 0x1C (double-click handler). Returns previous value.
summaries.00414CE0: Starts the cursor blink animation by getting the window handle and calling the blink start function.
summaries.00414D10: Returns the window handle stored at offset 8 of the current object.
summaries.00414D70: Sets the status bar text to param_1 using the global string buffer.
summaries.00414DA0: MFC library function — sets the stacked tabs flag on a CPropertySheet.
summaries.00414DD0: Shows a city information popup using the dialog system.
summaries.00414E00: MFC library function — returns whether the splitter window is currently being dragged.
summaries.00414E30: Bubble sort on two parallel byte arrays of size param_1, sorting by values in array param_3, swapping corresponding entries in param_2.
summaries.00414F02: Bubble sort on two parallel int arrays of size param_1, sorting by values in array param_3, swapping corresponding entries in param_2.
summaries.00415040: Builds a file path from param_2 into param_1. If DAT_00625114 is set, prepends a prefix from DAT_006250d8.
summaries.0041508C: Opens a file by name with the given mode. Stores the full path in DAT_006347c0 and error flag in DAT_00634810.
summaries.00415105: Returns the length of a file given its handle/descriptor.
summaries.00415133: Checks if a file exists by attempting to open it in read mode. Returns true if file exists.
summaries.004151E0: Copies technology data (names and prerequisite info) from the game tech table (DAT_00627684 array, stride 0x10) to the editor buffer (DAT_006a1d88/DAT_006a2d28 arrays) for all 100 technologies.
summaries.00415307: Copies technology data back from the editor buffer to the game tech table for all 100 technologies.
summaries.0041541A: Updates editor dialog controls with current tech editor values based on control types (9=text field, 12=combo box).
summaries.0041557B: Reads current values from editor dialog controls back into the editor data buffer. Returns count of changed values.
summaries.00415765: Thunk that redraws the technology editor window.
summaries.00415780: Exports the complete tech list (100 entries) with all properties formatted as text to a file handle.
summaries.00415952: Recounts active technologies and fixes dangling prerequisite references. Updates DAT_00655b1a (active tech count).
summaries.00415A40: Handler for the tech editor OK/Apply button. If no changes, saves to rules file and reloads. If changes detected, shows a notice and returns to editing.
summaries.00415B52: Populates the tech editor's prerequisite listbox showing which units require the selected tech and which techs lead to/from it.
summaries.00415E53: Opens a dialog to rename a technology. Updates the editor name buffer, combo box entries, and redraws.
summaries.0041612E: Shows the advances help topic from the game manual.
summaries.0041618D: Clears the editor dirty flag and invalidates the object cache.
summaries.004161B5: Opens the civilopedia entry editor for the current tech, indexed by era and category.
summaries.0041623D: Handles selection change in the tech editor combo box. Reads current control values, updates the selected tech index, and refreshes all displays.
summaries.00416354: Creates a combo box control in the tech editor for a specific field position. Populates it with tech names or category names depending on the param_1 field type.
summaries.00416734: Creates an edit text control in the tech editor for numeric fields.
summaries.00416828: Renders the complete tech editor window: background image, civilopedia icon, tech tree connections, and field labels.
summaries.00416C9E: Opens the full technology editor dialog. Creates all controls (combo boxes, edit fields, buttons), initializes data, and enters a modal loop until the editor is closed.

summaries.00419170: Main hotseat new game flow. Presents game setup menus (new/load/scenario), generates random world parameters, creates the game, and starts play.

summaries.00419D23: Parses the COSMIC section of RULES.TXT, reading 22 cosmic parameters with clamped ranges into the cosmic parameter array at DAT_0064bcc8.
summaries.00419ED3: Normalizes leader data: sets default leader names, ensures all negative trade/attitude values become positive (absolute value).
summaries.0041A046: Parses the CIVILIZE (advances/technologies) section of RULES.TXT. Reads all 100 technology entries with prerequisites, AI categories, and flags. Fixes dangling prereq chains.
summaries.0041A422: Parses the IMPROVE (city improvements) and ENDWONDER sections of RULES.TXT. Reads 67 improvements (cost, upkeep, tech prereq) and 28 wonder expiration techs.
summaries.0041A5C4: Parses the UNITS section of RULES.TXT. Reads all 62 unit type definitions including attack, defense, movement, cost, hit points, range, domain, tech prerequisite, and flags.
summaries.0041A95F: Parses the TERRAIN section of RULES.TXT. Reads 33 terrain type definitions with food/shield/trade yields, movement cost, defense bonus, and special resource data.
summaries.0041AB18: Parses GOVERNMENTS and LEADERS sections of RULES.TXT. Reads 7 government types and 21 civilization/leader definitions with city/leader names, gender, AI behavior, and government bonuses.
summaries.0041B00E: Master rules loader — parses all sections of RULES.TXT: cosmic, advances, improvements, units, terrain, governments/leaders, caravan commodities, unit orders, difficulty levels, and attitudes.
summaries.0041B177: Detects or prompts for language selection (English/French/German). Reads from INI, presents dialog if first run, saves preference.
summaries.0041B46A: Cleanup thunk for select_language — frees an allocated buffer via thunk_FUN_0059df8a.
summaries.0041B48F: Quits the game: closes map views if active, shuts down subsystems, sets quit flag.
summaries.0041B4C0: Full game engine initialization: sets up paths, timer period, font system, dialog system, label heap, loads rules, loads art assets, loads preferences.
summaries.0041B8B0: Shuts down the game engine: frees subsystem resources and restores timer period.
summaries.0041B8FF: Shows a dialog listing the player's recent tech discoveries (up to 12 most recent techs). Sets multiplayer callback if in MP mode.
summaries.0041BA52: Complete new game setup flow for singleplayer/multiplayer. Presents sequential dialogs for difficulty, opponents, barbarians, rules, gender, civilization, tribe customization, and city style. Initializes the game with all selected options.

summaries.00448F92: Returns the architectural epoch (0-3) of a civilization for city sprite selection, based on which technologies the civ has discovered.
summaries.00449030: Loads TERRAIN1.BMP/GIF and TERRAIN2.BMP/GIF sprite sheets and extracts individual terrain tile sprites into pre-allocated CString sprite objects.
summaries.0044999C: Extracts a 64x32 tile sprite from the current bitmap at the given coordinates.
summaries.004499D3: Extracts a rectangle sprite of arbitrary size from the current bitmap.
summaries.00449A0E: Loads icon sprite sheets (SP.GIF, ICONS.BMP/GIF, EDITORPT.GIF, PEOPLE.BMP/GIF, and DLL resource 0x56) and extracts hundreds of individual sprites for UI elements, buildings, wonders, advisors, people, and misc icons.
summaries.0044AB72: Extracts a 32x32 sprite from the current bitmap.
summaries.0044ABA9: Extracts a square sprite of size param_4 x param_4.
summaries.0044ABD5: Extracts a city style sprite (48x64) into the city style array at the given index.
summaries.0044AC07: Extracts a 64x48 sprite from the current bitmap.
summaries.0044AC3E: Scans a city sprite to find the blue marker pixel position (color index 4) for city flag placement.
summaries.0044AD47: Scans a city sprite to find the name anchor pixel (color index 3) for city name placement.
summaries.0044AE4C: Loads CITIES.BMP/GIF and extracts all city sprites: 6 styles x 4 sizes x 2 wall states, plus city size indicators and style preview sprites.
summaries.0044B239: Scans a unit sprite to find the blue marker pixel (color index 4) for unit flag/shield placement.
summaries.0044B30E: Loads UNITS.BMP/GIF and extracts all 63 unit sprites (9 columns x 7 rows) plus a small HP indicator sprite.
summaries.0044B49E: Master sprite loading function. Initializes the sprite surface manager and loads all art assets: cities, icons, terrain, and units.
summaries.0044C5A0: Initializes the sprite surface manager object. Sets vtable and clears sprite cache pointers.
summaries.0044C670: Initializes sprite cache fields in the rendering surface object.
summaries.0044C730: Initializes a render surface object with default dimensions (0x4000 x 0x4000) and mode flags.
summaries.0044C8E0: Initializes a 104-byte structure to all zeros (26 DWORD fields).
summaries.0044CAAB: Adjusts a pointer by +0x10 if non-null, then calls virtual cleanup function.
summaries.0044CBA0: Destructor for sprite surface manager object. Clears vtable, zeros field at 0x44, calls nested destructors.
summaries.0044CC01: Adjusts pointer by +0x48, calls destructor chain.
summaries.0044CC80: Shows the throne room improvement screen. Checks if any throne room categories still have available upgrades; if so, calls throne_room_add_improvement.
summaries.0044CD77: Thunk to throne room context destructor.
summaries.0044CD9B: Views the current throne room without adding improvements. Creates context, renders current state, destroys context.
summaries.0044CE16: Initializes the throne room display context. Creates rendering surfaces, sets up display area, and checks throne room completion status.
summaries.0044D027: Destroys the throne room display context, releasing all resources.
summaries.0044D162: Loads the throne room DLL (pv.dll) and initializes the throne room rendering context with it.
summaries.0044D296: Manages the throne room improvement selection and animation. Loads DLL, renders current state, lets player select an improvement piece, animates the addition, and updates the throne room data.
summaries.0044D9C1: Displays the current throne room state for viewing (no modifications).
summaries.0044DAB5: Draws the throne room title text ("Throne Room of [Leader Name]") with drop shadow effect.
summaries.0044DB92: Renders the complete throne room scene by compositing all throne room pieces from DLL resources based on the current throne room data.
summaries.0044E790: Presents the throne room piece selection interface. Shows available pieces as clickable regions, loads preview sprites, and returns the user's selection.
summaries.0044F4C9: Handles mouse clicks in the throne room improvement selection screen. Validates the clicked piece and accepts or rejects the selection.
summaries.0044F717: Handles keyboard input in the throne room screen. Accepts Enter key (0xD0-0xD2) in phase 0, plays error sound in phase 1.
summaries.0044F799: Idle handler for throne room. If context exists and in phase 0, invalidates the display for animation.

summaries.0046E6A9: Stops music playback and sets paused flag.
summaries.0046E6C8: Resumes music if enabled. If paused, selects new random track. If disabled, stops.
summaries.0046E8F0: Parses a hexadecimal string into an integer. Handles digits 0-9 and letters A-F (case insensitive).
summaries.0046EA3B: Creates the game color palette. Loads raw palette data, builds a Windows LOGPALETTE, creates an HPALETTE, then maps each of 236 game colors to the nearest palette entry. Stores the mapping in DAT_0066c408.
summaries.0046EE1E: Frees the palette mapping handle if allocated.
summaries.0046EE4E: Applies the color palette mapping to all active player surfaces (up to 8) plus 3 additional surfaces. Then calls FUN_0046f18f to realize the palette.
summaries.0046EF3F: Realizes palettes for all active player surfaces plus 3 extra surfaces.
summaries.0046EFD6: Performs a palette fade-out effect over 10 steps with animation delays.
summaries.0046F06F: Performs a palette fade-in effect over 10 steps.
summaries.0046F108: Restores palette entry flags to PC_RESERVED (0x04) for all 256 entries, then realizes palette range 10-0xEC.
summaries.0046F18F: Realizes palettes for all active players (up to 8) plus 3 extra surfaces. Calls FUN_00408460 for each.
summaries.0046F440: Returns ECX (this pointer). Used to get palette object pointer.
summaries.0046F460: Loads a 640x480x256 BMP file. Supports uncompressed (mode 0) and RLE8 compressed (mode 1) bitmaps. Optionally loads palette entries.
summaries.0046FBF3: Writes rendering buffer contents to a 640x480x256 BMP file. Constructs BMP header, writes palette and pixel data.
summaries.004702E0: Allocates a cutscene object (~0x137c bytes), loads loser art, shows loser text, then cleans up. Entry point for the game-over defeat cutscene.
summaries.004703D4: Constructor for cutscene display object. Initializes multiple sub-objects (GDI surfaces, string buffers, etc.) and sets member at offset 0xf18 to 0.
summaries.004704EC: Destructor for cutscene object. Destroys sub-objects in reverse construction order using SEH unwind.
summaries.0047056B: Thunk destructor calling FUN_005bd915 to destroy a sub-object of the cutscene object.
summaries.004705C9: SEH epilogue — restores FS_OFFSET from stack frame.
summaries.004705D7: Loads "loser.avi" and "civ2art.dll" art resources for the defeat cutscene. Sets up video display surface, font, and rectangle for text rendering. Returns 1 on success, 0 on failure.
summaries.004708DB: Displays the 3-page loser/defeat text sequence. Reads text from ARCHAEOLOGISTS section in game text file, renders with shadow effect, waits between pages.
summaries.00470C0C: Empty function — no-op stub, likely a virtual method placeholder.
summaries.00470C1C: Displays the final page of the loser cutscene text (ARCHAEOLOGISTS3 section). Renders text in upper case with shadow.
summaries.00471020: Scalar deleting destructor for loser cutscene object. Calls destructor, then optionally frees memory if param_1 bit 0 is set.
summaries.00471070: MFC library function returning the active CRichEditView from a CRichEditCntrItem.
summaries.004710D0: Plays the winner/victory cutscene. If param_1 < 0, shows the Centauri beaten version. Otherwise, loads winner video, formats civ name/population/year text, optionally shows Centauri arrival text or standard winner text.
summaries.00471362: Constructor for winner cutscene object. Identical structure to ctor_cutscene_object (FUN_004703d4).
summaries.0047147A: Destructor for winner cutscene object. Same structure as FUN_004704ec.
summaries.00471565: Loads winner video art: checks for "winwin.avi" and "civ2art.dll" using CD-ROM lookup. Sets up video playback surface and rect (2,100,0x27e,0x1e0).
summaries.00471856: Displays the 3-page winner text from "CENTAURI" section. Formats civ stats and renders with shadow (color 0xf1 instead of 0xff for winner).
summaries.00471C2A: Displays scrolling text from "CENTAURI3" game text section. Reads lines, formats and draws them vertically with a scroll offset that accumulates line heights.
summaries.00471DB7: Invalidates the object cache on the winner cutscene's CRichEditDoc at DAT_0062b87c+0x534.
summaries.00471DD8: Loads art for the "Centauri beaten" (player lost Alpha Centauri race) cutscene. Creates a standalone display with static background (no video), renders 5 pages of "CENTAURI_BEATEN" text.
summaries.004728C0: Scalar deleting destructor for winner cutscene object.
summaries.00472910: Sets two members on the cutscene object: this+0x118 = param_1, this+0xa20 = param_2.
summaries.00472950: If the filename has no '.' extension, appends "." (DAT_0062b970) + param_2. Then upper-cases the result.
summaries.004729AB: Replaces the file extension in param_1. If param_2 != param_1, copies param_2 to param_1 first. Strips old extension, appends "." + param_3.
summaries.00472A60: Shows the "DANGER" popup dialog with 4 parameters: sets %0 via thunk_FUN_00421d60, and 3 hex-format params via thunk_FUN_00467580.
summaries.00472AB5: Shows the "DANGERHEX" popup dialog with 4 parameters. Same structure as show_danger_popup.
summaries.00472B0A: Draws a number label on the map at a given tile position. Converts tile coords to screen coords, creates a scaled rectangle, renders the number with shadow (3 passes: offset -1, +3, -2 with different colors).
summaries.00472CF0: Scales a base sprite dimension by zoom factor: result = (param_1 * (param_2 + 8)) / 8, with rounding.
summaries.00472D20: Initializes the unit movement animation data structure at 0x006660xx. Sets unit type/owner params and initializes coordinates to invalid (-50) with default values.
summaries.00472E1D: Initializes unit movement data then places the unit on the map via thunk_FUN_005b345f(handle, param_3, param_4, 1).
summaries.00472E5C: If DAT_006660f7 >= 0 (animation active), stops the animation via thunk_FUN_005b4391(0x800, 1) and sets DAT_006660f7 = -1.
summaries.00472F10: C++ static initializer — constructs a global object and registers its destructor via atexit.
summaries.00472F2A: Constructs a static global object via FUN_005bd630.
summaries.00472F7B: Packs viewport state from 32-bit game structures (0x00655xxx) into 16-bit save file format (0x0066c6xx). Converts zoom level and 11 RECTs from int32 to int16.
summaries.00473064: Reverse of pack_viewport_state — unpacks 16-bit saved viewport data back into 32-bit runtime structures.
summaries.0047314E: Converts a RECT from 4 int32 values to 4 int16 values. Copies left/right/top/bottom with truncation.
summaries.00473190: Converts a RECT from 4 int16 values to 4 int32 values. Sign-extends each field.
summaries.004731D2: Shows a Windows Open/Save file dialog. Configures OPENFILENAMEA structure with filters, initial dir, and flags. Returns true if user selects a file.
summaries.004732A6: Loads unit and city data from save file. Handles 3 save format versions (< 0x29, == 0x29, >= 0x2a) with different record sizes. Assigns sequential IDs to loaded units/cities.
summaries.00473660: Loads the main game packet (global state + per-civ data) from a save file. Handles old format (< 0x28) with individual per-civ section reads, and new format (>= 0x28) with bulk reads.
summaries.00473C12: Writes a null-terminated string to a file — calls fputs followed by fputc(0). Returns 0 on success, -1 on failure.
summaries.00473C68: Reads a null-terminated string from a file into a dynamically allocated buffer. Reads char by char until NUL or max 254 chars.
summaries.00473D5E: Sets the default save file extension (DAT_0066c4e8) based on game type (DAT_00655b02) and param_1 (scenario flag).
summaries.00473E55: Builds the file filter string for Open/Save dialogs. Combines multiple file type filters (.sav, .hot, .eml, .net, .scn, .mp) into the null-separated format required by GetOpenFileName/GetSaveFileName.
summaries.00473FF2: Appends a single file type filter pair (description + pattern) to the filter buffer. Handles types: 0=.sav, 1=.hot, 2=.eml, 3-6=.net. Returns pointer past appended data.
summaries.004741BE: Writes the complete save file. Handles both normal saves and scenario exports. Writes magic header "CIVILIZE", version 0x2c, then all game data sections in order: global state, diplomacy, per-civ, map, units, cities, tech tree, viewport, events.
summaries.0047543C: Quickly checks if a save file is valid and loadable. Opens file, reads magic header, checks version, optionally loads game data to verify. Returns error code: 0=success, 1=not a save, 2=old version, 3=read error, 4=new version.
summaries.00475666: The main save file loading function. Opens the file, reads all sections (header, global, per-civ, map, units, cities, tech, viewport, events), validates data, and reconstructs runtime game state. Returns 0 on success, error code on failure.
summaries.0047758C: Handles the save game UI flow: builds default filename from leader name + year, shows save dialog, writes save file, shows success/error message. Handles special cases for email (timestamped names) and scenario saves.
summaries.00477D8C: The main "load game" UI entry point. Shows file dialog, loads selected save file, verifies unit data integrity, shows title screen if applicable, initializes game state post-load.
summaries.004786F8: Cleanup thunk destroying a surface object.
summaries.00479D20: Static initializer for the per-player viewport array. Constructs array and registers atexit destructor.
summaries.00479D3A: Constructs an array of 8 elements (stride 0x3f0) at DAT_0066c7a8 using vector constructor iterator.
summaries.00479D65: Registers FUN_00479d82 as atexit handler for the civ viewport array.
summaries.00479D82: Destructs the 8-element viewport array at DAT_0066c7a8.
summaries.00479DA8: Static initializer for a global object.
summaries.00479E7E: Scrolls the map viewport to a given Y coordinate, keeping current X. Calls thunk_FUN_00410402(param_1, this->field_2e2).
summaries.00479EAE: Scrolls the map viewport to a given X coordinate, keeping current Y. Calls thunk_FUN_00410402(this->field_2e0, param_1).
summaries.00479EDE: Initializes the map viewport object. Sets zoom mode (param_1), resets center coordinates, initializes viewport scroll state. If DAT_00628048 is 0 (spectator), centers viewport at map center.
summaries.00479FBE: Recalculates all viewport geometry metrics: tile dimensions at current zoom, number of visible tiles, viewport origin, clipping rects. Complex function handling map wrapping and viewport centering.
summaries.0047A540: Converts screen pixel coordinates to map tile coordinates. Handles the isometric diamond-grid subdivision to determine which tile a screen point falls in. Returns 0 on success, 1 if outside viewport.
summaries.0047A6B0: Converts map tile coordinates to screen pixel coordinates. Accounts for viewport origin offset and isometric grid layout.
summaries.0047A747: Calculates coast/shore quadrant data for a tile by examining all 8 neighbors. Determines which quadrants of the tile border water for coastline rendering.
summaries.0047A8C9: The main tile rendering function. Draws all terrain layers for a single map tile: base terrain, coast quadrants, special terrain overlays (hills/forest/jungle), rivers, roads/railroads, irrigation, mines, pollution, fortification, airbase, city walls, fog-of-war edges. Extremely complex multi-pass r
summaries.0047BA1D: Renders a city sprite on the map if visible to the current player. Checks city ownership, visibility flags, and diplomacy before drawing.
summaries.0047BBA5: Draws a single unit sprite at its position. Simple wrapper around thunk_FUN_0056baff.
summaries.0047BBEA: Draws a unit if UI conditions are met (DAT_0062804c == 0 or DAT_00628054 == 0 or DAT_006d1da8 != 1). Otherwise skips drawing (unit hidden during certain UI states).
summaries.0047BC59: Draws a specific unit (by index) at its map position, converting tile coords to screen coords first.
summaries.0047BD04: Draws a unit considering stacking priority. If the unit is the focused unit (DAT_00633e48) or alternate selection (DAT_00633e54), draws with appropriate highlighting. Otherwise checks if the active unit is at the same tile and draws accordingly.
summaries.0047BE63: Draws all visible units at a given tile. Handles unit selection highlighting, visibility checks, and stacking display priority.
summaries.0047C103: Draws a complete tile: terrain, city, units, and trade route indicator. The main per-tile rendering entry point.
summaries.0047C2F2: Checks if X coordinate param_1 falls within a range [param_2, param_2+param_3). Handles world wrap if DAT_00655ae8 & 0x8000 is clear.
summaries.0047C37F: Checks if a tile (param_1, param_2) is within a viewport rectangle (param_3, param_4, param_5, param_6). Checks X range with wrapping, Y range without.
summaries.0047C3E0: Checks if a tile is within the current viewport's visible area. Delegates to is_tile_in_viewport_rect with viewport bounds from this object.
summaries.0047C443: Draws city name labels on the map for all visible cities. Renders names with shadow and colored by city owner, positioned below the city sprite.
summaries.0047C7AA: Calculates the screen rectangle covering a group of tiles centered on (param_1, param_2) with radius param_3. Returns the intersection with the viewport clipping rect.
summaries.0047C869: Redraws all tiles in a diamond-shaped area centered on (param_1, param_2) with radius param_3. Iterates in row order, handles isometric coordinate staggering.
summaries.0047C9D4: Redraws all visible tiles in the viewport. Iterates through every tile position in the viewport grid.
summaries.0047CAEA: Calculates the screen rectangle for a tile group and invalidates it (marks for repaint).
summaries.0047CB26: Invalidates a single tile (radius 0) for repaint.
summaries.0047CB50: Performs a paint cycle: begin paint, poll network (if MP), end paint, begin paint again, poll again. Double-buffer flip sequence.
summaries.0047CBB4: Redraws a map area and optionally invalidates it. Handles cursor blink rendering for the active unit position. Sets DAT_006ad908 flag during drawing.
summaries.0047CCED: Updates a single map tile (radius 0, current player, with invalidate).
summaries.0047CD1F: Updates map tiles within radius 1 of a position (current player, with invalidate).
summaries.0047CD51: Performs a full map redraw: recalculates viewport geometry, redraws all tiles, refreshes paint buffers, and optionally redraws the minimap.
summaries.0047CE1E: Updates a map area for all active players (all viewports in MP).
summaries.0047CEA6: Updates a single tile for all active players.
summaries.0047CF22: Updates radius-1 area around a tile for all active players.
summaries.0047CF9E: Redraws entire map for all active players.
summaries.0047DCE0: Constructor for the map window object. Initializes base class and 4 sub-objects, sets vtable.
summaries.0047DE10: MFC library destructor for CBitmapButton. Destroys 4 bitmap sub-objects and the frame window base.
summaries.0047DEBE: Destructs the COleCntrFrameWnd base member at *(EBP-0x10).
summaries.0047DF20: Sets the global sprite rendering scale to (param_1 + 8) / 8. Used for zoom-dependent sprite sizing.
summaries.0047DFB0: Scales a value by the current viewport zoom level (this+0x2e4).
summaries.0047DFF0: Sets sprite scale to match current viewport zoom.
summaries.0047E030: Checks if a tile is visible to any active player viewport that can see the tile (has fog-of-war visibility).
summaries.0047E0E5: Enqueues a drawing command into the stacked draw circular buffer (100 entries at DAT_006ad920, stride 0x40). Used for MP deferred rendering. Handles queue full condition by flushing.
summaries.0047E2B3: Dequeues and processes one drawing command from the stacked draw buffer. Handles unit movement animation (type 0x70), tile updates (0x71-0x76), combat display (0x7c, 0x7d), and counter increment (0xa3).
summaries.0047E94E: The main network polling function. Processes all incoming multiplayer messages: game state synchronization, unit/city creation/deletion, combat resolution, diplomacy, chat, save/load, and dozens more message types. This is by far the largest and most state-mutating function in the block.
summaries.00482305: Thunk that calls FUN_0059df8a (likely a CRT destructor or cleanup). No-op wrapper.
summaries.00482311: Identical thunk to FUN_0059df8a. Duplicate destructor stub.
summaries.00482327: SEH unwind handler. Restores the FS:[0] exception chain from saved frame pointer.
summaries.00482337: Calls thunk_FUN_0047e94e(1,0) then if DAT_006c9178 is nonzero, sets bit 0x400 in DAT_006ad678[0xf] and invalidates the object cache. Used to force a repaint when the network state indicates it.
summaries.0048237D: Timer-based conditional invalidate. If more than 0x4B0 (1200) ms have elapsed since last invalidation, invalidates the display and resets the timer.
summaries.004823D6: Copies 7 dwords from param_1+0x10..0x28 into param_2[0..6]. Likely copies a network message payload struct.
summaries.00482470: Conditional invalidation. If DAT_006ad664 is nonzero and thunk_FUN_005ae006 returns 0 (checking if any human players remain) and DAT_006ad308 > 1, skips. Otherwise invalidates display.
summaries.004824E3: Handles server quitting in multiplayer. Sets DAT_006ad698=1, purges disconnected players, manipulates the player bit mask (DAT_00655b0b), transfers server role to next available client, sends network messages.
summaries.00482724: Checks if DAT_006ad698 == 2 (quit confirmed), and if so invalidates the display.
summaries.0048276D: Timer check: if DAT_006c9038 == 0 and elapsed time <= DAT_006ad8b8 * 60, skip. Otherwise invalidate.
summaries.004827D5: Same structure as timer_check_1 but checks DAT_006c8ff0 instead of DAT_006c9038.
summaries.0048283D: Timer check: if DAT_006ad664 != 0 and not timed out, skip. Otherwise invalidate.
summaries.004828A5: Handles the entire client-to-server transfer process in multiplayer. Disconnects from old server, reconnects to new one, remaps player slots, synchronizes game state.
summaries.0048308F: Re-links event string pointers after a memory operation (deserialization or reallocation). Walks a linked list of event records (stride 0x1C4) and patches up string pointers at various offsets within each record.
summaries.00484CC0: Initializes scenario variables to defaults. Sets DAT_0064bcb2=10, zeros all others.
summaries.00484D3B: Sets DAT_00628044 = 0, clearing the "game active" flag.
summaries.00484D52: Initializes the game display. If DAT_006ad684 == 0, calls FUN_00421bd0 first. Then calls display update functions.
summaries.00484D85: Handles the "quit game" action. Shows REALLYQUIT dialog, handles cleanup for single/hot-seat/multiplayer modes, optionally destroys the player civ, sets end-of-game flags.
summaries.00484FEC: Calculates the in-game year from a given turn number using the turn-to-year calendar tables (epoch table at DAT_0062c490). Handles scenario custom calendars (DAT_0064bcb4/b6).
summaries.00485208: Advances the year display in the UI, showing appropriate year strings. Handles both standard (AD/BC) and scenario calendar modes.
summaries.004853E7: Calculates power rankings for all civilizations, updates the power graph history, determines the leading civilization, and triggers war declarations based on power dynamics.
summaries.00485C15: Main barbarian spawning logic. Spawns barbarian units in wilderness and near cities based on difficulty, turn number, and barbarian activity level. Handles both land and sea barbarians.
summaries.004868FB: Applies global warming effects to the map. Iterates all tiles, for tiles meeting criteria (terrain type < 4, adjacent land check), degrades terrain (removes improvements, changes terrain type) in a pattern based on the warming counter param.
summaries.00486C2E: Updates the global pollution counter. Calculates pollution level from shield production minus recycling, adjusts DAT_00655b0e (pollution counter). If counter exceeds 16, triggers global warming via apply_global_warming.
summaries.00486E15: Calculates the research cost for a given tech level. Uses triangular number formula: sum of (7-difficulty)*i for i=0..param_1, divided by 2, plus 1.
summaries.00486E6F: Checks if a civilization has accumulated enough research to advance to the next tech. If so, increments DAT_0064ca9e and optionally triggers the tech choice dialog.
summaries.00487007: Refreshes map visibility for all tiles and unit positions. Sets DAT_006ad699=0 during refresh, 1 after.
summaries.0048710A: Resets unit movement/status for a new turn. Calls refresh_map_visibility, optionally calls thunk_FUN_005b6787 on all units (param==-3). Resets movement points, clears fortify-in-progress, handles spy countdown timers.
summaries.00487371: Master end-of-turn processing. Spawns barbarians, updates pollution, calculates power rankings, checks tech advance, increments turn counter, resets units, handles reputation decay, triggers random events, and checks for space race victory.
summaries.00487A41: Processes a complete civ turn: unit orders, city production, tax/science/happiness calculations, AI attitude adjustments, tribute demands, and military posture evaluation.
summaries.00488937: Performs auto-save. Generates save filename from civ name, deletes old backup, renames current auto-save to backup, saves game.
summaries.00488A45: Checks if a trade route path exists between two points. Uses pathfinding to trace a route, checking for blocking enemy units and road connectivity. Returns 0 (blocked), 1 (partial road), or 2 (full road).
summaries.00488CEF: Heals all units belonging to the specified civ. Calculates healing amount based on unit location (city, fortress, field), terrain type, and veteran status. Handles both land and air unit healing.
summaries.00489292: Checks if a civilization has reached a population milestone (every 10k up to 100k, every 100k after). Shows "FERTILE" notification and optionally prompts tax rate adjustment dialog if treasury is declining.
summaries.00489553: Executes a complete civilization turn: city processing, unit healing, per-civ turn logic, AI decisions, population milestones, auto-save. Clamps treasury to [0, 30000].
summaries.004897FA: Starts human turn if not already active or if param forces it. Sets UI state flags and triggers display updates.
summaries.00489859: Selects the next unit needing orders. Calls the unit finder, scrolls the map to the unit, activates it for input.
summaries.00489A0D: Activates the current unit for player input. Handles transition from "no unit selected" to active unit state.
summaries.00489B9B: Scrolls map to (param_1, param_2) if not already centered there.
summaries.00489BE2: Checks tutorial advice conditions for the currently selected unit. Suggests building cities, mining, irrigating, or building roads based on terrain and game state.
summaries.0048A004: Processes orders for the currently selected human unit. Handles tutorial prompts for damaged units, executes automated movement, processes settler orders, and loops until the unit needs manual input or all orders are done.
summaries.0048A374: Waits for player input when no unit is active. Loops processing messages until the player takes an action.
summaries.0048A416: Main loop for the human player's turn. Manages unit selection, order processing, waiting for input, and end-of-turn conditions.
summaries.0048A92D: Calculates max and min demographic values across all alive civs for 4 categories. Stores extremes in DAT_00673afc (max) and DAT_00673af8 (min).
summaries.0048AA24: Checks various turn-based advisor triggers: council meeting every 50 turns, tutorial messages for first turns, "one city" warning, expansion reminders.
summaries.0048AEDC: Checks for game-ending conditions: retirement at year 2000/2020, scenario end year. Sets DAT_00655af0 |= 2 and DAT_0064b1ac = 5 on game end.
summaries.0048B165: Shows the victory/defeat screen based on DAT_0064b1ac type (1=space race win, 2=space race other, 3=conquest, 4=domination, 5=retirement). Shows hall of fame. Asks "keep playing?" in singleplayer.
summaries.0048B340: Main game loop for singleplayer and hot-seat modes. Iterates through all civs each turn, processes AI and human turns, handles victory conditions, events log, and game end.
summaries.0048BF2D: Destructor stub called during game_loop_singleplayer cleanup.
summaries.0048BF51: MP client polling handler. Calls timeout check, then if any network event or quit condition is detected, invalidates display.
summaries.0048BFEC: Main game loop for MP client mode. Waits for server signals, processes local turns when notified, handles server transfer and disconnection.
summaries.0048C9F3: Main game loop for MP server mode. Orchestrates all player turns, sends turn signals to clients, processes AI civs locally, handles human turns with network synchronization.
summaries.0048D9AD: Sends a turn signal to a specific client in MP. If not in deferred mode (DAT_006c31d0), sends message 0x2B. Records timestamp in DAT_00673b00.
summaries.0048DA51: Checks if a client has timed out (>3599ms since last signal). If DAT_006c9090 is 0, increments timeout counters. If DAT_006c9090 is 1, retries the turn signal.
summaries.0048DAB9: Handles player disconnections in MP. For client mode: detects lost server. For server mode: detects lost clients, removes them from the game, updates player masks.
summaries.0048DE75: Scans for disconnected players (DAT_006c3188 nonzero) and removes them from the game. Clears their bits in DAT_00655b0b and DAT_00654fb0.
summaries.004904C0: Wrapper that calls thunk_FUN_0051d564(param_1, param_2, 0, param_3, param_4). Displays a tutorial dialog on a city screen.
summaries.00490560: MFC library function. Returns *(this + 0x159C), which stores the currently tracked city/unit index.
summaries.00490590: Initializes the chat window display. Validates selection index, copies item list, sets up scrollbar.
summaries.004906FD: Draws detailed terrain information in the chat/info window. Shows terrain type, food/shields/trade yields, special resources, and upgrade paths for the selected terrain tile.
summaries.00491C20: Handles item selection in the chat/civilopedia window. Updates display to show selected item info.
summaries.00491D61: Finds the display index for a given item ID by searching the item list at DAT_006a74bc. Returns -1 if not found.
summaries.004923C0: Calls FUN_005bd120 with the object's internal buffer pointer.
summaries.004923F0: Handles chat window commands: cycling through civ names (0x2B0), civ leader names (0x2B1), loading chat macros (0x2B2-0x2B4). Updates the chat input text.
summaries.0049275A: Loads a chat macro from file. Opens the file, reads a line at the current offset, advances the offset.
summaries.004929C0: MFC library Realloc with param. Opens/seeks to position.
summaries.00492AB0: Gets text length from chat edit control via Windows message.
summaries.00492AE0: Sets text in chat edit control via Windows message.
summaries.00492B20: Sets selection range in chat edit control.
summaries.00492B60: Negates the priority of an AI goal entry. Takes absolute value then negates it, effectively flipping the sign.
summaries.00492C15: Removes AI goal_b entries near a specified location. Scans 16 goal_b slots, if the goal type matches param_2 and distance <= param_5, clears the entry.
summaries.00492D18: Recursively shifts AI goal_a entries down by one position starting from param_2. Moves each entry to the next slot to make room for insertion.
summaries.00492DD0: Shifts AI goal_b entries down (iterative). Moves entries from index 14 down to param_2, shifting each up by one slot.
summaries.00492E60: Finds the maximum absolute priority among AI goal_a entries matching the given location and type. Returns 0 if no match.
summaries.0049301B: Adds a goal to AI goal list A. If duplicate exists with higher priority, skips. If list full, inserts by shifting lower-priority entries down. Also redirects nearby idle units to new goals of type 2/3.
summaries.004933F2: Adds a goal to AI goal list B (16 entries). Same insertion logic as ai_add_goal_a but for the smaller B list.
summaries.00493602: Decays AI goal priorities (negates negative ones = removes expired goals) and merges goal list B into goal list A.
summaries.0049376F: Clears all 16 entries in AI goal list B for a given civ. Sets type to 0xFF and priority to 0.
summaries.00493B10: Returns the noun name for a civilization (e.g., "Romans"). Uses custom name if set (negative index in name table), otherwise looks up from string resources.
summaries.00493BA6: Returns the leader title for a civilization based on civ type and government. Uses custom title if set.
summaries.00493C7D: Returns the people name for a civilization (e.g., "Roman"). Uses custom name if set.
summaries.00493D13: Returns the adjective form of a civilization name. Uses custom name if set.
summaries.00493E50: Plays the intelligence advisor music if DAT_0062ca38 is nonzero.
summaries.00493E83: Shows the initial intelligence advisor screen. Creates the dialog, shows results if successful.
summaries.00493F0F: Opens the intelligence advisor for a foreign civ. Allocates advisor object, initializes sprites, music, and display.
summaries.00494148: Closes the intelligence advisor. Tears down display, frees object, stops music.
summaries.004941EE: Plays an animation frame in the intel advisor (for param types 2, 3, 4). Validates frame range before playing.
summaries.004942A3: Constructor for the intel advisor display object. Initializes sub-objects with SEH frame protection.
summaries.004943C9: Destructor for intel advisor object. Destroys sub-objects in reverse order with SEH.
summaries.0049444F: Destructor stub for intel object sub-component 5. Calls FUN_005dd1a0.
summaries.0049445E: Destructor stub for intel object sub-component 4. Destroys a _Timevec at object+0x658.
summaries.0049446D: Destructor stub for intel object sub-component 3.
summaries.0049447C: Destructor stub for intel object sub-component 2.
summaries.0049448B: Destructor stub for intel object sub-component 1.
summaries.0049449A: Destructor stub for intel object sub-component 0.
summaries.004944BB: Initializes the intelligence advisor display tile. Loads DLL resources, sets up tile sprites, background, main art, military display, science display, portrait, and video.
summaries.00494704: Sets up the initial display state for the intel advisor, including sprite loading and animation start.
summaries.004947F0: Tears down the intel advisor display. Restores previous display state.
summaries.0049488E: Initializes tile sprites (40x40 tiles) from DLL resource 299.
summaries.00494949: Initializes the main art panel with civ flag and leader portrait.
summaries.00494B3A: Cleanup stub for intel_init_main_art. Calls FUN_005c656b.
summaries.00494B5F: Initializes the military ranking display for the intel advisor. Shows rank icons based on civ power ranking.
summaries.00494DAE: Recursively counts the depth of a technology in the tech tree (number of prerequisite techs). Uses DAT_0062768e (tech prerequisite table, 2 prereqs per tech, stride 0x10).
summaries.00494E2A: Initializes the science/technology display panel for the intel advisor. Shows the top 6 technologies of the target civ, sorted by tech tree depth, with formatted multi-line names.
summaries.00495BFA: Destroys a vector of 5 CString objects (each 0x3C bytes).
summaries.00495C35: Initializes the leader portrait display for the intel advisor.
summaries.00495E0C: Initializes video playback for the intel advisor (leader animations). Loads .AVI file for the civ's leader.
summaries.00496125: Plays one frame of the advisor video animation. Handles idle (type 0) and active (type 2-4) animations.
summaries.0049621D: Constructor for the intelligence advisor dialog. Initializes sub-objects and vtable.
summaries.0049632A: MFC destructor for CCommandLineInfo. Destroys sub-objects in reverse construction order.
summaries.00496402: Creates the main intel advisor window. Sets up the panel layout, creates buttons, renders the grid, shows the dialog.
summaries.004964B3: Creates OK and Cancel buttons for the intel advisor dialog, positioned at the bottom.
summaries.004965FF: Shows the intel advisor results and writes the selected attitude value back to DAT_0064ca92 for the target civ.
summaries.004966C4: Renders the 3x7 grid of attitude icons for the intelligence advisor. Each cell shows a small icon representing a diplomatic attitude option.
summaries.00496C3C: Handles click on the intel advisor grid. Calculates which cell was clicked, highlights the new selection and unhighlights the old one.
summaries.00496E5D: Handles double-click on the intel grid. Calls grid_click then confirms selection (button 100 = OK).
summaries.00496ECF: Handles button press in intel advisor. OK button (0x65) saves the selected grid cell to the object's result field, adjusting for government type if needed.
summaries.00497BF0: Destructor + delete for intel advisor object.
summaries.00497C90: Returns pointer to RECT at in_ECX + 0x24.
summaries.00497CC0: Sets a bitmap reference via internal buffer.
summaries.00497D00: Sets font size via internal object.
summaries.00497D40: Creates a button control with custom style flags.
summaries.00497DA0: Reads a null-terminated string from a file, up to 79 chars. Reads one extra byte (separator) after string.
summaries.00497E0F: Writes a null-terminated string to file, followed by 0x1A separator byte.
summaries.00497EA0: Creates a DirectSound buffer. Releases existing buffer if any, creates new one with given format. Sets up tracking fields (owner byte, size, position).
summaries.00497FA0: Initializes a sound buffer from an externally-provided pointer. Does not own the buffer.
summaries.00497FF3: Locks a DirectSound buffer for writing. Reports error via thunk_FUN_00589ef8 if lock fails.
summaries.0049805E: Clears sound buffer and lock pointers to 0.
summaries.00498082: Resets sound buffer play position to 0, restores available size.
summaries.004980A9: Unlocks a DirectSound buffer if currently locked.
summaries.004980EC: Destroys a DirectSound buffer. Unlocks if locked, releases buffer, clears all fields.
summaries.00498159: Consumes bytes from a sound buffer. Advances the read position and decreases remaining size. Returns pointer to consumed data.
summaries.00498310: Checks if a player has a password set. If not, calls mp_set_password to create one.
summaries.0049836A: Implements the password set/change dialog for multiplayer. If password exists, verifies old password first, then prompts for new one.
summaries.004985D0: Destructor stub for mp_set_password stack cleanup.
summaries.004985F4: Verifies a player's password. Shows password prompt, compares with stored (decrypted) password. Shows error on mismatch.
summaries.0049875F: Destructor stub for mp_verify_password stack cleanup.
summaries.00498784: Initializes the password system. Fills the 256-byte password buffer with random data, clears all 8 player passwords (first byte = 0), clears password flags, then encrypts.
summaries.0049882B: Scans all 8 player password slots. If a password is set (first byte != 0), sets the corresponding flag in DAT_00673d18 and DAT_00673d38 to 1. Otherwise 0.
summaries.004988B8: Encrypts the password buffer using a simple rotation + XOR cipher. Each byte is rotated right 3, combined with previous byte's shifted bits, then XORed with its index.
summaries.00498943: Decrypts the password buffer. Reverse of mp_encrypt_passwords: XOR with index, then rotate left 3.
summaries.004989D3: Prepares the password dialog by setting the title string from the civ name and a string resource ID.
summaries.00498A5C: Handles player turn authentication in multiplayer. If no password set and mode is email: prompts to set one. If password is set: loops verifying password until correct or cancelled.
summaries.00498D40: Loads city production preferences from CITYPREF.TXT. Reads NODEFEND and AUTOBUILD sections. Builds priority list of up to 32 preferred buildings.
summaries.00498E8B: The massive AI city production decision function. Evaluates all possible buildings, units, and wonders for a given city, scoring each based on dozens of factors including military threat, diplomatic situation, economy, technology level, government type, and strategic position. Returns the best produ
summaries.004A2020: Closes the currently open text file (DAT_0062cd20) if non-null, and sets it to null.
summaries.004A2055: Opens a text file and seeks to a named section (prefixed with @). The section name is uppercased for case-insensitive matching. Searches game dir first, then scenario dir.
summaries.004A2379: Tries to open a text file section. If it fails, temporarily swaps the game path with the scenario path and tries again.
summaries.004A23FC: Reads the next line from the open text file into DAT_00679640 (255 char buffer). Trims whitespace. If param_1==1, also strips comments/trailing text.
summaries.004A24B1: Extracts the next comma-delimited token from the current parse position. Advances DAT_00673f14 past the comma.
summaries.004A2534: Parses next token and converts it to a numeric value (discards it). Used to skip over fields in text files.
summaries.004A2552: Advances the token pointer (DAT_00673f14) to the end of the current line buffer.
summaries.004A257A: Reads next line from file and returns it as a string pointer (via FUN_00428a95 which likely allocates/interns the string).
summaries.004A25AA: Parses next comma-delimited token and returns it as an interned string/int.
summaries.004A25D5: Parses next token as a binary string (sequence of '0' and '1' chars) and returns the numeric value as a short.
summaries.004A2645: Opens a text file at a given section and skips param_3 lines. Closes the file afterward.
summaries.004A26BF: Parses next token and allocates a string copy with given max length.
summaries.004A28B0: Calculates civilization score for param_1. Sums population, wonders, future techs, pollution penalty, map exploration, happiness, difficulty bonus, and space race bonus. Special handling for scenarios with custom scoring.
summaries.004A3060: Loads game rules data into scenario editor buffers. Copies tribe names, leader names, government names, and commodity names into editor-specific arrays for editing.
summaries.004A3426: Copies scenario editor values back into the game's rule tables. Reverse of load_rules_to_editor.
summaries.004A3640: Updates scenario editor UI controls from the game data buffers. Handles text fields (type 9) and dropdown lists (type 0xC).
summaries.004A3757: Reads values from scenario editor UI controls back into the editor data buffers. Reverse of update_editor_controls_from_game.
summaries.004A3889: Wrapper that calls paint_editor_screen to refresh the scenario editor display.
summaries.004A38A4: Writes government rules data to a file in RULES.TXT format. Formats names and titles with column alignment.
summaries.004A3A21: Writes leader/tribe rules data to file. Reads existing line from a template, then formats and writes updated values. Preserves trailing text from original file.
summaries.004A41D8: Writes commodity names to a rules file.
summaries.004A4249: Saves scenario editor changes to rules file. Updates game from editor controls, writes rules sections (governments, leaders, commodities), shows error on failure, then invalidates editor cache.
summaries.004A4304: Shows a dialog to edit commodity names. Creates a list control with 16 entries, allows user to edit names, and writes changes back.
summaries.004A44C6: Shows dialog to edit government names (7 entries). Similar structure to edit_commodity_names_dialog.
summaries.004A468B: Shows dialog to edit government title names. Presents 14 entries (male/female titles for 7 governments) in a list.
summaries.004A496C: Shows the tribes editing dialog (TRIBES section editor).
summaries.004A49CB: Marks the editor as clean (DAT_006a1d7c=0) and invalidates the display cache.
summaries.004A49F3: Handles editor button clicks. Button 0xC9: saves controls, updates tribe selection, refreshes. Button 0xCA: just saves and refreshes.
summaries.004A4A58: Creates a dropdown/listbox control in the scenario editor. Populates it with items based on param_1 (control type: 0=tribes, 1=gender, 2=expansion, 3=city style, 4=AI personality).
summaries.004A4EB2: Creates a text input field in the scenario editor at a position determined by param_1.
summaries.004A4F89: Renders the entire scenario editor screen: background, tribe flag, city sprite, terrain sprite, tribe name, and labels for each editor section.
summaries.004A54D9: Opens and runs the scenario editor. Creates the editor window, initializes controls (5 dropdowns + text fields), loads rules data, creates buttons (Save/Load/Cancel), and enters the editor main loop. Runs until DAT_006a1d7c is cleared.
summaries.004A5D92: Entry point for showing the scenario editor. Initializes bitmap wrapper, opens editor, cleans up on exit.
summaries.004A6980: Gets scrollbar width by calling FUN_00407f90.
summaries.004A69B0: Initializes a scrollable list in the Civilopedia help system. Copies item array, sets scroll bounds, and triggers display refresh.
summaries.004A6B80: Shows help text for a technology. Computes help string offset from tech category and epoch.
summaries.004A6BDC: Shows help text for a building/wonder. Uses different string arrays for buildings (< 0x27) vs wonders.
summaries.004A6C4B: Shows help text for a terrain type.
summaries.004A6C85: Draws a city sprite in the help/Civilopedia view.
summaries.004A6CC5: Shows a city style picker dialog for the Civilopedia.
summaries.004A6DF7: Draws a unit sprite in the Civilopedia help view.
summaries.004A6E39: Shows a unit type picker dialog for the Civilopedia.
summaries.004A7070: Converts lowercase ASCII letter to uppercase. Pure function.
summaries.004A70B0: Initializes game options to default values. Sets tech paradigm, tax rates, difficulty settings, and game flags.
summaries.004A71BB: Initializes all multiplayer session state to zero/defaults. Clears player names, session data, chat history, and network state.
summaries.004A733D: Loads CIV2.DAT preferences file. If file doesn't exist or reads fail, initializes defaults.
summaries.004A73D9: Saves CIV2.DAT preferences file. On write failure, deletes the file to avoid corruption.
summaries.004A74BC: Resets spaceship data for civ param_1 to all zeros.
summaries.004A7577: Returns whether civ param_1's spaceship has launched (bit 1 of status byte).
summaries.004A75A6: Returns whether civ param_1 has started building a spaceship (bit 0 of status byte).
summaries.004A75D5: Returns 1 if civ param_1's spaceship has launched AND the arrival turn has not yet been reached.
summaries.004A762D: Destroys a civ's spaceship, showing appropriate message (returned/destroyed). Resets spaceship data.
summaries.004A76F5: Resets the civilization destruction history to empty.
summaries.004A7754: Assigns starting positions for settler units at game start. Evaluates tile quality and spacing between civilizations to ensure fair distribution. Adds extra settlers for civs that got poor starts.
summaries.004A7CE9: Creates a new civilization. Initializes all per-civ game state arrays, selects a tribe, finds a starting position on the map, creates initial settler(s), and reveals nearby tiles. Handles both single-player and multiplayer cases.
summaries.004A93B3: Expands a city's territory by claiming the best adjacent unclaimed tiles. Claims param_2 tiles total, prioritizing by terrain quality and proximity.
summaries.004A9785: Sets up a scenario start. For each active civ: grants initial techs, creates initial city with buildings, creates military and settler units, expands city territory, and optionally creates a second city if suitable location found. Complex function that handles different start levels (param_1=0 for e
summaries.004AA378: Destroys a civilization. Removes all units, shows destruction message, records in kill history, clears map visibility, and creates a replacement civ (barbarian or new AI). Handles multiplayer sync.
summaries.004AA9C0: Initializes a completely new game. Resets all global game state, creates all 8 civilizations, assigns starting positions, and sets initial rates for human player(s).
summaries.004ABEA0: Converts an (x,y) delta into a direction index (0-7). Pure function. Returns 8 if no match found.
summaries.004ABFE5: BFS pathfinding algorithm. Finds shortest path from (param_1,param_2) to the target stored in DAT_00673fa0/fa4. Uses a circular queue with max 2304 entries. Movement costs account for terrain type, roads, rivers, railroads, enemy territory, mountains, and unit type capabilities.
