summaries.004AD01E: Returns the BFS path cost at map position (param_1, param_2). Uses a coordinate transform relative to the BFS origin.
summaries.004AD076: Stores a BFS path cost at map position (param_1, param_2).
summaries.004AD0D1: Calculates path distance between two points using BFS pathfinding. Returns the path cost, or -1 if too far (distance > 22 hexes).
summaries.004AD20F: Finds a path using the road/rail network between two points. Uses road connectivity maps for fast long-distance pathfinding, then BFS within the road network.
summaries.004AD784: Finds the first adjacent tile matching a given terrain type (land=0 or sea=nonzero). Checks tiles at offset (0,0) and (1,1).
summaries.004AD822: Finds the nearest tile connected to the road network. Searches the tile itself and its 8 neighbors.
summaries.004ADAFC: Calculates the next move direction for a unit executing a goto order. Uses short-range direct pathfinding for nearby targets and road-network pathfinding for distant targets. Falls back to heuristic direction if pathfinding fails.
summaries.004AEE90: Returns pointer to land road connectivity byte at (param_1, param_2).
summaries.004AEEC0: Returns pointer to sea connectivity byte at (param_1, param_2).
summaries.004AEEF0: Returns pointer to BFS visited byte at (param_1, param_2).
summaries.004AEF20: Sets the first byte of a string to 0 (empty string).
summaries.004AEF36: Appends separator string from DAT_0062d064 to param_1.
summaries.004AEF57: Appends param_2 copies of separator_1 to param_1.
summaries.004AEF96: Appends the separator/formatting string at DAT_0062d068 to param_1.
summaries.004AF122: Appends a string identified by ID (param_2) to param_1. Resolves ID to string via FUN_00428b0c.
summaries.004AF14B: Appends label string at index param_2 from the labels array to param_1.
summaries.004AF174: Appends raw string param_2 to param_1.
summaries.004AF194: Appends a highlighted string: wraps the resolved string between two separator markers.
summaries.004AF1D5: Converts int param_2 to string (base 10) and appends to param_1.
summaries.004AF20A: Converts int param_2 to 8-digit binary string and appends to param_1. Left-pads with zeros.
summaries.004AF284: Converts long param_2 to string (base 10) and appends to param_1.
summaries.004AF2B9: Appends a gold amount: the number followed by the gold unit string (label 0x143).
summaries.004AF3E0: Creates a civ listbox (similar to unit listbox). Populates with civs that have been contacted or are visible. Creates scrollbar and help button.
summaries.004AF867: Wrapper calling FUN_004af8a3(0, param_1) for left/top listbox scroll.
summaries.004AF885: Wrapper calling FUN_004af8a3(1, param_1) for right/bottom listbox scroll.
summaries.004AF8A3: Sets scroll position for a civ listbox and repaints.
summaries.004AF904: Handles click on civ listbox. Supports single, shift, and ctrl selection. Identical logic to FUN_00468834 but for the civ listbox.
summaries.004AFB77: Hit-tests a civ listbox to determine which item was clicked. Returns item index or negative error code.
summaries.004AFC89: Renders the civ listbox contents: civ flag sprite, civ name, leader name. Handles selected/unselected highlighting.
summaries.004AEFB7: Appends the separator/formatting string at DAT_0062d06c to param_1.
summaries.004AEFD8: Appends the separator/formatting string at DAT_0062d070 to param_1.
summaries.004AEFF9: Appends the separator/formatting string at DAT_0062d074 to param_1.
summaries.004AF01A: Appends the separator/formatting string at DAT_0062d078 to param_1.
summaries.004AF03B: Appends the separator/formatting string at DAT_0062d07c to param_1.
summaries.004AF05C: Appends the separator/formatting string at DAT_0062d080 to param_1.
summaries.004AF07D: Appends the separator/formatting string at DAT_0062d084 to param_1.
summaries.004AF09E: Appends the separator/formatting string at DAT_0062d088 to param_1.
summaries.004AF0BF: Appends the separator/formatting string at DAT_0062d08c to param_1.
summaries.004AF0E0: Appends the separator/formatting string at DAT_0062d090 to param_1.
summaries.004AF101: Appends the separator/formatting string at DAT_0062d094 to param_1.
summaries.004B0157: Finds the best city for a given player (param_1) by iterating all cities and scoring them based on size, capitol status, and coastal status, then draws its sprite at a calculated screen position.
summaries.004B0720: Looks up a technology by name string, returning its index (0-99), or special values -2 ("nil") / -1 ("none") / -3 (not found).
summaries.004B07D1: Looks up a terrain type by name string, returning its index (0-10), or -1/-2 for special values, -3 if not found.
summaries.004B08E0: CRT static initializer that calls _E1. Part of Visual Studio 1998 Debug library initialization chain.
summaries.004B08F5: Empty CRT initializer stub. Does nothing and returns.
summaries.004B0905: Allocates a mirror buffer for the diff engine used in multiplayer state synchronization. Computes total size from 23 section sizes, allocates memory, then copies current game state into mirror.
summaries.004B0A0A: Frees the diff engine mirror buffer and sets pointer to null.
summaries.004B0A41: Copies all 23 game state sections into the diff engine mirror buffer.
summaries.004B0AD0: Copies state into mirror then bitwise-inverts all mirror data. This forces a full diff on next scan (every dword differs).
summaries.004B0B53: Core multiplayer diff engine: scans game state sections against mirror, finds changed regions, RLE-compresses diffs, and sends them over the network via message type 0x5c. Handles section boundaries, wrap-around, and flow control.
summaries.004B12B3: Checks if a specific game state section has changed compared to the diff engine mirror. Returns 1 if any dword differs, 0 if identical.
summaries.004B1396: Computes a simple additive checksum over a data buffer. Optimizes for 4-byte aligned, 2-byte aligned, and byte-level access.
summaries.004B14A4: Calculates total serialized game state size across all 24 sections, with special handling for sections 5 (units) and 6 (cities) which use dynamic counts. Adds 0x1e0 header bytes.
summaries.004B153C: Serializes 7 game state sections into a contiguous buffer with checksums. Each section gets a 0x14-byte header (size + checksum) followed by raw data. Used for save game serialization.
summaries.004B187F: Appends data to a serialization buffer, advancing the write cursor. Asserts buffer won't overflow.
summaries.004B18E1: Serializes 2 specific game state sections (section 0 and one other) into a compressed buffer. Lighter version of full serialize.
summaries.004B1A15: Serializes all 24 game state sections with RLE compression. Computes and stores per-section checksums.
summaries.004B1C11: Serializes only game state sections whose checksums have changed since last serialization. Compresses the result.
summaries.004B1DE3: Deserializes game state sections from a compressed buffer, restoring all section data. Handles RLE decompression if DAT_006c8ffc is set.
summaries.004B2010: Parses a save file data block. Saves turn state, decompresses if needed, reads two mandatory sections (btGame=1 and btMapStruct=12), then reinitializes section registry and diff engine.
summaries.004B2123: Reads one section node from a serialized buffer: extracts header (size, type), copies data to the section's game state pointer, computes checksum.
summaries.004B21D7: Registers all 24 game state sections with the diff engine, setting up pointers, sizes, and types. This is the master section registry initialization.
summaries.004B24A2: Calculates the decoded size of an RLE-compressed data stream by scanning run headers without actually decompressing.
summaries.004B251A: Decodes an RLE-compressed data stream into a destination buffer.
summaries.004B263E: RLE-encodes a data buffer. Finds runs of repeated bytes and literal sequences, encoding them into a compact format with 16-bit headers.
summaries.004B3080: Registers a single game state section with the diff engine, storing its address, size, type, and 4-byte aligned size.
summaries.004B3110: Sets a bit in the continent adjacency table indicating two continents/ocean bodies are adjacent.
summaries.004B315C: Computes continent adjacency by iterating all map tiles, checking which continent/ocean bodies are adjacent to each tile via 8-directional neighbor checks.
summaries.004B32FE: Assigns continent/ocean body IDs to all map tiles using a flood-fill algorithm. Runs two passes: first for ocean (body_type=1), then land (body_type=0). Merges small bodies into an "other" category. Calls continent_calc_adjacency at the end.
summaries.004B3CA0: Static initializer pair: initializes a global object and registers its atexit destructor.
summaries.004B3CBA: Initializes a static object with params (0, 0x10, 1).
summaries.004B3CDA: Registers FUN_004b3cf7 as an atexit handler.
summaries.004B3D11: CRT static init pair. Calls init then registers atexit.
summaries.004B401C: Constructs parley window 1 (DAT_006665da) and registers atexit destructor.
summaries.004B4036: Constructs parley window object at DAT_006665da.
summaries.004B405B: Registers FUN_004b4078 as atexit handler for parley window 1.
summaries.004B4092: Constructs parley window 2 (DAT_006665ea) and registers atexit destructor.
summaries.004B40AC: Constructs parley window object at DAT_006665ea.
summaries.004B40D1: Registers FUN_004b40ee as atexit handler for parley window 2.
summaries.004B4108: Constructs a parley (diplomacy negotiation) window object, initializing all fields: vtable, dimensions, negotiation state arrays, button states, scrollbar positions, and chat buffers.
summaries.004B4593: Destructs parley window. Saves chat log to file if enabled, frees chat and input buffers, destroys all child controls.
summaries.004B4705: Closes the chat log file handle.
summaries.004B4711: Calls base class destructor for the parley window.
summaries.004B4727: SEH cleanup handler. Restores FS_OFFSET from stack.
summaries.004B4735: Opens and initializes the parley (diplomacy/chat) window. For multiplayer chat mode (type 4), allocates chat buffers from INI settings, loads chat history from file. Sets up window layout, resolution, scrollbars, and child controls.
summaries.004B4BE3: Closes chat file handle (duplicate of FUN_004b4705).
summaries.004B4C09: Saves the current parley window position (x, y) into member fields at offsets 0x11c and 0x120.
summaries.004B4C81: Sets the parley window's hi-res flag based on screen width (> 999 → hi-res), then recalculates layout.
summaries.004B4CF0: Calculates the parley window dimensions and position based on resolution mode (lo-res: 520×335, hi-res: 800×460). Centers window on screen if no saved position.
summaries.004B4E8A: Calculates client area rectangles for the parley window's display and input areas based on window dimensions and border sizes.
summaries.004B4FB2: Updates scrollbar positions for the parley window. If resolution changed, recreates all controls. Otherwise just adjusts scrollbar ranges.
summaries.004B50CF: Frees all child controls (buttons, text boxes, list boxes, etc.) of the parley window. Iterates through ~30+ control slots, deleting each if non-null.
summaries.004B5C93: Creates all child controls for the parley/diplomacy window: 3 leader portraits, OK button, chat display/input text boxes, and (for non-MP modes) treaty/proposal buttons. Layout depends on hi-res flag and window type.
summaries.004B74C4: Handles button clicks in the parley window. Button 0x3e9 = close. Buttons 0x3ea/0x3eb = zoom in/out (toggle resolution).
summaries.004B75FB: OK button handler — calls close on the parley window.
summaries.004B7645: Sets focus to the negotiation parley window object (DAT_0067a7f0), then closes.
summaries.004B768D: Sets focus to the chat parley window object (DAT_0068ac30), then closes.
summaries.004B76D5: Closes the parley window. For the main negotiation window (DAT_0067a7a8), handles pending negotiation state: sends cancel message, displays PARLEYCANCEL dialog. Zeroes DAT_00626a2c.
summaries.004B7885: Repaints the parley window: recalculates client rects, rebuilds title, paints decorations, updates scrollbars, and refreshes text displays.
summaries.004B794A: Paints all decorative elements of the parley window: top/bottom borders, 3 leader portraits (scaled to window size), and centered title text with drop shadow.
summaries.004B7C90: Paints a horizontal border strip (top or bottom) of the parley window using either tiled background or flat fill.
summaries.004B7D72: Builds the title string for the parley window. For multiplayer chat: just the chat room name. For negotiation: "Leader Name of Civilization (Government)" with civ leader portrait text.
summaries.004B7EB6: Starts a diplomacy or chat session. For chat (type 4): validates at least one valid foreign contact exists, then opens chat window. For negotiation: initializes negotiation state, sets initial offer/demand scrollbar positions.
summaries.004B81DD: Handles incoming diplomacy responses (accept, reject, counter-offer, war declaration). Updates negotiation state machine, sends network messages, and displays appropriate dialog text (PARLEYACCEPT, PARLEYNOTHANKS, PARLEYCOUNTEROFFER). Can trigger treaty breaking (CANCELALLIED, CANCELPEACE, BREAKCEAS
summaries.004B8676: Sets the negotiation state based on the current offer type (DAT_0067a9b0). Maps offer types (0-4) to negotiation states (3-15) and configures scrollbar ranges from lookup tables.
summaries.004B888E: Cleans up (destroys) all controls on one side (left=0, right=1) of the negotiation window. Frees buttons, checkboxes, text boxes, and list controls for that side. Then repaints the cleared area.
summaries.004B8E5C: Validates gold amount entered in a parley negotiation edit box. Strips non-numeric chars, clamps to player's treasury, updates the gold-valid flag (DAT_0067a98c).
summaries.004B90AD: Sends a chat message in multiplayer diplomacy. Formats the message with sender name and year, sends to other human players via network message 0x68, appends to local chat display. Can trigger opening a new chat window for observers.
summaries.004B93A2: Appends a text string to the chat display buffer (DAT_0062d870), scrolling out oldest text if buffer is full. Updates both chat text box controls.
summaries.004B9504: Formats a civilization's name for display in chat. Player 0 = "Computer" (from string table). Others = "Title Name" (leader title + name).
summaries.004B957E: Handles keydown event. Key 0xd2 (Insert/special) → close window.
summaries.004B95C2: Triggers parley_handle_command with (0, 0xd0) — the "send message" command.
summaries.004B95E1: Clears the chat display buffer and resets both chat text box controls.
summaries.004B9635: Preprocesses keyboard input. ESC (0x1b) or Insert (0xd2) → close window and consume key. Otherwise passes through.
summaries.004B968A: Main command handler for the parley input box. Handles message send (0xd0), scrollbar commands (0x2b0-0x2b4), and close (0xd2). For message send: formats chat message, broadcasts to all relevant human players via network messages (0x68), clears input.
summaries.004BB370: Wrapper that calls FUN_005d84f6 (reads text from a file-like source).
summaries.004BB3B0: Destructor for a button widget. Calls base destructor then optionally frees memory.
summaries.004BB540: Returns the height of a widget by calling thunk_FUN_00407fc0.
summaries.004BB570: Sets a widget's size by calling FUN_005bc713 with the widget's window handle and param_1.
summaries.004BB5B0: Sets a flag byte at offset 0x24 of the widget (in_ECX) to 1, marking it as read-only.
summaries.004BB5E0: Sets keyboard focus to the widget's window handle (at offset 0x1c).
summaries.004BB620: Creates an edit box widget: registers window class, creates the HWND, sets initial text.
summaries.004BB6D0: Sets the cursor position in an edit box by sending a message to its HWND.
summaries.004BB710: Returns the text length of an edit box widget.
summaries.004BB740: Destructor for a scrollbar widget. Destroys the secondary window handle at offset 0x44.
summaries.004BB800: Inflates a rectangle by negative amounts (shrinks it). Wrapper for InflateRect with negated params.
summaries.004BB870: Allocates global memory via FUN_005dce4f, stores size in DAT_00634818 and success flag in DAT_00634814.
summaries.004BB8E0: Initializes the wonder view display: constructs the wonder view object, loads wonder art, plays wonder video (if applicable), and handles sound.
summaries.004BB99F: Constructs the wonder view object, setting up MFC base classes and storing global pointer in DAT_006a1864.
summaries.004BBA79: Destructs the wonder view object. Zeroes DAT_006a1864 and destroys child objects in reverse order.
summaries.004BBAF1: Destroys a child object of wonder view.
summaries.004BBB00: Destroys a child object of wonder view.
summaries.004BBB0F: Destroys a child object of wonder view.
summaries.004BBB3F: Loads wonder artwork from "civ2.wonder.dll", extracts the specific wonder's GIF sprite, creates a scaled bitmap surface, and displays it in the wonder view window.
summaries.004BBDFB: Plays a wonder video (AVI) if available and video features are enabled. Checks for "civ2.video.wonder##.avi" file, loads and plays it in the wonder view window. Falls back to static image if video not registered.
summaries.004BC0BB: Returns 0 unconditionally. Likely a virtual method stub.
summaries.004BC0D3: Refreshes the wonder view's rendering surface, blitting the wonder art bitmap.
summaries.004BC10F: Resizes the wonder view window to fit the wonder art dimensions (2x width for hi-res, plus height offset).
summaries.004BC193: Invalidates the wonder view's object cache to trigger a repaint.
summaries.004BC1B1: Same as wonder_view_invalidate_1. Likely a different message handler.
summaries.004BC1CF: Invalidates wonder view if param_1 is in range [0xd0, 0xd2] (specific wonder-related keys).
summaries.004BC480: AI function that assesses a civilization's military posture by analyzing unit counts, city counts, technology levels, diplomatic relationships, and military readiness. Returns a posture value 1-7.
summaries.004BC8AA: AI function assessing city defense adequacy for a civilization. Evaluates government type, unhappiness levels, defending unit counts, and technology level relative to rivals. Returns 1-7.
summaries.004BCB9B: AI economic assessment: evaluates a civilization's financial health by examining gold reserves, trade income, building maintenance, unhappiness, and technology level. Returns 1-7.
summaries.004BCFCF: AI diplomatic assessment: evaluates a civilization's diplomatic standing based on contact count, alliance status, reputation, treaty obligations, and espionage capabilities. Returns 1-7.
summaries.004BD2A3: AI function to assess and adjust tax rates. Checks cities for unhappiness, disorder potential, and we-love-the-king eligibility. Returns recommendation 1-6 for tax rate adjustment.
summaries.004BD9F0: Checks if a civilization (param_1) has a specific technology (param_2). Returns 1 if yes, 0 if no. Handles special cases: tech -2 = no (future tech marker), tech < 0 = yes (always available), tech 0x59 = no, tech >= 100 = no.
summaries.004BDAA5: Recursively checks if technology param_1 is a descendant of technology param_2 in the tech tree. Returns 1 if param_1 == param_2 or if either prerequisite of param_1 is a descendant of param_2.
summaries.004BDB2C: Calculates the AI's perceived value of researching a specific technology. Considers: tech epoch, exclusivity (whether other civs have it), government bonus, civilization leader trait bonuses, wonder prerequisites, and extensive per-civilization-type preference tables (20 civ types × specific tech bo
summaries.004BE6BA: When a tech is discovered that obsoletes units, upgrades all applicable units of that civilization to the newer type. Shows UPGRADE dialog for human players, sends network update in multiplayer.
summaries.004BEA84: Handles side effects when a civ discovers a tech that unlocks a new government form. For Monarchy (tech 0x36), auto-prompts revolution if player is in Despotism. For Republic/Democracy/Communism/Fundamentalism, offers revolution dialog. Also shows tutorial messages for key techs.
summaries.004BEE56: Triggers "We Love the King Day" celebration for a civilization. Picks the best city (weighted by size + capitol bonus + random factor) and shows GOLDENAGE dialog with that city's name.
summaries.004BEFD1: Formats an enabled item (unit/improvement/wonder) for display in the tech discovery dialog. Every 4th item starts a new line with a separator.
summaries.004BF05B: Master handler for when a civilization discovers a new technology. This is one of the most important game logic functions. It: updates the civ's tech bitmask, increments discovery count, shows discovery dialog, lists newly enabled units/improvements/wonders, handles government unlocks, upgrades unit
summaries.004BFD9A: Cleanup for tech discovery handler. Frees display resources.
summaries.004BFDBE: Checks if a civilization can research a specific technology. Requires: tech exists (name not empty), civ doesn't already have it, and civ has both prerequisites.
summaries.004BFE5A: Checks if a civilization can build a specific unit type. Validates tech prerequisites, obsolescence, unit role restrictions, government requirements, and city capabilities. Returns 1 if buildable, 0 if not.
summaries.004C02D8: Checks if a civ can build a specific wonder. Returns 1 if the wonder slot is vacant (DAT_00655be6 == -1) and the civ has the prerequisite tech.
summaries.004C03AE: Comprehensive check for whether a civ can build a specific city improvement or wonder. Handles all prerequisite tech requirements, building dependencies (e.g., Library before University), mutually exclusive buildings, and scenario restrictions.
summaries.004C09B0: AI picks a research goal from all researchable techs. Human players use weighted random selection; AI uses pure value + random noise. AI skips every 3rd tech based on difficulty for variety.
summaries.004C0B51: Blits a tech icon sprite at normal scale. Adjusts x-position by +0x26 if flag bit 1 is set.
summaries.004C0BE8: Blits a tech icon sprite at a scaled zoom level (scale factor -2). Adjusts position for centering.
summaries.004C0C83: Same as blit_tech_icon_normal but without the flag-based offset adjustment.
summaries.004C0CF7: Shows the research goal selection dialog with three tabs: All Techs, By Unit, By Building/Wonder. Allows player to browse and select a long-term research goal. Shows prerequisite chain for the selected goal.
summaries.004C195E: The main "choose research" dialog for human players. Shows current tech goal recommendation, list of available techs, and allows selecting one. For AI, auto-selects via ai_pick_research_goal. Also shows the "what does this lead to" preview.
summaries.004C21AD: Wrapper that calls choose_research_tech(param_1, 0) — the "choose next research" entry point.
summaries.004C21D5: Completes a tech research for a civ. Calls handle_tech_discovery to process the tech. For human players, shows the discovery animation, handles special events (new government forms, we-love-the-king day, new abilities like fortress/railroad/farmland/paradrop), and prompts for next research choice.
summaries.004C2788: Calculates the research cost (in beakers) for a civ to discover the next technology. Factors in: number of known techs, difficulty level, relative position to leading civ, game options, and scenario multipliers.
summaries.004C2B73: Adds research beakers to a civ's current research. If the tech cost is met, triggers research completion. Also handles the "choose research" prompt if no tech is selected.
summaries.004C4210: Sets the paradrop range for a unit type. Writes to the unit type table.
summaries.004C4240: Shows tech help text via the help display system.
summaries.004C42A0: Executes a settler/engineer work order (irrigate, mine, road, railroad, fortress, clean pollution, plant forest, etc.). Increments the work counter each call; when enough turns have passed, completes the improvement.
summaries.004C4ADA: Executes the fortify order — assigns the unit to a city if in one (with size > 2), or just fortifies in the field. Updates unit's home city assignment.
summaries.004C4D1E: Founds a new city at the unit's location. Reveals map to the founding civ, creates the city, optionally names it, removes the settler unit, clears tile improvements, and updates visibility for all civs.
summaries.004C4E6D: Executes the goto/move order for a unit. Computes direction via calc_unit_goto_direction and moves one step. When destination is reached, clears the order. For non-human land units at destination, may assign idle settler work orders.
summaries.004C50D0: Pillages improvements on a tile. Removes the highest-priority improvement (fortress > railroad > irrigation/mining > road). If pillaging in another civ's territory, declares war. Checks for diplomatic permission first.
summaries.004C5408: Dispatches unit order execution based on the order type byte (DAT_006560ff). Returns 1 if order was processed, 0 if unknown order.
summaries.004C54DA: For AI units, finds the nearest friendly city or transport ship to go to. Sets a goto order toward that destination. For sea units, also considers transport ships with cargo capacity.
summaries.004C59F0: Handles the diplomatic incident when a spy/diplomat is caught performing espionage. If caught by a human player, offers the choice to declare war. AI automatically responds based on relationship status. May trigger government overthrow scandal if caught by own government's spy.
summaries.004C5FAE: Executes a spy/diplomat's action in an enemy city — handles the chance of being caught (based on veteran status and whether exposed). Diplomat type 0x2F (spy) can survive and re-enter cities. On success, reports to player. On failure, may trigger diplomatic incident.
summaries.004C64AA: Checks if a spy gets caught during an action. Wrapper around spy_diplomat_action with param_2=-1. Shows "caught" dialog if spy dies.
summaries.004C654D: For human players with treaty status, asks permission before committing an act of espionage. Returns true if the player declined (preventing the action).
summaries.004C65D2: Calculates the "revolt distance" for a city — minimum distance to any friendly city with a courthouse. Used to determine incite revolt cost.
summaries.004C66BA: Executes a civil war — transfers nearby units from the old civ to the new rebel civ, reveals map around the city, and notifies players.
summaries.004C6BF5: The enormous (10KB) spy/diplomat city action handler. Implements all espionage operations: establish embassy (case 0), investigate city (case 1), steal technology (case 2), sabotage production (case 3), poison water supply (case 4), plant nuclear device (case 5), incite revolt (case 6), and counter-
summaries.004C9528: Handles bribing/picking up an enemy unit — the player pays gold to convert an enemy unit to their side. Checks government type (fundamentalist is immune), calculates bribe cost based on unit maintenance, city distance, and treasury. For multiplayer, sends network message and waits for confirmation.
summaries.004C9EBD: Spy option to sabotage an enemy unit — either bribe it or blow it up with explosives. For spy units (type /), offers the choice. Sabotage deals half the unit's HP as damage.
summaries.004CA1CD: Executes an airlift operation — moves a unit from one city to another. Has a 1-in-6 chance of being shot down for each air defense unit at destination. Marks both cities as having used their airlift for the turn.
summaries.004CA39E: Executes a paradrop operation. Validates range, checks for enemy units at target, determines which players can see the drop, performs the actual move, and triggers combat if landing on an enemy city.
summaries.004CC870: Shows help text for an improvement via the help display system.
summaries.004CC8B0: Shows help for a unit type.
summaries.004CC8F0: C++ static initializer — constructs a global object and registers its destructor via atexit.
summaries.004CC924: Registers FUN_004cc941 as an atexit handler.
summaries.004CC941: Destructor for the static object at DAT_006a18c0.
summaries.004CC95B: Static initializer for a global toolbar-related object. Calls the constructor (thunk_FUN_004187a0 via FUN_004cc975) and registers the destructor (thunk_FUN_00418870 via FUN_004cc98f/_atexit).
summaries.004CC9C6: Static initializer for a global string collection object. Calls the constructor (thunk_FUN_0043c460 with size 0x10 via FUN_004cc9e0) and registers the destructor (thunk_FUN_0043c520 via FUN_004cc9fe/_atexit).
summaries.004CCA35: Shows a message box if the memory allocation size exceeds the available pool. Otherwise delegates to FUN_00498159 for normal allocation.
summaries.004CCAB9: Initializes the rules editor section list with the first entry.
summaries.004CCAED: Adds an entry to the rules editor section table (up to 7 entries).
summaries.004CCB6A: Draws a decorative border frame around a rectangle using a tiled border texture from DAT_00647f18.
summaries.004CCDB6: Appends a formatted number to the global string buffer DAT_00679640.
summaries.004CCDEF: Appends a technology name to the string buffer, handling special cases for "no tech" (-2), "any tech" (-1), and normal tech IDs (0-99). Pads short names with spaces.
summaries.004CCF2D: Saves modified game rules to RULES.TXT. Creates a backup (RULES.BAK) if editing in the game directory, or saves alongside the scenario. Iterates through registered sections, calling their write callbacks.
summaries.004CD3D7: Saves a modified entry in CITY.TXT — finds a section by name, then replaces a specific line matching a name prefix.
summaries.004CD8A6: Updates civilization names in CITY.TXT to match current game state — replaces @section headers for each government type with the correct civ name.
summaries.004CDCF6: Returns 1 if param_2 is a digit (0x30-0x39), minus sign (0x2d), or special char (0xd6). Returns 0 otherwise.
summaries.004CDD3D: Shows a dialog with checkboxes (used for cheat/debug options). Pre-fills checkbox states from param_3 bitmask. On close, reads states back into DAT_00631ed8.
summaries.004CDF4B: Clamps param_1 between param_2 and param_3. Sets DAT_0062e014 = 1 if clamping occurred.
summaries.004CDFA4: Creates and configures a popup dialog window with optional scrollbar, video player, and background image.
summaries.004CE196: Loads up to 20 Civilopedia page strings from a text file and stores them in the page pointer array.
summaries.004CE322: Paints the Civilopedia header area with decorative elements.
summaries.004CE38A: Shows the Civilopedia viewer dialog with paginated text and navigation buttons.
summaries.004CE71B: Checks if a city name is available (not already used). First tries to find it in a text file; if not found, shows a "city name taken" dialog.
summaries.004CE83D: Sends CB_RESETCONTENT (0x14F) to all combobox controls in a dialog.
summaries.004CE8A4: Reads a long integer value from an INI-style configuration, converting the key to a string and the result from string to long.
summaries.004CE903: Allocates memory and copies a string, ensuring minimum size. Used to clone event data strings.
summaries.004CE98E: Deep-copies event nodes from one event manager to another, cloning all string fields at specific offsets (0x8, 0x10, 0x14, 0x20, 0x38-0x88, 0x88, 0x90, 0xc4, 0xcc, 0xd4, 0xdc, 0x13c, 0x140, 0x148, 0x174, 0x184).
summaries.004CEF35: Rebuilds event data by deep-copying it to a temporary buffer and back. Acts as a defragmentation/compaction step.
summaries.004CEFE9: Renames a city in all event nodes — searches all event node city-name fields and replaces matching names.
summaries.004CF144: Renames text strings in event nodes — searches 8 different text fields and replaces matches.
summaries.004CF3BA: Renames unit names in event nodes — checks 2 unit-name fields.
summaries.004CFF70: Sets the video source for a dialog.
summaries.004CFFB0: Sends a message to the child window at this+0x1c.
summaries.004CFFF0: Trims trailing spaces and tabs from a string by replacing them with null terminators.
summaries.004C193A: SEH cleanup for research goal dialog — frees an allocated buffer via thunk_FUN_0059df8a.
summaries.004C1950: SEH epilog for research goal dialog. Restores the previous exception handler frame.
summaries.004C217C: First SEH cleanup helper for choose_research_tech — frees a buffer via thunk_FUN_0059df8a.
summaries.004C2188: Second SEH cleanup helper for choose_research_tech — frees another buffer via thunk_FUN_0059df8a.
summaries.004C219E: SEH epilog for choose_research_tech. Restores the previous exception handler frame.
summaries.004C2763: SEH cleanup for calc_tech_cost — frees an allocated buffer via thunk_FUN_0059df8a.
summaries.004C2779: SEH epilog for calc_tech_cost. Restores the previous exception handler frame.
summaries.004C9504: SEH cleanup for spy_enters_city — frees an allocated buffer via thunk_FUN_0059df8a.
summaries.004C951A: SEH epilog for spy_enters_city. Restores the previous exception handler frame.
summaries.004CC975: Calls thunk_FUN_004187a0 to construct a global toolbar-related object.
summaries.004CC98F: Registers FUN_004cc9ac as an atexit handler for the toolbar object destructor.
summaries.004CC9AC: Calls thunk_FUN_00418870 to destroy the global toolbar-related object at program exit.
summaries.004CC9E0: Constructs a global string collection object by calling thunk_FUN_0043c460(0, 0x10).
summaries.004CC9FE: Registers FUN_004cca1b as an atexit handler for the string collection destructor.
summaries.004CCA1B: Calls thunk_FUN_0043c520 to destroy the global string collection at program exit.
summaries.004CDF26: SEH cleanup — frees an allocated buffer via thunk_FUN_0059df8a.
summaries.004CDF3C: SEH epilog. Restores the previous exception handler frame.
summaries.004CE6ED: First cleanup helper for the city name dialog — calls thunk_FUN_0040f570 (likely CString or edit control destructor).
summaries.004CE6F9: Second cleanup helper for the city name dialog — calls thunk_FUN_0040f570 (same destructor as FUN_004ce6ed).
summaries.004CE70C: SEH epilog for city name dialog. Restores the previous exception handler frame.
summaries.004CEFC5: Cleanup for rename_city_in_events — calls thunk_FUN_004fa569 (likely a CString or buffer destructor).
summaries.004CEFDB: SEH epilog for rename_city_in_events. Restores the previous exception handler frame.
summaries.004D007E: Trims leading whitespace (spaces and tabs) from a string in-place. Calls thunk_FUN_004cfff0 first (likely trims trailing), then copies the trimmed result back.
summaries.004D0160: Checks if the wonder window timer has elapsed (0x4b1 ticks) and invalidates the object cache if so, or if DAT_006ad698 is set.
summaries.004D01AE: Loads 6 power values from a civ's data (at offset 0x594*param_1 into per-civ data) into global array DAT_006a5b10.
summaries.004D0208: Shows either a wonder movie (negative param) or advance animation (positive param). Creates the wonder window, initializes it, then dispatches to the appropriate display function.
summaries.004D032A: SEH epilog — restores FS register from stack frame.
summaries.004D0339: Shows the between-turns advance animation. Only proceeds if param_1 matches DAT_006d1da0 (current player) and no window is already active.
summaries.004D043F: Draws the title text for the wonder/advance window, combining civ name, leader name, and title. Renders to a rect at the top of the window.
summaries.004D0517: Constructor/initializer for the wonder window object. Initializes multiple sub-objects (bitmaps, strings, controls), stores the advance/wonder ID, and sets initial display rectangles.
summaries.004D08B0: Destructor for the wonder window. Frees allocated resources (3 bitmap handles), destroys sub-objects in reverse order, and clears the global window pointer.
summaries.004D0A56: Wonder window teardown helper. Calls thunk_FUN_0046ab49 to destroy a dialog sub-object.
summaries.004D0A65: Wonder window teardown helper. Calls FUN_005cde4d to destroy a surface sub-object.
summaries.004D0A74: Wonder window teardown helper. Calls FUN_005cde4d to destroy a surface sub-object.
summaries.004D0A83: Wonder window teardown helper. Calls FUN_005cde4d to destroy a surface sub-object.
summaries.004D0A92: Wonder window teardown helper. Calls FUN_005cde4d to destroy a surface sub-object.
summaries.004D0AA1: Wonder window teardown helper. Calls FUN_005cde4d to destroy a surface sub-object.
summaries.004D0AB0: Wonder window teardown helper. Calls FUN_005cde4d to destroy a surface sub-object.
summaries.004D0ABF: Wonder window teardown helper. Calls FUN_005cde4d to destroy a surface sub-object.
summaries.004D0ACE: Wonder window teardown helper. Calls FUN_005bd915 to destroy a bitmap sub-object.
summaries.004D0ADD: Wonder window teardown helper. Calls thunk_FUN_0043c520 to destroy a font sub-object.
summaries.004D0AEC: Wonder window teardown helper. Calls thunk_FUN_0043c520 to destroy a font sub-object.
summaries.004D0AFB: Wonder window teardown helper. Calls thunk_FUN_0043c520 to destroy a font sub-object.
summaries.004D0B0A: Wonder window teardown helper. Calls FUN_005c656b to destroy a CString sub-object.
summaries.004D0B19: Wonder window teardown helper. Calls _Timevec::~_Timevec on the sub-object at offset 500 from the base pointer.
summaries.004D0B28: Wonder window teardown helper. Calls thunk_FUN_0044cba0 to destroy a window sub-object.
summaries.004D0B37: Wonder window teardown helper. Calls thunk_FUN_0044ca60 to destroy a window sub-object.
summaries.004D0B4A: SEH epilog for the wonder window destructor chain. Restores the FS register from the saved stack frame.
summaries.004D0B58: Creates the wonder window — loads the DLL resource, builds the info text, determines display mode (0=normal, 1=has video, 3=space race), creates the dialog, and draws buttons.
summaries.004D0D64: Creates the actual dialog window for the wonder display, setting up the surface, loading a background image resource, and registering message handlers.
summaries.004D0EA6: Main advance animation display function. Loads civ power values, builds the advance scene, sets up buttons, creates animation timers, and enters the message loop. For space race advances, shows starfield. Can recursively call itself after playing wonder video.
summaries.004D13B8: Shows the advance animation between turns. Similar to show_advance_animation but specifically for the between-turns display, with cross-fade transition effects.
summaries.004D1725: Shows a wonder movie by first refreshing the display, playing the video (via play_wonder_video), then showing the advance animation with mode 3.
summaries.004D17BF: Massive function that builds the visual scene for an advance animation. Contains hardcoded layout tables for 88 scene elements defining positions, sizes, and layer assignments for ancient/medieval/industrial/modern era artwork. Loads multiple DLL bitmap resources and composites them based on civ pow
summaries.004D49D5: Build advance scene cleanup helper. Calls FUN_005bd915 to destroy a bitmap sub-object.
summaries.004D49E1: Build advance scene cleanup helper. Calls FUN_005bd915 to destroy a bitmap sub-object.
summaries.004D49ED: Build advance scene cleanup helper. Calls FUN_005bd915 to destroy a bitmap sub-object.
summaries.004D49F9: Build advance scene cleanup helper. Calls FUN_005bd915 to destroy a bitmap sub-object.
summaries.004D4A05: Build advance scene cleanup helper. Calls FUN_005bd915 to destroy a bitmap sub-object.
summaries.004D4A11: Build advance scene cleanup helper. Calls FUN_005bd915 to destroy a bitmap sub-object.
summaries.004D4A1D: Build advance scene cleanup helper. Calls FUN_005bd915 to destroy a bitmap sub-object.
summaries.004D4A29: Build advance scene cleanup helper. Calls FUN_005bd915 to destroy a bitmap sub-object.
summaries.004D4A35: Build advance scene cleanup helper. Calls FUN_005bd915 to destroy a bitmap sub-object.
summaries.004D4A3E: Build advance scene cleanup helper. Calls FUN_005bd915 to destroy a bitmap sub-object.
summaries.004D4A4A: Build advance scene cleanup helper. Calls FUN_005bd915 to destroy a bitmap sub-object.
summaries.004D4A56: Build advance scene cleanup helper. Calls FUN_005bd915 to destroy the last bitmap sub-object.
summaries.004D4A6C: SEH epilog for build_advance_scene. Restores the FS register from the saved stack frame.
summaries.004D4A7B: Builds the descriptive text shown in the wonder/advance window. Formats power category values, scores, population, land area, military strength, and other civilization statistics into a rich text string.
summaries.004D4FD1: Draws the next character of the wonder info text with typewriter effect. Handles newlines, color changes (based on score rating), and triggers end-of-text actions (creates timers, draws score display).
summaries.004D53AB: Draws the button graphics for the wonder window. Creates "Close" and navigation button images from DLL resources, with localization support (English/French/German).
summaries.004D56EA: Cleanup — bitmap destructor and SEH epilog.
summaries.004D570B: Initializes and displays the starfield animation for space race victories. Creates 80 randomly-positioned stars with 3D perspective projection, draws all text immediately (no typewriter effect), then sets up the star display.
summaries.004D5B21: Plays a wonder video (AVI) file. Allocates a video player object, opens "civ2_video_launch.avi", creates a playback window, and enters a message loop until video completes.
summaries.004D5E41: Sets up click hotspots for the wonder window buttons. Configures either 1 or 2 buttons depending on whether this is the current player's advance and the display mode.
summaries.004D5EF9: Draws the initial button states. If mode is not 1 and not 0, clears the first button area. Draws both buttons in their default states.
summaries.004D5F79: Draws the left button (close/ok) in one of three visual states: normal, hover, or pressed, based on params.
summaries.004D60A5: Draws the right button (play video) in one of three visual states. Only visible when mode is 1 (has video) and belongs to current player.
summaries.004D61C3: Handles mouse click events on the wonder window. Dispatches to left button (advance text) or right button (play video) based on hotspot hit testing.
summaries.004D6367: Timer callback that draws the next character of wonder text with param=1 (timer-driven).
summaries.004D6384: Toggles the animation state flag at offset 0x8ec of the wonder window and redraws the right button accordingly.
summaries.004D63FB: Animates the starfield display for space race victories. Updates 80 star positions with parallax scrolling, erases old positions, draws new ones.
summaries.004D6744: Handles keyboard input for the wonder window. Keys 0xd0-0xd2 (Enter/Space/Escape) advance text or close the window.
summaries.004D686B: If a video player exists, sends it a message via InvalidateObjectCache.
summaries.004D68A7: Handles mouse button down on wonder window. Records which button was pressed and shows pressed visual state.
summaries.004D6A30: Handles mouse button release on wonder window. If released over the same button that was pressed, executes the button action (close or play video).
summaries.004D6CBC: Handles mouse movement over the wonder window. Updates button hover states as mouse moves between hotspot regions.
summaries.004D8AF0: Returns the first dword pointed to by the ECX register (vtable accessor or first field accessor).
summaries.004D8B20: Destructor for the video player object. Calls cleanup function, optionally frees memory.
summaries.004D8B70: Creates a button hotspot with given index, position, and size by calling thunk_FUN_0046ace7.
summaries.004D8BC0: Loads improvement data from the game's internal tables into the editor's working copies. Copies 67 improvement names and their properties.
summaries.004D8CAA: Saves editor's working improvement data back to the game's internal tables. Reverse of editor_load_improvements.
summaries.004D8D80: Updates editor dialog controls (edit fields and combo boxes) to reflect the currently selected improvement's properties.
summaries.004D8ED6: Reads values from editor dialog controls and stores them in the editor's working data. Returns count of clamped values.
summaries.004D9095: Wrapper that triggers the editor paint function.
summaries.004D90B0: Writes improvement definitions to a rules file. Formats each improvement with name, prereqs, and cost.
summaries.004D91F8: Writes the wonders section of the rules file with wonder cost values and names.
summaries.004D929A: Validates editor changes, saves improvements to rules file, and shows appropriate message boxes. On validation failure, shows notice dialog.
summaries.004D93B9: Shows a dialog to rename an improvement and updates the editor's name list.
summaries.004D953F: Shows help text for the improvement editor.
summaries.004D959E: Closes the improvement editor by setting DAT_006a1d7c = 0 and invalidating the window.
summaries.004D95C6: Opens the effects editor for the selected improvement.
summaries.004D9619: Opens the miscellaneous properties editor (food, gold, etc.) for the selected improvement or wonder.
summaries.004D9718: Handles listbox selection changes in the improvement editor. Validates current edits before switching to new selection.
summaries.004D986E: Creates a combo box control in the editor dialog, populating it with either improvement names or tech names depending on the control type.
summaries.004D9A9F: Creates a numeric edit control in the editor dialog.
summaries.004D9B93: Main paint handler for the improvement editor. Draws the background, improvement icon, pedia text, and labels for all editor controls.
summaries.004DA107: Full initialization of the improvement editor window. Creates the dialog, all controls, loads data, and enters the editor's message loop.
summaries.004DA9BE: Cleanup pair — destroys modal dialog context and restores SEH.
summaries.004DA9E2: Entry point for showing the improvement editor. Creates the editor context and calls editor_init.
summaries.004DAA3B: Cleanup — destroys font/resource context and restores SEH.
summaries.004DB210: Initialization stubs for the parley (diplomacy) system. FUN_004db210 calls FUN_004db225 which calls thunk_FUN_00428cb0.
summaries.004DB23F: Loads and indexes a diplomacy text file. Scans for "@" section headers and builds a lookup table mapping section names to file offsets.
summaries.004DB450: Unloads the diplomacy text index by resetting counters and freeing the buffer.
summaries.004DB481: Searches the loaded index for a section name. Returns the file offset if found, -1 otherwise.
summaries.004DB690: Builds a diplomacy transaction packet. Allocates memory, populates header with magic (0x66606660), type (0x82), size, player IDs, and serializes offer data.
summaries.004DBAB4: Serializes a diplomacy offer into a packet buffer. Handles 7 offer types: treaty (5/6), techs (8), gold (9), units (10), maps (0xc), attitude (0xd), cities+units (0x11).
summaries.004DBEE6: Builds a human-readable description of a diplomacy transaction. Handles all offer types including bilateral trades (type 0xe) and demands (type 0xf).
summaries.004DCAFA: Builds text description of tech items in a diplomacy offer. Lists tech names with proper comma/and separators.
summaries.004DCC0C: Builds text description of a gold amount in a diplomacy offer.
summaries.004DCC83: Builds text description of unit items in a diplomacy offer. Groups units by type with counts.
summaries.004DCEA5: Builds text description of cities and their populations in a diplomacy offer.
summaries.004DD016: Builds text description of an attitude/relationship change request (war, peace, alliance). Uses different phrasing depending on whether this is a demand (param_2=0) or offer (param_2=1).
summaries.004DD176: Builds text description of map sharing items in a diplomacy offer. Lists civ names with proper separators.
summaries.004DD285: Executes a completed diplomacy transaction. Dispatches to appropriate handler for each offer type, then checks if either civ has been eliminated.
summaries.004DD8AD: Executes map sharing between two civs. Reveals tiles, transfers unit/city visibility, and updates trade routes and supply counts.
summaries.004DDE9E: Gives a list of technologies from one civ to another. Iterates the tech list and calls thunk_FUN_00467825 with flag 0x2000 for each.
summaries.004DDF04: Transfers gold between two civs. Clamps to available amount, adjusts treasuries, and modifies diplomatic relations based on amount.
summaries.004DDFB2: Gives technologies from param_2 to param_1. For each tech in the list, checks if param_2 knows it and param_1 doesn't, then transfers.
summaries.004DE049: Transfers units from their current owner to param_1. Iterates unit list, looks up each unit, and calls parley_transfer_city if valid.
summaries.004DE0E2: Transfers a city from one civ to another. Updates ownership, visibility, trade routes, unit assignments, garrison reassignment, and triggers notifications.
summaries.004DE990: Transfers ownership of specific units from one civ to another. Handles splitting unit stacks, finding valid placement tiles, and updating ownership records.
summaries.004DED07: Finds a valid tile to place a transferred unit. Searches 45 tiles around the unit's current position for an unoccupied tile matching the unit's domain (land/sea/air).
summaries.004DEF54: Builds text describing a treaty type (ceasefire, peace, alliance, withdrawal) for diplomacy descriptions. Uses different wording for demands (param_2=0) vs offers (param_2=1).
summaries.004DF10F: Executes a treaty agreement between two civs. Sets or clears diplomatic status flags (ceasefire=2, peace=4, alliance=8).
summaries.004E0140: Calls two initialization functions — stack allocation and atexit registration.
summaries.004E015A: Allocates 0x1000 bytes of stack space via CRT function.
summaries.004E0179: Registers FUN_004e0196 as an atexit cleanup handler.
summaries.004E0196: Atexit handler that calls thunk_FUN_00578770 (likely frees menu resources).
summaries.004E01B0: Shows a dialog to select a wonder from the list of 28 wonders (0x1c). User can browse and select one to view.
summaries.004E02CB: Dialog destructor for show_wonder_selection — frees an allocated buffer via thunk_FUN_0059df8a.
summaries.004E02EF: Initializes the layout of all city windows. Computes window positions based on screen resolution (standard or hi-res mode), creates rect structures, and resets city window state for all 8 players.
summaries.004E068D: Handles loading a saved game. Verifies the save file, resets game state, initializes city windows, refreshes the map display, and starts the game. Falls back to title screen on failure.
summaries.004E0A8C: Sets a dialog checkbox state. Converts param_2 to boolean (!=0) and calls the checkbox setter.
summaries.004E0AB0: Shows the game options dialog with 11 checkboxes. On OK, reads checkbox states and updates the game options flags (sound, animations, tutorial, etc.).
summaries.004E0D71: Shows graphic options dialog with 6 checkboxes for display settings (throne room, animated heralds, high-res maps, etc.).
summaries.004E0F18: Shows multiplayer game options dialog. Handles turn timer and unit movement doubling settings, with server coordination for multiplayer games.
summaries.004E1314: Toggles unit movement point doubling for multiplayer. Doubles or halves all land unit movement points.
summaries.004E1452: Shows message notification options dialog with 11 checkboxes controlling which game events generate notifications.
summaries.004E1763: Removes a civilization from the game (kill or retire). Destroys all units, removes cities, updates map visibility, and handles multiplayer synchronization.
summaries.004E22C9: Handles the quit or retire game action. Shows confirmation dialog, optionally shows retirement score screens, then either returns to title or removes the player from multiplayer.
summaries.004E2597: Toggles hidden terrain debug mode. Sets a flag, refreshes the map, shows a message, then unsets the flag and refreshes again.
summaries.004E25EF: Shows a dialog to pick background music. Detects CD type (Fan Worlds=0x18, Scenario=0x12, Gold=0xc, Original=10) by querying the drive, then shows a track selection dialog.
summaries.004E27DF: Dialog destructor for pick_music — frees an allocated buffer via thunk_FUN_0059df8a.
summaries.004E2803: Main menu command dispatcher. Routes menu command IDs (0x101-0x9f0) to their handler functions. Guards against execution during network operations or other busy states.
summaries.004E3A86: Constructs the entire main menu bar from text file data. Reads menu structure from "@GAME", "@KINGDOM", etc. sections, creates 9 top-level menus with all items, separators, and keyboard shortcuts.
summaries.004E4C92: Updates a menu item's label text and enabled state. Loads a string resource, formats it, and applies to the specified menu item.
summaries.004E4CEB: Updates all menu item enabled/disabled states based on current game state. Handles unit orders visibility, cheat menu availability, multiplayer restrictions, and context-sensitive labels.
summaries.004E7240: Calls manage_window_C692 with the window handle from the object's field at offset 8.
summaries.004E7270: Acquires (completes) a wonder for a city. In single-player, directly assigns the wonder. In multiplayer, sends request to server and waits for confirmation.
summaries.004E7458: Classifies a production item: returns 0 for positive (improvement/wonder), 1 for units (negative but > -0x22), 2 for special (< -0x22).
summaries.004E7492: Initializes two global production variables from a city's current production type and accumulated shields.
summaries.004E74DF: Calculates adjusted food box size based on difficulty. If production type changed, applies a penalty percentage from DAT_0064bcda.
summaries.004E7549: Sets a worker tile status (2-bit field) in a city's tile assignment bitmask. Each tile uses 2 bits in the 32-bit field at DAT_0064f356.
summaries.004E75A6: Gets a worker tile status (2-bit field) from a city's tile assignment bitmask. Returns 1 for tiles beyond index 15.
summaries.004E75EA: Counts how many tiles in a city have a specific worker status value. Loops through 16 tiles.
summaries.004E7641: Evaluates all 25 tiles around a city (21 workable + center) and sets status flags in DAT_006a6530 array. Flags: 1=unavailable, 4=hostile unit present, 8=city present, 0x20=hostile civ's unit.
summaries.004E78CE: Returns whether a specific tile (param_2) is being worked by city param_1. Checks bit in 32-bit field.
summaries.004E790C: Sets or clears a tile's worked status in a city's worked-tiles bitmask.
summaries.004E7967: Calculates distance to capital and corruption-related variables for a city. Finds the nearest city with a palace, checks for trade route connections, and evaluates nearby enemy units.
summaries.004E7D7F: Checks if a unit requires shield support based on government type. Increments counters and returns non-zero if the unit exceeds the free support limit.
summaries.004E7EB1: Calculates the food box size (rows to grow) for a city. Base value depends on difficulty and government, with AI adjustments based on game state.
summaries.004E80B1: Calculates shield production rows and unit support costs for a city. Iterates all units, determines which need support, and tracks out-of-city military units.
summaries.004E868F: Calculates the food/shield/trade yield for a single city tile. Applies terrain bonuses, irrigation/mining/road modifiers, government bonuses, special resources, and pollution effects.
summaries.004E8C8C: Checks if auto-irrigation/mining should be triggered for a tile based on terrain type and government level.
summaries.004E8DB5: Checks if a road should be auto-built on a tile for trade bonus. Only triggers if tile has no river and no existing road.
summaries.004E8E4D: Calculates all 3 resource types (food/shields/trade) for a tile and accumulates into city totals.
summaries.004E8ECF: Clears all worked tile assignments for a city. Returns flag indicating if any tile had enemy unit (0x20 flag).
summaries.004E8F42: Assigns city workers to optimal tiles. Uses a multi-pass greedy algorithm considering food, shields, and trade priorities. Handles forced laborers, entertainers, and starvation detection.
summaries.004E9719: Adjusts the specialist count stored in the upper 6 bits of DAT_0064f370. Adds or removes specialists.
summaries.004E97AE: Synchronizes worker tile status flags with the current tile assignment state. Sets status=1 for assigned tiles that were previously unassigned.
summaries.004E9849: Calculates a corruption divisor based on government type (0-5). Returns 4 for anarchy, scaling up: +1 for govt>0, +1 for govt>1, +1 for govt>2, +1 for govt>4.
summaries.004E989A: Calculates trade corruption for a city based on distance to capital, government type, and difficulty. Communism uses fixed rate; others use distance-based formula.
summaries.004E9C14: Calculates a city's production output including building bonuses, factory effects, and waste. Determines trade multiplier from libraries/universities/research labs, factory/power plant bonuses for shields, and corruption-adjusted final values.
summaries.004EA031: Adjusts happy/content/unhappy citizen counts to ensure they sum correctly. Balances specialists and content citizens when there are too many unhappy.
summaries.004EA1F6: Distributes a city's trade income into luxury, tax, and science based on the government's tax rate settings. Applies marketplace, bank, stock exchange, and science building multipliers.
summaries.004EA8E4: Complete happiness calculation for a city. Determines content/happy/unhappy citizens based on government, martial law, luxury, wonders, and improvements. Also calculates trade route income and corruption.
summaries.004EB327: Calculates trade route income. Counts deficit routes (marked negative) and surplus routes from other cities.
summaries.004EB4A1: Complete city recalculation — assigns workers, calculates trade routes, syncs tile status, computes production, and determines happiness. This is the master "recalc everything" function for a city.
summaries.004EB4ED: Entry point for full city production calculation. If param_2 is 0, returns cached happy-unhappy difference. Otherwise performs full recalculation.
summaries.004EB571: Shows a city event notification dialog (e.g., "Building completed", "Famine", etc.). Creates a popup with buttons for OK/Continue.
summaries.004EB7E5: Dialog destructor for city event dialog — frees an allocated buffer via thunk_FUN_0059df8a.
summaries.004EB80A: Enhanced version of city event dialog with a production item image. Creates a larger dialog showing the completed building/wonder.
summaries.004EBBAD: CString destructor cleanup for show_city_event_dialog_v2 — calls FUN_005cde4d to destroy a CString object.
summaries.004EBBDE: Processes city food production at end of turn. Handles famine (population loss/settler disbanding), growth (population increase/aqueduct check), and we-love-the-king day celebrations.
summaries.004EC1C6: Assigns a trade commodity to a newly built caravan/freight unit. Randomly selects from the city's 3 supply commodities.
summaries.004EC312: Handles discovery of espionage (spy embassy established). Halves defense rating, sets espionage flag, and penalizes diplomatic relations with all other civs.
summaries.004EC3FE: Massive end-of-turn city production processing function. Handles completing buildings, wonders, units, and special items. Processes unit upgrades, rush-buy AI logic, wonder races, and space race components.
summaries.004EEEE7: First dialog resource destructor for process_city_production — frees a buffer via thunk_FUN_0059df8a.
summaries.004EEF23: Handles unit support deficit — when a city can't support all its units, disbands the furthest ones. Also handles converting excess land units to shields for Fundamentalist government.
summaries.004EF578: Handles civil disorder and "we love the king" celebrations. Sets disorder flags, triggers revolution under sustained disorder for communist/fundamentalist governments, and manages celebration growth.
summaries.004EFBC6: Processes a city's science contribution. Doubles science for the researching civ if they're first to discover the tech. For AI civs in space race, doubles science for missing space tech.
summaries.004EFD44: Handles city pollution generation and nuclear meltdown checks. Randomly pollutes a tile near the city based on production level, and checks for nuclear plant meltdown.
summaries.004E02E1: SEH epilog for show_wonder_selection. Restores the previous exception handler frame.
summaries.004E27F5: SEH epilog for pick_music. Restores the previous exception handler frame.
summaries.004EB7FB: SEH epilog for city event dialog. Restores the previous exception handler frame.
summaries.004EBBB9: Modal dialog cleanup for show_city_event_dialog_v2 — calls FUN_005c656b to pop the modal dialog state.
summaries.004EBBCF: SEH epilog for show_city_event_dialog_v2. Restores the previous exception handler frame.
summaries.004EEEF3: Second dialog resource destructor for process_city_production — frees a buffer via thunk_FUN_0059df8a.
summaries.004EEEFF: Third dialog resource destructor for process_city_production — frees a buffer via thunk_FUN_0059df8a.
summaries.004F00F0: Calculates the upkeep cost for a specific building type for a given civ. Adjusts cost based on difficulty level, tech prereqs, and government type (Fundamentalism exempts certain buildings).
summaries.004F0221: Processes building upkeep for a city. Iterates all 38 building types, deducts upkeep cost from civ treasury, and sells buildings if treasury goes negative.
summaries.004F03B7: Searches for a nearby expansion site for a city's settler/engineer. Uses pathfinding to find reachable empty tiles on the same continent, prioritizing friendly or neutral territory.
summaries.004F080D: Handles city expansion by checking adjacent tiles for buildable land and dispatching settler/engineer creation. Adjusts expansion priority based on city size and palace.
summaries.004F0A9C: Main city turn processing function. Handles food storage, production, pollution, building upkeep, city expansion, and disorder checks. Returns city value or -999 if city was destroyed.
summaries.004F1220: Handles space race victory condition. If multiplayer scenario version < 3 or scenario flag set, reveals entire map and gives all civs full visibility. Otherwise sends network notification.
summaries.004F3D30: Returns a byte from the city tile flag array at the given index.
summaries.004F3D60: Constructor for the Civilopedia window object. Sets up SEH frame, initializes dialog base, allocates stack, sets vtable pointer.
summaries.004F3E20: Scalar deleting destructor for the Civilopedia window. Calls destructor then optionally frees memory.
summaries.004F3E70: Destructor for the Civilopedia window. Sets up SEH frame, calls cleanup helpers.
summaries.004F3EBB: Helper for pedia destructor. Adjusts a member pointer and calls cleanup.
summaries.004F3EE5: Calls CDaoFieldInfo destructor on a member.
summaries.004F3EF8: Restores FS segment register from SEH chain. Standard SEH epilog.
summaries.004F3F30: Wrapper to display a city-related message. Passes through to thunk_FUN_004eb571 with param_3 inserted as 0.
summaries.004F3F60: Sets a global flag used by the Civilopedia system.
summaries.004F3F80: CRT static initializer. Calls pedia manager constructor and registers atexit handler.
summaries.004F3F9A: Thunk that calls the pedia manager constructor.
summaries.004F3FB4: Registers the pedia manager destructor for atexit cleanup.
summaries.004F3FD1: Atexit handler that calls the pedia manager destructor.
summaries.004F3FEB: Constructor for the Civilopedia manager object. Initializes 15 sub-panels, a scrollable listbox, a scrollbar, sets vtable, and zeroes out index arrays for advances (100), improvements (39), wonders (28), units (62), governments (7), terrain (33), and concepts (256).
summaries.004F44A7: Destructor for the Civilopedia manager. Frees string lists, clears item lists, destroys scrollbar objects, then destructs all 15 sub-panels and the sprite cache in reverse order.
summaries.004F4763: Fifteen identical thunks (0x004F4691-0x004F4763) that each destruct one dialog panel via thunk_FUN_0040f570.
summaries.004F4785: SEH epilog that restores FS register.
summaries.004F4793: Clears the linked list of Civilopedia display items. Walks the list at offset 8000 in the object, calling scalar deleting destructor on each node, then calls thunk_FUN_00419b80 for final cleanup.
summaries.004F4809: Frees all 7 string linked lists used by the Civilopedia (advances, improvements, wonders, units, governments, terrain, concepts). Each list is at a different offset in the object (0x16dc-0x16f4).
summaries.004F4B9F: Initializes the complete layout of the Civilopedia window. Creates all buttons for the different category tabs (advances, improvements, wonders, units, governments, terrain, concepts, search), sets up scrollbar, panel dimensions, and loads all index data from describe.txt.
summaries.004F5B24: Resets the Civilopedia view to initial state. Invalidates cache if active, clears item list, resets navigation, closes display.
summaries.004F5B6F: Handles the "Back" button in the Civilopedia. Navigates to the previous history entry, restoring category, subcategory, and selection state.
summaries.004F5DD1: Closes the Civilopedia display panel. Resets navigation state, frees resources, restores previous UI.
summaries.004F5E52: Refreshes the current Civilopedia page. Clears items, redraws frame, header, and content.
summaries.004F5ED2: Shows the search category (8) in the Civilopedia.
summaries.004F5F23: Handles selection of a specific entry in the Civilopedia. Shows the appropriate article based on category: advances (1/8), improvements (2), wonders (3), units (4), terrain (5), governments (6), or concepts (7).
summaries.004F6244: Draws the decorative frame around the Civilopedia window including borders, title text with shadow effect, and background panels.
summaries.004F6564: Draws a background panel for the Civilopedia. Uses one of two cached background images (top or bottom) or falls back to default fill.
summaries.004F6646: Dispatches to the appropriate item list drawing function based on current subcategory state.
summaries.004F66C6: Renders the scrollable item list in the Civilopedia. Shows 2 columns of 9 items each, with category-specific icons (tech tree sprites, building sprites, unit sprites, terrain sprites, etc.) and text labels.
summaries.004F7313: Sets the scroll position to a page boundary and redraws the item list.
summaries.004F734A: Hit-tests a click position against the Civilopedia item list. Returns the item index at the click position, or negative values for border regions.
summaries.004F7454: Ensures a given item index is visible by adjusting the scroll position if needed.
summaries.004F74EB: Finds the next entry in the current Civilopedia category that starts with a given letter (for keyboard navigation). Searches from current selection forward, wrapping around.
summaries.004F76CE: Handles keyboard input in the Civilopedia item list view. Supports arrow keys, page up/down, home/end, and letter keys for jump-to-entry.
summaries.004F7A30: Handles mouse click on the Civilopedia item list. Hit-tests the click, selects the clicked item, and redraws.
summaries.004F7AC7: Sets the title text for the Civilopedia window based on the category. Uses label IDs 0x29f-0x2a5 for categories 1-7.
summaries.004F7BD1: Opens a specific category in the Civilopedia. Initializes display state, sets title, configures tabs, and optionally shows the window.
summaries.004F7C99: Loads all Civilopedia index data from the describe.txt file. Parses 7 sections: advances, improvements, wonders, units, governments, terrain, and concepts. Creates sorted linked lists for each category.
summaries.004F896A: Bubble sorts the Civilopedia entry linked list alphabetically, also reordering parallel index arrays.
summaries.004F8A9B: Gets the name string for a Civilopedia entry by index from a linked list.
summaries.004F8AF9: Pushes the current navigation state onto the Civilopedia history stack for back-button support. Avoids duplicate entries.
summaries.004F8D04: Timer handler for the Civilopedia. Calls a paint update and conditionally invalidates the display cache.
summaries.004F8D51: Forces invalidation of the Civilopedia display cache.
summaries.004FA0F0: Standard MFC scalar deleting destructor for CControlBarInfo.
summaries.004FA1C0: CRT static initializer for the scenario event manager object.
summaries.004FA1DA: Constructs the static event manager object.
summaries.004FA1F4: Registers event manager destructor for atexit.
summaries.004FA211: Atexit handler that destructs the static event manager.
summaries.004FA250: Resolves a civilization name string to a civ index for the scenario event system. Handles special tokens: ANYBODY (-2), TRIGGERATTACKER (-3), TRIGGERDEFENDER/TRIGGERRECEIVER (-4).
summaries.004FA359: Resolves a unit type name to its index for scenario events. Handles ANYUNIT (-2) special token.
summaries.004FA403: Resolves a terrain type name to its index for scenario events.
summaries.004FA47E: Resets the event manager's memory pool, reinitializing it with a capacity of 12 items.
summaries.004FA4BE: Constructor for the event manager object. Allocates stack, initializes string pool, sets counters to 0, calls init.
summaries.004FA569: Destructor for the event manager. Frees memory pool and cleans up SEH.
summaries.004FA5D9: Initializes the event manager. Zeros counters and calls reset_pool.
summaries.004FA617: Allocates a new event node from the pool, appends to the linked list, and returns it zeroed.
summaries.004FA707: Event action: plays a WAV sound file. Searches game directory then root directory.
summaries.004FA82D: Event action: sets the no-schism flag to prevent civil war.
summaries.004FA854: Event action: plays a CD audio track. Polls CD drive up to 10 times until a valid track count is found, then plays the specified track.
summaries.004FA944: Event action: modifies a civilization's treasury. Resolves receiver from trigger context, validates civ, adds/subtracts amount, clamps to 0-30000.
summaries.004FAAB0: Event action: displays text popup with up to 20 lines. In multiplayer, also sends text via network.
summaries.004FABA6: Event action: forces aggression between two civs. Resolves both civs from trigger context, validates both are alive, then triggers aggression.
summaries.004FAD02: Event action: destroys a civilization. Resolves target civ, validates alive, sets game over flag if human player, then calls destroy function.
summaries.004FADFB: Event action: gives a technology to a civilization.
summaries.004FAED4: Event action: creates a unit at one of up to 10 specified locations. Validates terrain accessibility (sea units need water, land units need land/city), creates unit, optionally assigns veteran status and home city.
summaries.004FB29F: Event action: moves matching units to a new location. Only works for AI-controlled civs. Finds units matching type and owner within the specified map rectangle, sets their orders to goto (0xb) with target coordinates.
summaries.004FB5B2: Event action: changes terrain in a rectangular area. Destroys all cities and units in the area first, then overwrites terrain type and clears tile improvements. Recalculates city tile workers and handles civ elimination if all cities destroyed.
summaries.004FBA0C: Checks all events for TURN triggers. Fires events where type == 4 and (turn == -1 for EVERY turn, or turn == param_1).
summaries.004FBA9C: Checks all events for TURNINTERVAL triggers. Fires events where type == 8 and (interval == 0 or turn % interval == 0).
summaries.004FBB2F: Checks all events for RANDOMTURN triggers. Fires events where type == 0x40 and random roll matches denominator.
summaries.004FBBDD: Checks all events for RECEIVEDTECHNOLOGY triggers. Fires when a civ acquires the specified technology.
summaries.004FBD2B: Checks all events for SCENARIOLOADED triggers (type == 0x20). Fires all matching events unconditionally.
summaries.004FBD9D: Checks all events for UNITKILLED triggers. Fires when specified unit type is killed by specified attacker/defender civs.
summaries.004FBE84: Checks all events for NEGOTIATION triggers. Complex matching of talker/listener by civ and human/computer type. In multiplayer, also checks reverse direction.
summaries.004FC20D: Checks all events for NOSCHISM triggers. If any matching event fires, returns 0 (prevents schism).
summaries.004FC2BB: Checks all events for CITYTAKEN triggers. Fires when a specific city is captured by matching attacker/defender civs.
summaries.004FC3AE: Dispatches all actions for a triggered event. Checks action flags in the event node and calls appropriate action handlers. Handles JUSTONCE by setting a "fired" flag.
summaries.004FC516: Parses the entire scenario events.txt file. This is the largest function in the block (12KB). Implements a state machine parser that reads @IF conditions (UNITKILLED, NEGOTIATION, NOSCHISM, RECEIVEDTECHNOLOGY, CITYTAKEN, TURN, TURNINTERVAL, RANDOMTURN, SCENARIOLOADED) and @THEN actions (TEXT, CHANGE
summaries.00500E00: CRT static initializer. Creates a 16-point font and registers atexit destructor.
summaries.00500E6F: CRT static initializer. Creates a 10-point font and registers atexit.
summaries.00500EDE: CRT static initializer for the city window object.
summaries.00500F49: CRT static initializers for two surface/bitmap objects. Each calls FUN_005bd630 for construction and registers FUN_005bd915 for atexit destruction.
summaries.0050101F: Loads the city window background image (CITY.GIF). Caches the result keyed by zoom level (DAT_006aa78c). Only reloads if zoom level changed.
summaries.005011B4: Creates and renders an arrow button (left/right navigation) for the city window. Draws button with border, arrow icon centered within the button rect.
summaries.005013BC: Shows a modal city refresh message. Guards against reentrancy with DAT_00630d1c flag. Displays CITYMODAL message and optionally closes city dialog.
summaries.00501440: Initializes city window member variables to defaults. Sets panel flags, clears selection, resets view mode.
summaries.005014BF: Closes the city window dialog panel (region 2).
summaries.005014E5: Closes all three sub-panel dialogs in the city window.
summaries.0050152B: Closes all panels then destroys the city window.
summaries.00501551: Constructor for the city window object. Sets up SEH frame, initializes base classes, sets vtable, initializes member variables.
summaries.0050160A: Destructor for the city window. Sets vtable, closes/destroys all panels, destructs sub-objects in reverse order.
summaries.005016B2: Blits a city window panel from the offscreen buffer to the display surface, accounting for scroll offset.
summaries.00501733: Prepares a panel for drawing: clears surface, sets draw state, blits background.
summaries.00501780: Refreshes the top panels of the city window (citizens, resources, map). Only refreshes if no modal state is active.
summaries.00501819: Handles clicking on a citizen icon in the city window. Cycles through specialist types (entertainer/taxman/scientist). Shows error if trying to convert last citizen.
summaries.005019C1: Draws all citizen icons for the city: happy citizens, content citizens, unhappy citizens, and specialists (entertainers/taxmen/scientists). Uses epoch-appropriate sprites.
summaries.00501E63: Simplified version of citizen icon drawing for the happiness breakdown section. Same layout but without shadow/highlight effects.
summaries.0050207F: Draws the citizen row panel at the top of the city window: header labels (food/shields produced), citizen icons, and click regions for each citizen.
summaries.005022C0: Handles clicking on the resource map in the city window. Determines which tile was clicked using isometric hit-testing, then toggles the tile worker assignment on/off.
summaries.005025D5: The largest function in this block (9.7KB). Draws the complete resource display panel for the city window: the city radius resource map with terrain/city/unit sprites, food/shield/trade resource bars with surplus/deficit indicators, food storage bar, production bar, and trade distribution (tax/scien
summaries.00504C05: Draws the food storage box in the city window. Shows granary icon with food units stacked in rows, plus granary building icon if present or wonder-equivalent active.
summaries.0050503E: Draws the production box in the city window. Shows the item being produced (unit sprite or building icon) and production shield progress.
summaries.005055DD: Draws the buy button panel area. Simple panel prepare and invalidate.
summaries.00505666: Draws the supported units panel in the city window. Shows unit sprites with upkeep cost icons (shields, food, gold) for each unit homed to this city.
summaries.00505D3D: Handles selling a city improvement. Shows confirmation dialog, removes building, adds sale price to treasury, sets "sold this turn" flag.
summaries.00505FFA: Draws the city improvements list with building names and wonders. Each entry shows building icon and name. Supports scrolling.
summaries.00506448: Formats unit information text for a city window unit popup: civ name, veteran status, unit type, location coordinates, nearest city, and home city.
summaries.00506637: Handles the unit popup menu when clicking a supported unit in the city window. Options: activate (goto to unit), disband (adds production to city), center on unit, or rename.
summaries.00506A42: Handles the unit popup menu when clicking a present (visiting) unit in the city window. Extended options: activate, disband, fortify, assign home city, unload, goto, rename.
summaries.005070E5: Draws the city info panel (present units, trade routes, map view). Shows units present at city tile in grid layout with click regions, trade route info, and mini-map of city surroundings.
summaries.00507B69: Draws the world mini-map view in the city info panel. Shows terrain (land vs water), unit positions, and city location highlighted.
summaries.00507F31: Draws building bonus icons (marketplace, bank, stock exchange, palace) in the happiness breakdown panel.
summaries.00508177: Draws the "military/garrison" section of the happiness breakdown. Shows garrison units for democracies/republics, or government name for despotism/communism.
summaries.00508552: Draws the full happiness breakdown panel showing 5 rows: base happiness, luxury effect, building effect, military presence, and wonders. Each row shows citizen transitions between happy/content/unhappy.
summaries.00508ADC: Dispatches city info panel drawing based on current view mode: 0=units/trade, 1=world map, 2=happiness breakdown.
summaries.00508BC5: Redraws all 8 city window panels: citizens, resources, food storage, production, buy, supported units, improvements, and info.
summaries.00508C84: Calculates a panel rectangle from scaled coordinates, accounting for zoom level and window offset.
summaries.00508D24: Sets up all 12 city window panel rectangles at their correct positions and sizes.
summaries.00508EC6: Draws the border/margin fills around the city window panels to handle window sizes larger than the city view.
summaries.00509028: Calculates city window zoom level (1/2/3) based on window dimensions, sets up fonts, loads background, centers the city view, and initializes all button panels.
