summaries.005092AF: Builds and displays the city window title bar text: city name, zoom indicator, content citizen count, and treasury.
summaries.00509429: Full city window refresh. Validates city still exists, recalculates yields, redraws all panels and title.
summaries.0050951F: Shows the city window by calling window show functions.
summaries.00509545: Brings the city window to the top of the Z-order.
summaries.00509590: Opens the city window for a specific city, handling disorder state. Checks network busy flags, sets city index, shows city window (modal or non-modal), creates Close and Change buttons, shows tutorial hints on first disorder.
summaries.0050994F: Closes the city window. Sets close flag, frees resources, restores previous UI state.
summaries.0050998F: Calculates and sets the city window position/size based on zoom level, centering on screen.
summaries.00509A49: Auto-selects zoom level based on display resolution. Sets zoom to 2 (or 3 if resolution > 999).
summaries.00509A82: Sets the city window animation flag to 1 (start animating).
summaries.00509A9C: Clears animation flag and refreshes city window.
summaries.00509AC0: Formats the "turns to complete" text for city production. Calculates turns = (cost * gold_per_shield - 1 - current_shields) / net_production + 1.
summaries.00509B48: Handles the "Buy" button in the city window. Calculates rush-buy cost (2x remaining shields for buildings, 2x + shields^2/20 for units, doubled if no production started), shows confirmation, deducts gold, completes production.
summaries.0050A1B2: SEH cleanup helper for city_button_buy. Calls thunk_FUN_0059df8a to destroy the stack frame object.
summaries.0050A1C8: SEH epilog for city_button_buy. Restores the FS register from the saved stack frame.
summaries.0050A1D6: Draws a unit or wonder sprite at a specific position in the production change dialog, with vertical centering.
summaries.0050A2F7: Draws the "best city" sprite for a given civ in the production change dialog. Finds the largest city of that civ and draws its sprite.
summaries.0050A473: The second-largest function in this block (4.5KB). Handles the city production change dialog. Shows list of all buildable units, improvements, and wonders. Handles production selection, auto-production mode, advisor recommendations, penalized production switching, and wonder assignment.
summaries.0050B638: Stack frame destructor for city_button_change's first stack allocation.
summaries.0050B644: Stack frame destructor for city_button_change's second stack allocation.
summaries.0050B650: Stack frame destructor for city_button_change's third stack allocation.
summaries.0050B674: Timer-based city window handler. Detects when city window should auto-close (e.g., in multiplayer when turn ends) with a 1200ms timeout.
summaries.0050B74E: Handles the city rename button. Shows text input dialog, validates, updates city name, refreshes displays.
summaries.0050B9A4: View-mode toggle button for the city info panel. Sets DAT_006aa768 = 0 (units/trade view), recalculates yields, redraws info panel, and plays click sound.
summaries.0050BA07: View-mode toggle button for the city info panel. Sets DAT_006aa768 = 1 (world map view), recalculates yields, redraws info panel, and plays click sound.
summaries.0050BA6A: View-mode toggle button for the city info panel. Sets DAT_006aa768 = 2 (happiness breakdown view), recalculates yields, redraws info panel, and plays click sound.
summaries.0050BACD: Handles the "View" button (shows advisor/improvement details). Defers if network busy.
summaries.0050BC4F: Close button handler for city window. Plays sound, closes city window (or sets close flag in modal mode), refreshes advisor reports.
summaries.0050BCDA: Scrollbar handler for improvement list. Updates scroll position and redraws.
summaries.0050BD13: Previous city navigation button. Finds the city alphabetically before the current one (among cities owned by the same civ) and opens it. Wraps around to the last city if at the beginning.
summaries.0050BF72: Next city navigation button. Finds the city alphabetically after the current one (among cities owned by the same civ) and opens it. Wraps around to the first city if at the end.
summaries.0050C1D1: Main mouse click dispatcher for the city window. Hit-tests click regions and dispatches to: resource map (1), citizen row (2), present units (3), improvement list (4), supported units (6).
summaries.0050C405: Left-click handler for the city window. Forwards the mouse coordinates to city_mouse with param_3 = 0 (left button).
summaries.0050C427: Right-click handler for the city window. Forwards the mouse coordinates to city_mouse with param_3 = 1 (right button).
summaries.0050C449: Handles city deletion notification. If deleted city == current city, closes city window.
summaries.0050C494: Handles unit movement notification. If a unit moves to/from the city tile, refreshes the relevant panels.
summaries.0050C679: Refreshes city window if the specified city index matches the currently displayed city.
summaries.0050C6EF: Refreshes top panels when a tile within distance 3 of the city is modified.
summaries.0050C7A3: Zeroes all 10 button/widget object pointers in the city window (offsets 0x16b4-0x16d8).
summaries.0050C859: Destroys all city window buttons and widgets (up to 11 objects), then zeros all pointers.
summaries.0050CA8D: Creates the "Close" button for the city window. Positions it based on panel mode (normal vs. advisor view).
summaries.0050CCB3: Creates/updates the "Change" production button. Button label changes based on whether city has auto-production enabled (flag 0x10 in city flags).
summaries.0050CF06: Creates all buttons and widgets for the city window: Close, Buy, Change, three view toggle buttons (Units/Map/Happiness), two arrow navigation buttons, Rename, View, and a scrollbar for the improvements list. Also sets up the sprite scaling and loads the unit sprite sheet.
summaries.0050DADA: Sets advisor display mode when entering production advisor. Marks need for button recreation.
summaries.0050DB36: Restores normal button state after leaving advisor mode.
summaries.0050DB92: Window management helper. Calls thunk_FUN_004080f0 on the city window rect at DAT_00655344.
summaries.0050DBB8: Window management helper. Calls thunk_FUN_004080f0 on the city window rect at DAT_00655344, same as citywin_DB92.
summaries.0050DBDE: Handles city window control commands: 1=close, 2=zoom out (decrease DAT_006aa78c), 3=zoom in (increase, max 3 at high res).
summaries.0050DCB6: Full initialization of the city window. Initializes members, auto-selects zoom, creates window, registers message handlers (close, paint, key, mouse, resize, timer), registers sprite sheets.
summaries.0050DEA8: Marks the city window as closing: sets 0x15a0 = 1, destroys all buttons, cleans up.
summaries.00511320: Returns a 4-byte value at offset +4 from the ECX (this) pointer. Simple member field accessor.
summaries.00511350: Clamps a value `param_3` to the range [param_1, param_2]. If param_3 >= param_1 and param_1 < param_2, returns param_2; otherwise returns param_1 (lower bound).
summaries.005113B0: Draws a horizontal line by delegating to thunk_FUN_005a97cc with the global drawing surface DAT_00635c64 and 4 coordinate parameters.
summaries.005113F0: Draws a 3D frame/border by delegating to thunk_FUN_005a99fc (draw_3d_border) with the global surface and 3 params.
summaries.00511430: Initializes the global text buffer at DAT_00679640 by calling a string init function.
summaries.00511460: Invalidates a rectangular region by unpacking a RECT structure (param_3) and computing width/height deltas, then calling thunk_FUN_0046ace7.
summaries.005114D0: Blits a sprite with 8 parameters by calling thunk_FUN_00548c78 with the global surface prepended.
summaries.00511520: Draws text at a location by calling thunk_FUN_004a6cc5 with DAT_006359d4 (a popup/dialog context) and 3 params.
summaries.00511560: Scalar deleting destructor. Calls base destructor then conditionally frees memory if bit 0 of param_1 is set.
summaries.005115B0: Base destructor for an MFC-style object. Sets up SEH, conditionally frees a sub-object at offset +0x1c, then calls a chain of cleanup functions.
summaries.00511624: Cleanup helper calling FUN_005bd915 (a CString destructor or similar).
summaries.00511630: Another cleanup helper identical to FUN_00511624.
summaries.0051163C: Cleanup helper calling thunk_FUN_0040f510 (likely a CWnd destructor).
summaries.0051164F: SEH epilogue — restores the FS segment chain from the saved value on the stack frame.
summaries.00511690: Scales a value based on the display scale factor at `this + 0x15d4`. If scale != 2, returns `(scale * param_1) / 2`; otherwise returns param_1 unchanged.
summaries.005116F0: Formats a string into the global buffer DAT_00679640 using thunk_FUN_0043ce5a.
summaries.00511720: Constructor for an MFC-derived class. Initializes base class, constructs 2 sub-objects, and sets a byte at offset +200 to 0.
summaries.005117F0: Sets member field at offset +0x2c of the this pointer to param_1.
summaries.00511820: MFC library function. Sets member at offset +0x2cc to param_1. Used throughout for various flag-setting on property sheets.
summaries.00511850: Second instance of EnableStackedTabs with offset +0x2d0.
summaries.00511880: Enqueues a multiplayer event message. If MP mode (DAT_00655b02 > 2), serializes string list and integer list into a buffer, then sends via thunk_FUN_0046b14d with message type 0x6a.
summaries.00511A0E: Appends a multiplayer event node to a singly-linked list. Allocates a node with header fields and optional payload data.
summaries.00511ACF: Frees all nodes in the multiplayer event linked list and resets head/tail to NULL/0.
summaries.00511B2D: Processes pending messages and conditionally invalidates the active popup window. Skips invalidation if no AI flag, no network state, and less than ~1.2 seconds elapsed.
summaries.00511BA2: Master multiplayer event dispatcher. Dequeues an event from the linked list and dispatches it through a massive switch statement (cases 0x00-0x65, ~102 cases). Handles retirement, barbarian attacks, global warming, space race, city capture, wonder events, diplomacy requests, combat results, espionag
summaries.00514220: Constructor for the network message queue. Zeroes 24000 bytes of the object, then calls reset.
summaries.00514254: Initializes the network message queue by calling reset.
summaries.00514272: Resets the network message queue. Iterates 2000 slots, freeing any allocated data buffers and zeroing entries. Sets head=400, tail=400, and counts to 0.
summaries.0051435F: Returns 1 if the message type (param_1) is in the "alpha" range [0x2a, 0x66], 0 otherwise.
summaries.0051438F: Enqueues a network message into either the primary queue (slots 400-1999, circular) or the alpha queue (slots 0-399) depending on the message type. Handles queue-full conditions with error logging.
summaries.005149D6: Dequeues a network message. Prioritizes alpha queue over primary queue. Returns sender, data pointer, and size via output params.
summaries.00514E10: Static initializer that constructs a CString and registers an atexit destructor.
summaries.00514E44: Registers FUN_00514e61 as an atexit handler.
summaries.00514E61: Destroys a static CString object at program exit.
summaries.00514E7B: Shows the advisor council dialog. Constructs the council window, initializes it with the given government type, and if init succeeds, plays the council video sequence.
summaries.00514F16: Constructs the council advisor dialog object. Initializes base classes, sub-objects (CStrings at +0x64c and +0x688), sets DAT_00631a98 to this pointer, initializes rects and display parameters.
summaries.005150B9: Destructs the council advisor dialog. Sets DAT_00631a98 = 0 and destroys all sub-objects in reverse construction order.
summaries.00515197: Cleanup helper destroying a Timevec at offset +0x648.
summaries.005151F4: Initializes the advisor council window. Loads the DLL resource, creates the dialog surface, sets up video panels, finds the advisor index matching the requested government type, draws the initial label, and positions the scroll panel.
summaries.00515516: Plays the full advisor council video sequence. For each of 12 advisors matching the current government, loads background, plays music/sound, scrolls panels up/down with animation delays, then displays advice text.
summaries.00515999: Draws an advisor label panel. In simple mode (DAT_00631a9c == 0), draws the advisor name with shadow. In detail mode, also draws advisor title and formatted text with tech year information. Blits the result to the display surface.
summaries.00515C15: Loads an advisor background image for the council panel. Creates bitmap surfaces, loads GIF resources indexed by advisor type, and composites them into the panel area.
summaries.00515DC8: Scrolls the advisor panel by a given pixel offset. Adjusts internal rects and blits the scrolled region. Positive offset scrolls down, negative scrolls up.
summaries.00515F3C: Animates the council panel scrolling down. Plays a sound, then loops scrolling by 15 pixels per frame with 22ms delays until reaching position 0x133.
summaries.00516005: Animates the council panel scrolling up. Loops scrolling by -4 pixels per frame until position reaches -0xad.
summaries.00516042: Invalidates the council dialog window for redraw.
summaries.00516063: Checks if a button ID is in the council button range (0xd0-0xd2) and invalidates the window if so.
summaries.00516570: Shows the government council (full advisor council with video). Sets DAT_00631ad0=1, constructs the council, initializes video, runs it if init succeeds, then cleans up and sets DAT_00631ad0=0.
summaries.0051661A: Constructs the government council dialog. Initializes base class, creates popup surface, dialog, 6 sub-windows. Sets vtable and DAT_00631acc to this.
summaries.005167D9: Destructs the government council dialog. Resets vtable, sets DAT_00631acc=0, destroys 9 sub-objects in reverse order.
summaries.0051689F: Government council cleanup helper. Calls thunk_FUN_0040f570 to destroy a sub-window object.
summaries.005168AE: Government council cleanup helper. Calls thunk_FUN_0040f570 to destroy a sub-window object.
summaries.005168BD: Government council cleanup helper. Calls thunk_FUN_0040f570 to destroy a sub-window object.
summaries.005168CC: Government council cleanup helper. Calls thunk_FUN_0040f570 to destroy a sub-window object.
summaries.005168DB: Government council cleanup helper. Calls thunk_FUN_0040f570 to destroy a sub-window object.
summaries.005168EA: Government council cleanup helper. Calls thunk_FUN_0040f570 to destroy a sub-window object.
summaries.005168F9: Government council cleanup helper. Calls _Timevec::~_Timevec on the sub-object at offset 0xe30 from the base pointer.
summaries.00516908: Government council cleanup helper. Calls thunk_FUN_0059df8a to destroy a stack frame object.
summaries.00516917: Government council cleanup helper. Calls FUN_005dd1a0 to destroy a video player object.
summaries.00516926: Government council cleanup helper. Calls thunk_FUN_0044cba0 to destroy a window sub-object.
summaries.00516939: SEH epilog for the government council dialog. Restores the FS register from the saved stack frame.
summaries.00516947: Initializes the government council video system. Creates the dialog surface, gets advisor recommendations, loads advisor scripts, constructs buttons, draws panels, loads all advisor video files. Handles cheat mode overrides. Returns 1 on success, 0 on failure (DLL not found, VFW not registered).
summaries.00516FD4: Runs the government council video playback loop. Flips surfaces, suspends music, initializes display, shows all panels, runs MP message loop, then cleans up.
summaries.00517158: Draws the council panel borders, frames, title text with shadow. Creates 5 advisor panel frames with 3D borders and a main speech panel.
summaries.00517673: Creates 6 buttons for the advisor council UI — 5 advisor buttons and 1 close/ok button. Each is positioned based on panel geometry.
summaries.005179A3: Parses an advisor script from a game text file. Reads advisor type codes (M=military, S=science, T=trade, F=foreign, A=attitude), matches them against recommendation values, and builds up to 3 speech entries with text lines.
summaries.00517DD3: Renders advisor speech text into the speech panel. Handles text wrapping, shadow rendering, and multi-line display with vertical advancement.
summaries.00518007: Loads all advisor video clips. For anarchy mode, loads single video. Otherwise iterates 5 advisor slots, opens script files, gets video IDs, and positions them in the panel.
summaries.005181D8: Handles button clicks in the council dialog. For the close button (0xca), clears speech and invalidates. For advisor buttons (0-4), loads the advisor's script, positions video, renders speech, and advances the speech index.
summaries.00518342: Positions the advisor video panel at the appropriate slot location. For buttons 0-4, positions at column offset.
summaries.00518392: Parses a single advisor letter code to an index: A/a=4, F/f=3, M/m=0, S/s=1, T/t=2. Returns -1 for unknown.
summaries.00518471: Auto-advances to the next advisor speech in the council sequence. Called by timer/auto-play. Positions video, renders speech, loads video for the current speech slot.
summaries.00518582: Gets an advisor recommendation for a given civ and advisor type. Dispatches to specific advisor evaluation functions based on param_2: 0=military, 1=science, 2=trade, 3=foreign, 4=attitude.
summaries.00518E80: Sets the video playback position by calling FUN_005bc4a1 with the video handle from ECX+8.
summaries.00518EC0: Shows a numeric input dialog using the standard popup system with DAT_006359d4.
summaries.00518F00: Initializes a scrollbar-driven list control. Sets up item count, scroll range, current position, and adjusts to 9-item page alignment. Copies item data from source to display arrays.
summaries.00519140: Closes the active popup if one is open (DAT_00631b78 != 0).
summaries.00519171: Shows a labeled popup dialog. Closes any existing popup first, sets DAT_00631b78=1, shows the popup with given label string.
summaries.0051D9A0: Configures multiplayer startup. Reads CIV.INI settings (NetTimeOut, Adapter, MaxPlayers). Shows multiplayer type selection dialog. Based on selection, launches hotseat, LAN, modem, or PBEM game setup.
summaries.0051DD97: Main new game setup flow. Presents difficulty, enemies, barbarians, rules dialogs. Handles advanced options including accelerated start, checkbox-based rule flags, and game timer configuration. Writes extensively to game configuration globals in 0x0065-0x0066 range.
summaries.0051EA0C: Similar to mp_check_invalidate but with additional conditions on DAT_006ad66c and DAT_006ad670, and longer timeout (0x961 = 2401ms).
summaries.0051EA8E: Shows the game timer configuration dialog. Supports preset times (30s to 5min) and custom entry. For multiplayer, broadcasts timer changes to all players and handles acceptance/rejection.
summaries.0051F11A: Checks for pending network messages and invalidates if found.
summaries.0051F19C: Massive civilization selection dialog for multiplayer setup. Handles gender selection, tribe selection (with 21 available tribes), custom tribe creation (name, leader, city names), city style selection, and PBEM email address entry. For network games, coordinates with server for slot availability.
summaries.0052181D: SEH epilog — restores previous exception handler from stack.
summaries.0052182C: Builds a label string for multiplayer dialogs. Starts with a label ID, appends an existing label prefix (if any) with a separator, then allocates and stores the result.
summaries.005218CB: Manages the multiplayer scenario load dialog flow. Loads a save/scenario file, verifies units, shows the difficulty and advanced options dialogs, and configures game parameters.
summaries.00521FE0: Manages the multiplayer player selection dialog. For each player slot (up to param_1 players), shows a dialog for choosing which civ to play as, entering a player name, and selecting gender. Assigns civs to players and configures their settings.
summaries.0052263C: Sets the animation display style for multiplayer. If param_1 == -1, cycles through styles 5->7->6->5. If param_2 is set, forces style to 5.
summaries.005226FA: Returns a scroll offset value based on a cycling state machine. States 1-4 cycle through different scroll positions (map-width-based or -999 for center).
summaries.005227E3: Shows the hotseat multiplayer configuration dialog. Allows selecting the number of human players (up to 6 slots based on DAT_006c3164). For multiplayer mode >= 3, iterates players but does nothing (empty loop).
summaries.00522B2B: Handles a player joining a multiplayer game. Temporarily sets the player bitmask to the full session mask, shows the game setup UI, counts available vs occupied slots, and triggers the player selection dialog. Restores the original player state on completion.
summaries.00522DFA: Negates (makes positive) all leader ID values in the leader table. For all 21 leader slots, converts any negative values in the male/female/neutral name IDs and the 7 city name pairs to positive.
summaries.00522F8F: Sets up AI players for a network game. First negates all non-human leader IDs. Then randomly assigns AI civs to fill remaining slots (up to DAT_00655b0d+1 minus param_1 humans). Optionally shows an opponent selection dialog if DAT_00631ee4 is set.
summaries.005233FC: Registers a network player (human or AI) into the game. Configures the civ's leader, gender, names, and city assignments based on the registration packet (param_1). Assigns a network slot from DAT_006ad30c table.
summaries.00523D8A: Sends all network state messages for a player. Sends 12 different message types (0x17-0x1E, 0x21-0x24) with param_1 as the target.
summaries.00523F02: Shows a dialog for choosing an additional player to join a multiplayer game. Similar to mp_choose_players_dialog but for adding one more player to an in-progress game. Shows available civs, handles name entry and gender selection.
summaries.005261A0: Initializes the Civilopedia topic list. Validates the selection index, copies the item list, sets up scrollbar, and refreshes the display.
summaries.0052630D: Renders the detail view for a Civilopedia entry. Shows the item's category icon, cost in production shields, prerequisite tech icon, and the descriptive text.
summaries.00526913: Handles selection of a Civilopedia item. Searches for the selected item ID in the list, updates the selection state, refreshes all display panels, and enters the detail message loop.
summaries.00526CA0: Massive 26KB function that constructs the entire diplomacy dialog (parley window) panel. Handles 21 different panel types (cases 0-20) based on param_1: civ selection buttons, treaty status selection lists, demand type lists, tribute type lists, war/peace proposals, technology exchanges, gold demand
summaries.0052D4D2: Handles treaty status list selection. Gets the dialog object, refreshes display, and stores the selected treaty type.
summaries.0052D523: Handles demand type selection in the diplomacy dialog. Updates the demand type and rebuilds the right panel.
summaries.0052D588: Generic selection handler for diplomacy dialog panels. Stores the selection value at a computed offset based on param_1.
summaries.0052D5DD: Handles tribute type selection. Maps the selected tribute type (0-4) to a specific negotiation panel type (7-11) and rebuilds the dialog panel.
summaries.0052D6FF: Handles war/peace selection in diplomacy. Maps selection (0=war, 1=peace) to panel type (12 or 13) and rebuilds.
summaries.0052D7DC: Handles checkbox toggles in the diplomacy proposal panel. Enforces mutual exclusivity rules between treaty options (e.g., selecting "war" disables "peace" options and vice versa).
summaries.0052D9A1: Validates that both negotiation panels have content, then advances the negotiation.
summaries.0052DA23: Rejects all proposals in the diplomacy dialog. Sends rejection network messages (0xA6 for proposals, 0xA8 for counter-proposals) and updates button states.
summaries.0052DB3D: Accepts all proposals in the diplomacy dialog. Sends acceptance network messages (0xA5, 0xA7).
summaries.0052DC7E: Shows the intelligence report for the target civ in the diplomacy dialog. Checks if the player has the prerequisite for intelligence (embassy or tech 0x18/9), then opens the intelligence dialog.
summaries.0052DD73: Advances the diplomacy negotiation state machine. Based on the current panel state, reads selections from the list boxes and navigates to the next appropriate panel. Key state transitions: - State 3 (main menu) -> 6 (status), 12/13 (war/peace), 7-11 (tribute types), 5 (treaty), 15 (full view) - Stat
summaries.0052E326: Navigates backwards in the diplomacy state machine. From states 5-15, returns to state 3 (main menu). From state 14 (combined), returns to state 5 (treaty).
summaries.0052E4C9: Handles acceptance of a diplomacy deal. Sends acceptance network message (0x83), shows confirmation text ("PARLEYACCEPT2"), resets negotiation to main menu (state 3).
summaries.0052E57C: Handles rejection of a diplomacy deal. Sends rejection network message (0x84), navigates to state 2 (counter-proposal), and rebuilds panels.
summaries.0052E685: Ends negotiations. Sends network message (0x85) and closes the diplomacy dialog.
summaries.0052E71A: Handles clicks on civ selection buttons in the diplomacy dialog. Reads the checkbox state and stores it in the corresponding DAT_0068af08 entry.
summaries.0052E7B7: Handles checkbox state changes in the diplomacy dialog. Manages the "select all" checkbox logic — when the master checkbox is toggled, propagates to all individual civ checkboxes.
summaries.0052E971: Updates the enabled/disabled states of all buttons in the diplomacy dialog based on the current negotiation state. Manages visibility of accept/reject/advance/back buttons.
summaries.0052EC47: Finds a unit by its alive flag value (param_1). Searches backwards through the unit array, then checks if the unit can reach a land tile. Returns the unit index, -1 if not found, or -2 if the unit is stranded (only sea tiles adjacent).
summaries.0052ED95: Finds a city by its alive flag value (param_1). Searches backwards through the city array. Returns the city index or -1 if not found.
summaries.00530E80: MFC/CRT library function returning the end-of-get-area pointer from a streambuf object (this+0x2c).
summaries.00530EB0: Sets the end-of-get-area pointer on a streambuf-like UI object (this+0x2c = param_1).
summaries.00530EE0: Creates a listbox window control. Sets up the control properties (font, position, size) and creates the actual Win32 window via create_window_C0F0.
summaries.00530FB0: Marks a listbox as needing redraw by setting this+0x34 = 1.
summaries.00530FE0: MFC/CRT library function returning the width field from an ios object (this+0x30).
summaries.00531010: Initializes a scrollbar control object. Calls base initialization (thunk_FUN_0040f480) and sets this+0x44 = 0.
summaries.005310A0: Creates a scrollbar window control with specified range, position, and size. Initializes multiple object members and creates the Win32 scrollbar child window.
summaries.00531210: Sets the active AI civ for processing. Validates param_1 is in [0,8], then sets the global active civ index and pointer to that civ's data record.
summaries.00531263: Converts (x,y) map coordinates to a linear byte offset in the map tile data array. Formula: DAT_006d116a * param_2 + param_1 + DAT_006365ec.
summaries.00531287: Returns the AI role code for a unit. Normally returns the role from the unit type table (DAT_0064b1ca at type*0x14). Special case: if role == 5 (settler) and unit has flag 0x200 set, returns 0x15 (engineer role).
summaries.005312E4: Finds the best direction for a settler/engineer to move to found a new city. Evaluates all 9 positions (8 neighbors + current) based on terrain suitability, enemy proximity, and resource potential. Returns direction index (0-8) or -1 if no good site.
summaries.00531567: Iterates through units stacked with param_1 and cancels goto orders for units of matching domain types (param_2 bitmask). If param_3 is 0, only the first match is cancelled.
summaries.00531607: Sets a goto order on a unit. Assigns order code 0x0b (goto), sets the reason code, and target coordinates.
summaries.00531653: Sets a goto order for a naval unit, finding a coastal tile adjacent to the target that shares a water connection with the unit's current position. Searches the 20-tile extended neighborhood of the target.
summaries.0053184D: The main AI turn processing function. Evaluates all units and cities for a civ, calculates per-continent military balance, assigns threat levels, manages settler/engineer movements, handles unit upgrades, and sets AI strategic goals. This is one of the core AI decision-making functions.
summaries.005351AA: AI turn logic for barbarian units (civ 0). Handles naval raiders, city capture/ransom, land unit movement/attack decisions. More aggressive and simpler than regular AI. Returns 0 normally, 1 if unit was destroyed/disbanded.
summaries.005369F3: When a city is threatened (param_1 = city index), alerts all AI naval units within movement range to redirect toward the city. Only affects units of non-human civs that are at peace with the city owner and have naval capability (role >= 5) and 2+ stacked units.
summaries.00536BC9: Calculates a weighted city score for a continent. Uses diminishing returns: first 5 cities worth 3 each, next 5 worth 2, next 5 worth 1. Returns total weight.
summaries.00536C4C: Finds the best target city for a nuclear missile. Evaluates all enemy cities based on population, defenses, military comparison, diplomatic state, and whether the attack would start a war. Moves the nuke to an adjacent empty tile and updates diplomatic state.
summaries.00537331: Handles AI movement decisions for naval units and ranged/bombardment units. Finds targets to attack (enemy units/cities), evaluates whether to bombard or launch missiles, and sets goto orders. Also handles transport logic (finding cities to pick up/drop off troops).
summaries.00538A29: The master AI unit turn function — the single largest function in the entire binary at 44,777 bytes. Handles ALL AI unit movement decisions for non-barbarian civs. This is the brain of the Civ2 AI for tactical unit control.
summaries.00543B80: Attempts to settle a unit (found city or auto-process) for the AI. Checks that the unit is valid, has settler-like orders, can settle at its location, and is not blocked by combat, then calls thunk_FUN_005b6787 to execute the settlement.
summaries.00543CD6: Main AI unit automation loop. Iterates all units belonging to the current AI civ, processes each unit's automated actions (settling, moving, etc.) in two passes: first for settler-type units, then for all others.
summaries.00548B70: Calculates spacing for drawing a row of icons evenly distributed across a width. Returns the computed spacing value.
summaries.00548C78: Draws a row of icons with computed spacing, using Bresenham-style error accumulation for sub-pixel positioning.
summaries.00548DF0: Sets keyboard focus to the events editor window and triggers a repaint.
summaries.00548E23: Writes the action portion of a scenario event to a file in text format. Handles all 14 action types: TEXT, MOVEUNIT, CREATEUNIT, CHANGEMONEY, PLAYWAVEFILE, MAKEAGGRESSION, JUSTONCE, PLAYCDTRACK, DONTPLAYWONDERS, CHANGETERRAIN, DESTROYACIVILIZATION, GIVETECHNOLOGY, HASTRIGGERED.
summaries.00549AEE: Writes all scenario events to a file in text format. Traverses the event linked list and writes each trigger with its associated actions.
summaries.0054A4C4: Saves scenario events to the EVENTS.txt file. Backs up existing file, copies non-event sections, writes new events between @BEGINEVENTS and @ENDEVENTS markers.
summaries.0054A874: Returns the bitmask of the Nth set bit in param_2. Used to map a listbox selection index to an action/trigger bit.
summaries.0054A8D3: Counts how many bits below param_1's position are set in param_2. Used to compute the listbox index from a bit flag.
summaries.0054A912: Shows a picker dialog to select a civ, unit type, tech, terrain type, or special category. Returns the selected index or -1 on cancel.
summaries.0054ADC6: Picks a civilization for an event action's receiver/target field. Uses different picker modes depending on whether the trigger type is RECEIVEDTECHNOLOGY (0x100) or other.
summaries.0054AE93: Updates the enabled/disabled state of event editor buttons based on the currently selected trigger and action. Enables/disables add, edit, delete, help buttons as appropriate.
summaries.0054B0ED: Save button handler for the events editor. Either saves events to file and refreshes, or shows a debug notice message.
summaries.0054B1D5: Shows help text for a specific trigger type in the events editor.
summaries.0054B2EC: Shows help text for a specific action type in the events editor.
summaries.0054B433: Handles the Help button press in the events editor. Determines whether trigger or action help is requested and shows the appropriate help dialog.
summaries.0054B5EB: Resets the events editor state, reloading event data from backup and invalidating the display.
summaries.0054B635: Populates the trigger listbox in the events editor with formatted descriptions of all event triggers.
summaries.0054BC1A: Populates the action listbox for the selected trigger in the events editor.
summaries.0054C36E: Deletes the currently selected event from the linked list after confirmation dialog.
summaries.0054C4A1: Edits an existing event trigger's parameters via a series of picker dialogs. Each trigger type has its own editing flow.
summaries.0054D4E6: "New Trigger" button handler. Presents the trigger type picker, creates a new event node, adds it to the linked list, then opens the trigger editor. If editing is cancelled, removes the new node.
summaries.0054D6DA: Deletes a selected action from an event trigger after confirmation.
summaries.0054D7EF: Edits the parameters of a specific event action. Each action type has its own complex editing flow with multiple picker dialogs.
summaries.0054EED0: "New Action" button handler. Presents the action type picker, adds the action bit to the selected trigger's action mask, then opens the action editor. If editing fails, removes the bit.
summaries.0054F16B: Paints the events editor window. Draws header text, event pool usage bar (used/total), and border decorations.
summaries.0054F3B9: Initializes and runs the full events editor dialog. Creates all UI elements (listboxes, buttons), loads event data, enters the main event loop, and cleans up on exit.
summaries.0054FFC8: Entry point for opening the events editor. Creates the window frame, initializes the editor, then cleans up.
summaries.00550017: Thunk that calls FUN_004183d0 (likely a destructor or cleanup).
summaries.0055002D: SEH epilog — restores FS:[0] from stack frame.
summaries.00551CD0: Destructor wrapper — calls a destructor then conditionally deletes the object via `operator_delete`.
summaries.00551D20: Library function — returns the end-of-get-area pointer from a streambuf (offset 0x2c).
summaries.00551D50: Sends UI message 0x3DBF using a window handle from the object at offset 0x1c.
summaries.00551D80: Sends UI message 0x3E92 with a parameter, using window handle from object offset 0x1c.
summaries.00551DC0: Setter — stores param_1 at object offset 0x30.
summaries.00551DF0: Setter — stores param_1 at object offset 0x34.
summaries.00551E20: Logs a normal dialog message using the global log string at DAT_006359d4.
summaries.00551E60: Logs a debug dialog message using the "DEBUG" label string at 0x006359dc.
summaries.00551EA0: Static initializer — allocates a global CString (16 bytes) and registers atexit destructor.
summaries.00551EBA: Allocates/constructs a global object of size 0x10 at index 0.
summaries.00551ED8: Registers FUN_00551ef5 as an atexit handler.
summaries.00551EF5: Atexit destructor — calls thunk_FUN_0043c520 (CString destructor wrapper).
summaries.00551F0F: Static initializer — allocates global object (size 10) and registers atexit destructor.
summaries.00551F29: Allocates global object of size 10 at index 0.
summaries.00551F64: Atexit destructor for global CString object.
summaries.00551F7E: Third static initializer — allocates global object (size 0x10) and registers atexit destructor.
summaries.00551FED: Initializes dialog metrics — calculates border widths, button spacing, and padding values based on screen resolution. Sets DAT_00633580 to 0x10 (normal) or 0x18 (large screen). Computes layout constants from system metrics.
summaries.005520FA: Sets the dialog background pattern/image to param_1.
summaries.00552112: Renders a complex dialog title bar with borders, buttons, timer display, and player/civ name. Handles multiplayer timer countdown formatting (mm:ss), draws shadow text and title text with background.
summaries.00552E5B: Handles a dialog button click — calls the callback function pointer stored in the dialog object at offset 0x2a8 with the button's associated data.
summaries.00552ED2: Creates and positions dialog buttons — destroys old buttons, recalculates inner content rectangle, then creates new button windows for each entry. Uses a button stride of 0x1c bytes per button.
summaries.0055318C: Adds a button to a dialog (max 6 buttons). Stores the button's callback label, text measurement values, and associated data at the appropriate slot.
summaries.0055324C: Sets the dialog title string (at offset 0x134, max 0x83 chars) then redraws the title bar and invalidates the rect.
summaries.005532D7: Destroys all 6 button window objects in the dialog by calling their destructor and zeroing the handle pointers.
summaries.00553379: Cleans up a dialog — destroys buttons then destroys the window.
summaries.0055339F: Constructor for dialog class — calls base class constructor, sets vtable, initializes 6 button handle slots to 0.
summaries.00553444: Library destructor for COleCntrFrameWnd — sets vtable, destroys buttons, destroys window, calls base destructor.
summaries.005534BC: Creates and initializes a dialog window with title, flags, position, and size. Configures borders, scrollbar, buttons, and callback handlers. Centers on screen if flag bit 1 is set.
summaries.00553D30: Sets message handler at offset 0x60, returns old handler.
summaries.00553D70: Sets message handler at offset 0x64, returns old handler.
summaries.00553DB0: Validates a folder name by uppercasing it and checking each character against a valid-character table at DAT_006335f8. Returns 0 if all chars valid, non-zero if an invalid char found.
summaries.00553DFD: Creates a new scenario folder. Prompts user for name, validates it, checks for duplicates, creates the directory and a sound subdirectory, then saves the scenario path.
summaries.00553FF6: Toggles cheat mode on/off. Shows confirmation dialog, optionally creates a scenario folder. Sets flag bits 0x8000 in DAT_00655aea and 0x10/0x80 in DAT_00655af0.
summaries.00554145: Shows a civilization picker dialog — lists all active civs (skipping barbarians if param_1==0). Returns selected civ index or -1 if cancelled.
summaries.00554297: Toggles cheat mode in multiplayer. Checks if any password-protected players exist; if so, shows warning and refuses. Otherwise toggles 0x8000 flag in DAT_00655aea, saves/restores turn timer values.
summaries.00554423: Cheat: reveals the map for a selected civilization. Picks a civ, then calls the map reveal function.
summaries.00554460: Toggles all technologies for a civ. If civ doesn't have all techs, grants all 100. If it does, removes them all. Updates tech bitmap and tech count.
summaries.005545D3: Cheat dialog for editing technologies of a civ. Shows list of all techs with indicators for known/available status. Allows granting or removing individual techs or toggling all.
summaries.00554962: Cheat: picks a civ then opens the government change dialog for it.
summaries.0055499F: Cheat terrain editor. Allows changing terrain type and improvement flags on the tile at cursor position. For ocean/land tiles, shows terrain type list; otherwise shows improvement checkbox dialog. Updates map visibility.
summaries.005551B3: Cheat: places a new unit at cursor position. Shows filterable unit type list (can toggle veteran, obsolete, and prerequisite-ignore modes). Creates unit for selected civ/type.
summaries.0055560F: Cheat: changes the active player/observer. Shows civ list plus "Observer" and "King of kings" options. Updates human player bitmask and active player index.
summaries.0055583F: Cheat: changes which civ is human-controlled. Lists active civs (1-7) plus "no human". On selection, updates human bitmask and active player.
summaries.00555A02: Cheat: sets the game year. Prompts for a new year value, updates turn counter and year display.
summaries.00555A8B: Cheat: destroys a civilization. Picks a civ, removes its bit from DAT_00655b0b, deletes all its cities, then calls thunk_kill_civ. Sends MP notification if applicable.
summaries.00555CB1: Cheat: opens unit editor for the top unit at cursor position.
summaries.00555CED: Maps an AI continent goal type to a sprite icon index. Returns different icon codes based on param_1 value (5->0x2a, 0x15->0x1d, 0->0x6a, 1->0x7a, 4->0x55, 2->0x5e, default 0x29).
summaries.00555D70: Cheat: displays AI continent goals on the map. Shows colored icons for each civ's 48 continent goals and 64 continent valuations in a visual overlay.
summaries.005560C9: Reveals a rectangular area of the map by iterating over the visible tile range and calling a reveal function for each tile.
summaries.0055615C: Cheat: sets a civ's treasury. Picks civ, shows current gold, prompts for new amount (clamped 0-30000), updates treasury.
summaries.0055625B: Cheat unit editor dialog. Allows editing veteran status, movement points, hit points, home city, fortification, and trade route cargo for a selected unit.
summaries.005569E3: Cheat city editor. Offers menu: set city size, clear wonders, clear disorder, copy improvements from another city, set shields, toggle coastal flag. All modify city data arrays.
summaries.00556F30: Dialog destructor helper and SEH epilog pair.
summaries.00556F54: Comprehensive cheat civ editor. 12+ options: edit treaties, attitudes, betrayal count, reset patience, reset all contacts, open city production dialog, edit research progress, cancel research, change research goal with names, copy techs from another civ, toggle space race.
summaries.00557E08: Dialog destructor helper and SEH epilog pair.
summaries.00557E2C: Cheat victory conditions editor. Shows total/player population scores, allows toggling victory flags, changing victory civ, editing 4 victory objective values.
summaries.00558177: Dialog destructor helper and SEH epilog pair.
summaries.0055819B: Cheat: toggles 4 scenario rule flags — flat earth, no unit restrictions, no tech trading, and accelerated start.
summaries.005582AD: Master scenario editor dialog with 12+ options: paradigm shift, year increment, start year, max turns, clear/set fog of war, setup city-state data, edit scenario name, toggle flags, edit victory conditions, edit rules.
summaries.0055891D: Saves the current game via thunk_save_game(1).
summaries.00559C20: CRT static initializer. Calls FUN_00559c3a to construct the video window object (DAT_006ab1b8) and FUN_00559c54 to register its atexit destructor.
summaries.00559C3A: Constructs the video window object by calling thunk_FUN_00559e3c (CMiniFrameWnd-style constructor) on the static object at DAT_006ab1b8.
summaries.00559C54: Registers FUN_00559c71 as an atexit handler to destroy the video window object on program exit.
summaries.00559C71: Atexit handler that destroys the video window object by calling CMiniFrameWnd::~CMiniFrameWnd on DAT_006ab1b8.
summaries.00559C8B: CRT static initializer. Calls FUN_00559ca5 to construct a bitmap surface object and FUN_00559cbf to register its atexit destructor.
summaries.00559CA5: Constructs a bitmap surface object by calling FUN_005bd630 (bitmap constructor) on a static object.
summaries.00559CBF: Registers FUN_00559cdc as an atexit handler to destroy the bitmap surface object on program exit.
summaries.00559CDC: Atexit handler that destroys the bitmap surface object by calling FUN_005bd915 (bitmap destructor).
summaries.00559CF6: Creates a video playback surface — initializes a bitmap surface from resource, blits initial frame.
summaries.00559DBD: Cleanup helpers for video surface (surface destructor, bitmap cleanup, SEH epilog).
summaries.00559DED: Initializes video playback parameters: width=100, height=100, step=1, frame=0.
summaries.00559E3C: Constructor for video window — calls dialog ctor, initializes video surface, sets vtable to PTR_FUN_0061d6e0.
summaries.00559F60: Blits a video frame rectangle from source to destination surface.
summaries.00559FCF: Prepares rendering context and blits video frame.
summaries.0055A00B: Sets up a rectangle at an offset from the video window origin.
summaries.0055A051: Renders one video frame — creates buttons, creates surface, sets up rect, renders title bar, blits frame, finalizes.
summaries.0055A10D: Creates a rect with padding from system dialog frame metrics.
summaries.0055A192: Calculates window position from a position-mode enum (0-9). Handles left, center, right for X; top, center, bottom for Y. Uses screen dimensions (DAT_006ab198 = 640/1024, DAT_006ab19c = 480/768).
summaries.0055A329: Returns width or height for popup windows based on a dialog type enum (1-13). Returns different values for param_1==0 (width) vs param_1!=0 (height).
summaries.0055A41D: Shows a popup window — computes dimensions and position, creates dialog, sets handlers, enables scrolling.
summaries.0055A567: Conditional cleanup functions for popup windows — destroy window if video loaded.
summaries.0055A5E4: Loads the civ2_intro.dll library for intro video playback.
summaries.0055A64A: Unloads the intro DLL if loaded.
summaries.0055A930: Wrapper for thunk_FUN_005a9afe — blits full source rect to dest.
summaries.0055A980: Builds the trade route connectivity map. For each map quadrant pair, checks if trade routes exist between adjacent quadrants and marks connectivity bits in a bitmap. Uses two passes (supply/demand).
summaries.0055AC37: Finds a valid trade tile near (param_1, param_2) by checking offsets 0 and 1. Returns the tile's continent ID or -1 if no valid tile found.
summaries.0055ADD0: Application entry point — creates "Civilization II Once Only" mutex to prevent multiple instances. If already running, brings existing window to front. Otherwise initializes the application.
summaries.0055AE80: Stops the turn timer — kills timer, updates minimap overlay, sends MP notification if applicable.
summaries.0055AF2E: Starts the turn timer — closes open windows, calculates remaining time, creates a 500ms repeating timer, sends MP notification.
summaries.0055B046: Resumes the turn timer if time remains and game is active.
summaries.0055B0FB: Called every 500ms while turn timer is active. Decrements remaining time, updates timer display with color-coded icon (green/yellow/red). When time runs out, forces end of turn.
summaries.0055B2C6: End-turn prompt handler. Stops timer, checks if user wants to end turn (via thunk_FUN_0051ea8e). If yes and no timer, sets DAT_0066c988=0; if timer, sets it to 1 and starts timer. Sends MP notification.
summaries.0055B3C8: Kills the drag timer if active.
summaries.0055B3FD: Restarts the drag timeout timer (500ms) for multiplayer (DAT_00655b02 > 2).
summaries.0055B451: If drag state active, calls check_drag_timeout_mp.
summaries.0055B47E: Enters window drag mode — logs event, sets drag state, records timestamp, installs Windows hook for drag tracking. Sends MP notification if multiplayer.
summaries.0055B515: Exits window drag mode — unhooks Windows hook, clears drag state, sends MP notification.
summaries.0055B59E: Cancels an active drag by posting WM_LBUTTONUP to the drag window.
summaries.0055B5FA: Checks if drag has timed out (620ms) in multiplayer. If so, cancels the drag. Always restarts the drag timer.
summaries.0055B677: If drag active for > 124ms, invalidates the main window for repaint.
summaries.0055B6C7: Handles player resignation. In single player, ends the game. In multiplayer, shows confirmation dialog, removes civ from active bitmask, sends resign notifications, potentially eliminates the civ.
summaries.0055BBC0: Calculates war readiness score for a civ pair. Counts enemy military units near the border, assigns threat levels based on tile improvements (roads, forts, rivers) and unit counts. Used by AI diplomacy.
summaries.0055BEF9: Checks if a civ can declare war. Returns 0 if government too low (< Democracy), if scenario prevents it, if random betrayal check fails, or if no Warrior Code/Statue of Liberty. Returns 1 if war is possible.
summaries.0055C066: Sets a civ's government type. If changing to/from anarchy, clears embassy flags. Refreshes all cities of the civ. If cities were building Capitalization and new govt isn't Communism, switches to generic production.
summaries.0055C277: Checks if a specific government type is available for a civ. Checks prerequisite techs (Monarchy->tech 0x36, Republic->0xf, Democracy->0x1f, Communism->0x47, Fundamentalism->0x15) and Statue of Liberty wonder.
summaries.0055C3D3: Revolution/government change dialog. Shows available government types, lets player choose. Applies the change via set_government_type. Shows "NEWGOVT" message. Handles tutorial for democracy.
summaries.0055C69D: Handles AI revolution/government change notifications. If human civ, opens revolution dialog. If AI civ, shows "overthrown"/"changed government" messages to human players who have embassy/knowledge. Updates civ status flags and AI attitude values.
summaries.0055CBD5: AI war declaration decision function. Evaluates whether AI civ param_1 should declare war on civ param_2. Considers alliances, military power ratios, betrayal history, trade dependencies, personality type, and civ ranking. Returns 1 for war, 0 for peace.
summaries.0055D1E2: Handles AI technology exchange between two civs during diplomacy. Finds the best tech each side can offer, and if both have something to trade, executes a swap. Also handles one-sided tech gifts when allied.
summaries.0055D685: Checks if a 3rd party (param_2) should join a war between param_1 and param_3. Evaluates existing treaties, recent contact, personality. If conditions met, declares war and shows notification.
summaries.0055D8D8: Master diplomatic contact processing function. Handles all phases of civ-to-civ contact: initial meeting, treaty negotiations, war declarations, peace treaties, alliance formation, and multiplayer parley protocol. The largest and most complex function in this block.
summaries.0055F5A3: AI government selection logic. Evaluates available governments using preference scores (DAT_0064ca74 array) and selects the best available. Handles scenario restrictions and tech-level limits.
summaries.0055F7D1: AI military aid — transfers military units from one civ to an allied civ's enemy. Finds the best military unit to transfer, moves it to an allied city under threat, changes ownership. Shows notifications.
summaries.00560084: AI turn-start diplomacy processing. For each AI civ: handles revolution timing, randomizes AI behavior, processes AI-AI and AI-human diplomatic interactions (war expiration, peace, alliances, tech exchange). The main per-turn AI diplomacy driver.
summaries.00560D95: Core AI diplomacy evaluation function. Calculates an "attitude score" for AI civ param_1 toward human civ param_2 based on 15+ factors including military units near borders, power rankings, tech level, personality, wonders, alliances, and trade. Handles treaty violations (withdrawing units or declar
summaries.00562021: AI proposes alliances or crusades against a common enemy. Checks if conditions are right (shared enemy, power disparity), offers gold for military alliance or proposes a crusade/jihad. On acceptance, transfers gold, declares joint war, and adjusts treaties.
summaries.00564470: Finds the CD-ROM path for game data. Gets the executable directory, then scans drives for the CD-ROM twice, prompting user if not found.
summaries.00564549: Returns true if CD-ROM path is empty (DAT_006ab680 == '\0').
summaries.00564574: Returns pointer to CD-ROM path (DAT_006ab680) or NULL if empty.
summaries.0056459F: Scans all logical drives for a CD-ROM (type 3 = DRIVE_CDROM but the code checks for 3 which is DRIVE_FIXED — likely a Ghidra mistype, or the game checks fixed drives). Opens a test file to verify.
summaries.00564713: Resolves a file path by trying multiple locations: exe directory, relative paths, and CD-ROM path. Modifies param_1 in-place with the resolved path. Returns 1 if found, 0 if not.
summaries.00564BF0: Parses a number literal string with prefix detection: 0x = hex, 0b = binary, 0d = decimal, otherwise decimal. Returns the parsed integer.
summaries.00564D00: Initializes the Civilopedia entry list. Copies item IDs from the main array to the display array, sets scrollbar range and position, initializes sub-panels.
summaries.00564E6D: Draws the Civilopedia tech detail page. Shows tech name, prerequisites with clickable links, enabled buildings/wonders/units, and descriptive text from pedia files. Handles custom scenario pedia files.
summaries.00566584: Selects and displays a Civilopedia entry. Finds the entry in the list, updates display state, draws the detail page, and triggers a UI refresh.
summaries.005666DA: Draws the Civilopedia tech tree diagram. Shows the selected tech at center, its 2 prerequisites, their 4 prerequisites, and the 8 great-grandparent techs. Draws connecting lines between levels using draw_tree_connector_line and draw_tree_horizontal_line.
summaries.005674B9: Draws a vertical connector line with horizontal arm in the tech tree. Draws shadow (color 10), then foreground (color 0x7a). Handles direction based on child position.
summaries.005675B7: Draws a horizontal line from a node to the left edge of the tech tree panel. Shadow + foreground.
summaries.005680A0: CRT static initializer that calls palette_init (via FUN_005680ba → FUN_005c64da) and registers palette_destroy as an atexit handler (via FUN_005680d4).
summaries.0056810B: CRT static initializer that calls port_constructor (via FUN_00568125 → FUN_005bd630) and registers port_destructor as an atexit handler (via FUN_0056813f).
summaries.00568176: Returns 1 (always true). Likely a capability check stub.
summaries.005681C9: Loads the civ2art.dll art resource. Resolves path, loads GIF at resource 999, sets up drawing surface. Falls back to a 0x40x0x20 dummy surface on failure.
summaries.00568381: Post-art-load initialization — finalizes window, initializes palette, calls noop.
summaries.005683C5: Animates a screen reveal effect. Divides screen into grid squares and reveals them in a pattern (top-to-bottom, bottom-to-top, left-to-right, right-to-left, or random). Uses GlobalAlloc for the reveal order buffer.
summaries.00568861: Returns the era level for a civ: 2 if has Navigation+Astronomy (Modern), 1 if has Railroad+Industrialization (Industrial), 0 otherwise (Ancient).
summaries.00568B00: CRT static initializer that calls dialog_ctor (via FUN_00568b1a → thunk_FUN_0055339f) for DAT_006abc68, and registers its destructor as an atexit handler.
summaries.00568C41: Sets status window lock flag (DAT_00633e00 = 1) to prevent redraws.
summaries.00568C58: Clears status window lock and triggers full redraw.
summaries.00568C7E: Sets lock flag and calls UpdateWindow.
summaries.00568CA2: Calculates the status panel layout based on screen dimensions. Determines zoom level (2/3/4), font size, and panel heights. Adjusts panel widths for different resolutions.
summaries.00568E86: Draws an advisor icon in the status panel (if unlocked and panel visible).
summaries.00568F43: Draws the turn number and year info section of the status panel. Shows active/inactive turn indicator, turn label, and year text.
summaries.0056911D: Draws coordinate text (x,y and continent ID) at a given position. Used in the status panel.
summaries.005691A1: Formats the unit orders text for the status panel display. Shows order name (fortify, goto, sentry, etc.) and for goto orders, shows the destination city name or coordinates.
summaries.00569363: Draws the status panel header section: civ name, year, treasury, tax/science/luxury rates with graphical bars and research progress indicator.
summaries.00569801: Draws the complete status panel unit section. Shows active unit with sprite, movement points, name, orders, and nationality. Below that, shows terrain info (type, improvements, pollution, special resources). Lists other units on the same tile. Handles scroll for overflow.
summaries.0056A65E: Refreshes the status panel — clears background, recalculates layout, redraws header and units, invalidates screen rects.
summaries.0056A787: Full redraw of the entire status window — creates buttons, recalculates layout, clears surfaces, draws status panels, draws borders, finalizes display.
summaries.0056A98B: Handles the end-turn button click. If in city screen, closes it. If units awaiting orders, processes them. If turn complete, ends the turn.
summaries.0056A9F4: Calculates the main game window rectangle from the status bar position.
summaries.0056AA7F: Both invalidate the main game window rect for repaint.
summaries.0056AACB: Sets up the main game window — creates dialog, configures event handlers (keyboard, mouse, scroll, resize), sets keyboard mapping, enables scrolling.
summaries.0056AC46: Closes the main game window — locks status and destroys window.
summaries.0056AC67: Draws the minimap timer/turn indicator overlay. Lazy-initializes the surface on first call. Caches the previous state to avoid unnecessary redraws. Shows a color-coded progress bar and optional icon.
summaries.0056AEED: Atexit handler that destroys the minimap overlay bitmap surface.
summaries.0056B810: Strips the last newline (0x0a) from a string by replacing it with null.
summaries.0056B847: Appends a newline character to a string.
summaries.0056B8A0: CRT static initializer that constructs a CString object (via FUN_0056b8ba → thunk_FUN_0043c690) and registers its destructor as an atexit handler.
summaries.0056B90B: Sets the unit display font size based on zoom level. Calculates size as sprite_height/3, adjusts 10->11 to avoid bad glyph. Caches current size.
summaries.0056B96E: Selects which unit to display from a tile's unit stack. Prioritizes: active unit (weight 4) > settlers (weight 2) > normal military (weight 1) > units building cities (weight -1). Returns best unit index.
summaries.0056BAFF: Draws a complete unit sprite at the given coordinates. Renders the unit icon, health bar (green/yellow/red), color-coded background, orders character, and fortification/goto destination indicators. Handles sentry/sleeping display and veteran markers.
summaries.0056C5FC: Blits a source rect to dest rect with manual clipping. Adjusts source/dest offsets for negative coordinates, clips to bounds, then calls thunk_FUN_005a9afe.
summaries.0056C705: Animates unit movement between tiles. Creates per-viewport off-screen buffers, captures background, then steps the unit sprite across frames with timing control. Handles multi-viewport display and sound effects.
summaries.0056D289: Draws a complete city sprite on the map. Shows the city graphic (based on era, size, walls, fortification), city-state owner color, and population number with formatting. Handles all display variants (coastal, walled, capital, different eras).
summaries.0056E180: Calculates animation step size for unit movement based on current zoom level.
summaries.0056E1C0: Returns (param_2 + 8) * param_1 / 8 + rounding. Used for pixel-per-frame animation stepping.
summaries.0056E1F0: Returns the dark color for a civ based on its government type and race. Civ 0 (barbarians) uses index 0.
summaries.0056E270: Initializes a player popup state object: sets field 4=1 (active), field 0x28=0 (count), zeros 7 slots (fields 0x54-0x6c).
summaries.0056E2E9: Creates and initializes a multiplayer game session. Handles the full lifecycle: creates player popup, manages player list, processes lobby connections, initializes new game or loads saved game, configures DirectPlay sessions, handles host/join flow.
summaries.0056EC92: Checks if all joined MP players have selected their civs. If so, transitions game state to "ready" (DAT_006ad640=2) and invalidates the window.
summaries.0056ED80: Checks for incoming MP chat messages and invalidates window if any pending.
summaries.0056EDD3: Returns a linked player node, preferring prev-linked node, then next-linked node. Used for linked list traversal of player list.
summaries.0056EE2C: Frees all nodes in the player linked list. Zeroes count and head/tail pointers.
summaries.0056EED7: Removes a specific node from the player linked list, re-links neighbors, decrements count.
summaries.0056EF93: Adds a new player node to the tail of the player linked list. Allocates node (24 bytes) and name buffer, stores ID and name, updates max width.
summaries.0056F113: Rebuilds the player list display from current MP session state. Clears old list, adds entries for each active slot, selects the previously-selected entry.
summaries.0056F301: Adds a default "empty" player entry to the list if the list is empty.
summaries.0056F372: Checks if the default/empty entry matches the current format string. Returns the matching node or 0.
summaries.0056F3E0: Traverses the player linked list to find a node with matching ID (field 4).
summaries.0056F42B: Finds the slot index (0-6) for a given player ID by searching DAT_006ad354 (stride 0x54).
summaries.0056F910: CRT static initializer that calls port_constructor (via FUN_0056f92a → FUN_005bd630) and registers port_destructor as an atexit handler (via FUN_0056f944).
summaries.0056FBFD: Scans a unit sprite region for blue marker pixels (color index 8 and 4) at the sprite's anchor point offsets. Used to detect recolor anchor points in the sprite sheet.
summaries.0056FCE4: Recolors the UNITS spritesheet for the current civ colors. Loads UNITS.BMP/GIF, iterates all 62 unit types (9x7 grid), applies civ-specific palette recoloring to each sprite, then saves back as UNITS.BMP.
summaries.0056FFDA: Recolors the CITIES spritesheet for current civ colors. Loads CITIES.BMP/GIF, recolors city sprites at 6 rows x 4 columns (x2 for walled variants), plus 8 flag sprites, plus 4 special sprites. Saves back as CITIES.BMP.
summaries.005680BA: Thunk that calls FUN_005c64da (palette_init) to initialize a GDI palette surface at static init time.
summaries.005680D4: Registers FUN_005680f1 (palette_destroy thunk) as an atexit handler so the palette surface is cleaned up on program exit.
summaries.005680F1: Atexit handler that calls FUN_005c656b (palette_destroy) to clean up the GDI palette surface.
summaries.00568125: Thunk that calls FUN_005bd630 (port_constructor) to initialize a bitmap port object at static init time.
summaries.0056813F: Registers FUN_0056815c (port_destructor thunk) as an atexit handler so the bitmap port is cleaned up on exit.
summaries.0056815C: Atexit handler that calls FUN_005bd915 (port_destructor) to clean up the bitmap port object.
summaries.00568B1A: Thunk that calls thunk_FUN_0055339f (dialog_ctor) to construct a dialog object at DAT_006abc68.
summaries.00568B34: Registers FUN_00568b51 (dialog destructor thunk) as an atexit handler.
summaries.00568B51: Atexit handler that destroys the dialog object at DAT_006abc68 via COleCntrFrameWnd::~COleCntrFrameWnd.
summaries.00568B6B: CRT static initializer that constructs a CString object (via FUN_00568b85 → thunk_FUN_0043c690) and registers its destructor as an atexit handler.
summaries.00568B85: Thunk that calls thunk_FUN_0043c690 to construct a CString object at static init time.
summaries.00568B9F: Registers FUN_00568bbc (font_destroy thunk) as an atexit handler for a CString object.
summaries.00568BBC: Atexit handler that calls thunk_FUN_0043c520 (font_destroy) to clean up a CString/font object.
summaries.00568BD6: CRT static initializer that constructs a second CString object (via FUN_00568bf0 → thunk_FUN_0043c690) and registers its destructor as an atexit handler.
summaries.00568BF0: Thunk that calls thunk_FUN_0043c690 to construct a second CString object at static init time.
summaries.00568C0A: Registers FUN_00568c27 (font_destroy thunk) as an atexit handler for the second CString object.
summaries.00568C27: Atexit handler that calls thunk_FUN_0043c520 (font_destroy) to clean up the second CString/font object.
summaries.0056B8BA: Thunk that calls thunk_FUN_0043c690 to construct a CString object at static init time.
summaries.0056B8D4: Registers FUN_0056b8f1 (font_destroy thunk) as an atexit handler for a CString object.
summaries.0056B8F1: Atexit handler that calls thunk_FUN_0043c520 (font_destroy) to clean up a CString/font object.
summaries.0056F92A: Thunk that calls FUN_005bd630 (port_constructor) to initialize a bitmap port object at static init time.
summaries.0056F944: Registers FUN_0056f961 (port_destructor thunk) as an atexit handler so the bitmap port is cleaned up on exit.
summaries.0056F961: Atexit handler that calls FUN_005bd915 (port_destructor) to clean up a bitmap port object.
summaries.0056F97B: CRT static initializer that constructs CString object at DAT_006ac170 and registers sprite_free_data as its atexit destructor.
summaries.0056F995: Constructs a CString object at global address DAT_006ac170.
summaries.0056F9AF: Registers FUN_0056f9cc (sprite_free_data thunk) as an atexit handler for the CString at DAT_006ac170.
summaries.0056F9CC: Atexit handler that calls FUN_005cde4d (sprite_free_data) to free sprite data associated with the CString at DAT_006ac170.
summaries.0056F9E6: CRT static initializer that constructs CString object at DAT_006ac8a8 and registers sprite_free_data as its atexit destructor.
summaries.0056FA00: Constructs a CString object at global address DAT_006ac8a8.
summaries.0056FA1A: Registers FUN_0056fa37 (sprite_free_data thunk) as an atexit handler for the CString at DAT_006ac8a8.
summaries.0056FA37: Atexit handler that calls FUN_005cde4d (sprite_free_data) to free sprite data associated with the CString at DAT_006ac8a8.
summaries.0056FA51: CRT static initializer that constructs CString object at DAT_006ac8e8 and registers sprite_free_data as its atexit destructor.
summaries.0056FA6B: Constructs a CString object at global address DAT_006ac8e8.
summaries.0056FA85: Registers FUN_0056faa2 (sprite_free_data thunk) as an atexit handler for the CString at DAT_006ac8e8.
summaries.0056FAA2: Atexit handler that calls FUN_005cde4d (sprite_free_data) to free sprite data associated with the CString at DAT_006ac8e8.
summaries.0056FABC: CRT static initializer that calls port_constructor (via FUN_0056fad6 → FUN_005bd630) and registers port_destructor as an atexit handler (via FUN_0056faf0).
summaries.0056FAD6: Thunk that calls FUN_005bd630 (port_constructor) to initialize a bitmap port object at static init time.
summaries.0056FAF0: Registers FUN_0056fb0d (port_destructor thunk) as an atexit handler so the bitmap port is cleaned up on exit.
summaries.0056FB0D: Atexit handler that calls FUN_005bd915 (port_destructor) to clean up a bitmap port object.
summaries.0056FB27: CRT static initializer that calls dialog_ctor (via FUN_0056fb41 → thunk_FUN_0055339f) for DAT_006ac1b0, and registers its destructor as an atexit handler.
summaries.0056FB41: Thunk that calls thunk_FUN_0055339f (dialog_ctor) to construct a dialog object at DAT_006ac1b0.
summaries.0056FB5B: Registers FUN_0056fb78 (dialog destructor thunk) as an atexit handler for the dialog at DAT_006ac1b0.
summaries.0056FB78: Atexit handler that destroys the dialog object at DAT_006ac1b0 via COleCntrFrameWnd::~COleCntrFrameWnd.
summaries.0056FB92: CRT static initializer that calls init_menu_control (via FUN_0056fbac → thunk_FUN_005784a0) for DAT_006ac488, and registers CBitmapButton::~CBitmapButton as an atexit handler.
summaries.0056FBAC: Thunk that calls thunk_FUN_005784a0 (init_menu_control) to construct a bitmap button/menu control at DAT_006ac488.
summaries.0056FBC6: Registers FUN_0056fbe3 (CBitmapButton destructor) as an atexit handler for the menu control at DAT_006ac488.
summaries.0056FBE3: Atexit handler that destroys the bitmap button at DAT_006ac488 via CBitmapButton::~CBitmapButton.
summaries.0057075C: SEH cleanup thunk — calls FUN_005c656b (CString destructor or stack cleanup).
summaries.00570772: SEH unwind handler — restores FS:[0] exception chain from saved frame pointer.
summaries.00570780: Loads TERRAIN1.BMP/GIF, processes 11 base terrain sprite rows plus 3 additional rows and 9 more sprite pairs, extracts and processes color-keyed sprites, then saves the modified bitmap back. Part of the sprite editor tool.
summaries.00570FC6: Loads TERRAIN2.BMP/GIF and processes 16 terrain overlay sprites in a grid (8 cols x 2 rows), plus 4 additional sprites at y=0x14B, plus 8 groups of 4 sprites for river/road overlays. Writes modified bitmap back.
summaries.00571910: Loads ICONS.BMP/GIF and processes a 5x4 grid of icon sprites starting at position (0x157, 0xD3), with cell size 0x25 x 0x15. Writes modified bitmap back.
summaries.00571BCE: Processes a second set of icon sprites from ICONS.BMP/GIF: 38 icons in an 8-col grid (0x25 width, 0x15 height), 28 more icons in a 7-col grid, 1 icon at (199, 0x100), and 8 icons in a row at y=0x164.
summaries.00572089: Opens the sprite editor dialog window. Sets up dialog title, position, size, scrollbars and style flags based on param_2 bitflags. Creates the window via FUN_005bb4ae and initializes popup UI state.
summaries.005722AB: Refreshes the sprite editor display: sets up a clip rect, copies bitmap to display, saves cursor position to per-view array, calls callback if set, invalidates cache, and repaints.
summaries.00572364: Sets DAT_006ac88c to 0 and invalidates the sprite editor's object cache to force a redraw.
summaries.00572389: Captures the current sprite editor viewport by selecting the drawing surface, creating a full-size rect, copying it to the cache, then saving the current cursor position to the "previous" cursor position globals.
summaries.005723EE: Draws 3-4 color swatches in the sprite editor toolbar showing the currently selected foreground/background/tertiary/quaternary colors. Each swatch is a 32x32 or 16x16 filled rectangle with a border.
summaries.0057261A: Draws a single palette color entry in the color picker grid. Computes grid position from palette index, fills a 10x10 rect with the color, then draws a selection indicator (larger border) if the color matches either DAT_00634000 or DAT_00634004.
summaries.00572740: Hit-tests a click position against the sprite editor toolbar buttons. Returns button index 0-14 or -1 if outside all buttons. Toolbar is a 3x5 grid starting at (0x11E, 0x14) with cell size 0x24 x 0x26.
summaries.005727D8: Initializes the sprite editor view mode based on DAT_006ac924 (sprite sheet type). Fills background with DAT_006ac874 color and optionally copies initial sprite data depending on mode (1/2/0xB vs 5/7/8/10).
summaries.00572887: Paints a pixel or brush stroke at the given (x,y) position in the sprite editor. Supports 3 brush modes (DAT_006ac124): mode 0 = single pixel (4x4), mode 1 = cross pattern (3-wide, 12x12), mode 2 = block pattern (4-wide, 16x16). Uses selected color from DAT_006ac894 or fallback DAT_006ac874.
summaries.00572DA0: Draws the cursor crosshair overlay in the sprite editor viewport. Draws vertical and horizontal guide lines through the current cursor position using color 0x6A, with a smaller inner crosshair at the cursor using color 0x29.
summaries.00572FFF: Main mouse click handler for the sprite editor. Dispatches to 15 different toolbar actions (swap colors, undo, change brush mode, open file dialog, etc.) based on hit-test result, and handles pixel painting in the canvas area and color picking in the palette grid.
summaries.00573AFD: Releases mouse capture and clears the drag flag for the sprite editor.
summaries.00573B1D: Handles mouse move events in the sprite editor. Updates cursor style based on region (palette=0x205, canvas=0x204/0x205, outside=0x201), and if dragging (DAT_006ac8a4 != 0), performs line-drawing from previous position using Bresenham-like interpolation in mode 1, or single-pixel painting in mode 2.
summaries.00573E59: Main entry point for opening the sprite editor. Initializes sprite dimensions, palette, viewport, creates the editor dialog with OK/Cancel buttons, enters a message loop until DAT_006ac88c becomes 0, then cleans up.
summaries.00574239: Full redraw of the sprite editor: fills background, copies bitmap to canvas at 4x zoom, draws all 192 palette entries (indices 10-201), draws the 3x5 toolbar button grid with tool icons, draws color swatches, and sets up the scrollbar.
summaries.00574522: Draws a centered sprite preview in the preview dialog. Computes center position based on dialog dimensions, draws the sprite at 2x zoom with grid lines.
summaries.005746A1: Computes the source coordinates and dimensions of a specific sprite within a sprite sheet, based on the sprite sheet type (DAT_006ac924, 0-11) and sprite index. Returns the total sprite count for the sheet type. Handles all 12 sprite sheet layouts (icons, cities, terrain, units, etc.).
summaries.00574C47: Edits a single sprite by index: gets its coords from the sprite sheet, selects the drawing surface, extracts the sprite region from DAT_006ac0a8 via FUN_005cedad, and redraws the preview.
summaries.00574CA6: Opens the sprite preview dialog. Nearly identical structure to sprited_open_dialog (FUN_00572089) but uses a different set of dialog state globals (0x006ac5xx range) and references DAT_006ac1f8 as parent.
summaries.00574E98: Initializes the sprite preview: sets up 9x7 sprite grid, initializes view mode, copies bitmap to cache, sets DAT_006ac890 = 0, and invalidates the preview cache.
summaries.00574F50: Applies sprite editor changes back to the source sprite sheet. Shows a confirmation dialog ("DEBUG"/"RUSURE"), then iterates through all sprites in the current sheet type, writing each modified sprite back to its source position in the appropriate sprite data arrays (DAT_00646cb8, DAT_0063fe50, etc.
summaries.00575D89: Writes a sprite from the editor bitmap (DAT_006ac0a8) back to a destination buffer. Extracts sprite at given coords with mode 7, then resets the sprite grid to 9x7.
summaries.00575DC4: Closes the sprite preview by setting DAT_006ac890 = 0 and invalidating the preview object cache.
summaries.00575DEC: Sets up the sprite preview window with a listbox of sprites, OK/Cancel/Apply buttons, and enters a modal loop. The listbox allows selecting individual sprites for editing. When DAT_006ac890 becomes 0 (via close or apply), the loop exits.
summaries.00576267: Opens a file-open dialog for the sprite editor, allowing the user to import a BMP or GIF file. Validates the filename matches the expected sprite sheet name, checks for odd/even terrain numbering, loads the file, then refreshes the preview.
summaries.00578390: MFC CBitmapButton destructor. Destroys 4 bitmap resources (up/down/focus/disabled states), the underlying button control, and unwinds the exception handler.
summaries.00578402: Destructor helper for CBitmapButton — destroys one bitmap.
summaries.0057843E: Destructor helper — destroys OLE container frame.
summaries.005784A0: Constructor for the menu control object. Calls parent constructors, creates 3 child objects, and sets the vtable pointer to PTR_FUN_0061d6fc.
summaries.005785D0: Standard C++ ios_base::precision setter. Reads old precision from this+0x10, writes new value, returns old.
summaries.00578610: Standard C++ ios_base::width setter. Reads old width from this+0x14, writes new value, returns old.
summaries.00578650: Resets the menu control's list and reinitializes it with param_1 items. Calls thunk_FUN_004980ec to clear, then thunk_FUN_00497ea0 to set 0xB slots with param_1 items.
summaries.0057868C: Resets HTML stream state — zeroes this+0x1C and this+0x18.
summaries.005786B6: Fully resets the menu control: clears list via thunk_FUN_0049805e, resets HTML stream, then reinitializes with param_1 items.
summaries.005786F1: Creates a new menu control. Allocates memory, initializes base object, and calls menu_ctrl_reset_and_init with param_1 items.
summaries.00578770: Destroys a menu control by clearing its internal list and cleaning up memory.
summaries.005787DE: Searches the linked list of menu items (starting at this+0x1C) for one whose ID (at node+4) matches param_1. Returns node pointer or 0 if not found.
summaries.00578840: Returns the 1-based visible index of a menu item node within the linked list, skipping hidden items (those with bit 1 set at node+8).
summaries.005788A9: Returns the menu item ID at the given 1-based visible index, skipping hidden items. Returns -1 if index is out of range.
summaries.00578922: Searches all top-level menu items and their sub-item lists for a sub-item with matching ID (param_1). Returns the sub-item node pointer.
summaries.005789AA: Returns the 1-based visible index of a sub-item within its parent menu item, skipping hidden entries.
summaries.00578A1C: Returns the sub-item ID at the given visible index within a specific top-level menu item. Returns -1 if not found.
summaries.00578ABD: Replaces all '|' characters (0x7C) with '\t' (tab) characters in the given string. Used for menu text formatting.
summaries.00578B06: Adds a new top-level menu item to the linked list. Allocates a 0x1C-byte node, sets ID, label text (with pipe-to-tab conversion), and appends to the end of the list. Clears the "populated" flag.
summaries.00578C12: Adds a sub-item under a top-level menu item (found by ID). Allocates 0x1C-byte node, sets sub-item ID, label, and appends to the parent's sub-item list. Allocates text buffer with max of strlen or param_4 size.
summaries.00578D8A: Dispatches a menu item click by resolving the visible indices to item IDs and calling the callback function at DAT_0063430c.
summaries.00578DE8: Sets the host window for the menu control. Stores the window handle and this pointer, calls menu_setup_parent, and enables stacked tabs.
summaries.00578E38: Sets the menu callback function pointer and the associated control reference.
summaries.00578E60: Toggles the checked state of a menu sub-item. Finds the sub-item by ID, inverts bit 0 of its flags, gets its visible indices, and calls menu_check_item to update the native menu.
summaries.00578EC7: Toggles the grayed/disabled state of a menu sub-item. Similar to toggle_item_checked but operates on bit 2 of the flags and calls menu_enable_item.
summaries.00578F2C: Populates the native menu from the internal linked-list representation. If param_1 is 0, clears all existing items first. For each visible top-level item, creates the header and populates its sub-items. Applies checked/grayed states and sets the host window.
summaries.005791DF: Shows or hides a top-level menu item by setting/clearing bit 1 in its flags. Clears the "populated" flag when visibility changes.
summaries.00579260: Shows or hides a sub-menu item by setting/clearing bit 1 in its flags.
