summaries.005792E1: Sets or clears the checked state of a sub-menu item (bit 0). If the menu is populated and the item is visible, also toggles the native menu check state.
summaries.005793A3: Removes a sub-menu item by ID. Removes it from the native menu and unlinks it from the linked list.
summaries.0057940D: Sets or clears the grayed/disabled state of a sub-menu item (bit 2). Updates native menu if populated and visible.
summaries.005794CF: Sets or clears the checked state for all sub-items of a given top-level menu item.
summaries.0057953E: Updates the display text of a sub-menu item. Copies new text, applies pipe-to-tab conversion, and updates the native menu if populated and visible.
summaries.005799C0: Creates a native menu header from a pipe-delimited string. Stores the result in the first field of the menu control.
summaries.00579A00: Inserts a menu item into the native menu at the specified position.
summaries.00579A40: Deletes a menu item from the native menu at the given position.
summaries.00579A80: Changes the text of a native menu item.
summaries.00579AC0: Checks or unchecks a native menu item.
summaries.00579B00: Enables or grays a native menu item.
summaries.00579B40: Sets up the parent window for the menu control by getting the native menu handle and associating it with the window.
summaries.00579B90: Returns the first field of the menu control (the native menu handle).
summaries.00579BC0: Sets this+0x98 to param_1. Standard MFC property sheet method.
summaries.00579BF0: Updates the host window's menu bar if a parent window (this+0x78) is set.
summaries.00579C40: Checks if an attack between param_1 and param_2 violates existing treaties. If the two civs have a peace/ceasefire/alliance treaty and either is human, calls thunk_FUN_0045ac71 to trigger a reputation penalty. Also checks for ceasefire violations and sets a reputation damage flag (0x1000) on the tre
summaries.00579DBB: Calculates the strategic value of a city (param_1 = city index) for capture/transfer purposes. Formula: (city_size * civ_treasury) / (civ_num_cities + 1), with overflow protection when treasury exceeds 32000/city_size.
summaries.00579ED0: Checks whether civ param_1 is allowed to attack civ param_2 given current treaties. Shows appropriate warning dialogs (ANNOYALLIED, ANNOYPEACE, ANNOYCEASE, ANNOYVASSAL, ANNOY) and checks for senate override. Returns 0 if attack blocked, 1 if allowed.
summaries.0057A27A: Handles stealing a technology when a civ captures a city or defeats another civ. For human players, presents a radio-button dialog to choose which tech to steal. For AI, picks the tech with highest value. Transfers the selected tech and sends multiplayer notifications.
summaries.0057A685: Finds the most centrally-located city for a given civ (param_1). Computes the sum of distances from each city to all other cities of the same civ, and returns the city index with the smallest total distance.
summaries.0057A7E9: Transfers ownership of a single city (param_1) from civ param_2 to civ param_3. Decrements old owner's city/population counts, increments new owner's, updates the city owner field, and refreshes map visibility.
summaries.0057A904: Handles civil war when a civ's capital is captured. Creates a new rebel civ, splits treasury, copies government/tech/diplomacy data, transfers cities and units to the rebel based on geographic proximity to the capital. Sets initial treaty relations between parent and rebel.
summaries.0057B5DF: The main city capture handler — one of the most complex functions in the binary. Handles all aspects of a city being captured: treaty violations, civil war checks, wonder capture, gold plunder, population reduction, building destruction, partisan spawning, unit reassignment, diplomatic notifications
summaries.0057E2C3: Calculates a unit's maximum hit points. Base HP = unit_type_hp * 8. Adds 50% for veteran status (bit 0x2000 in flags). Adds 50% for "hardened" status (bit 0x10 in flags).
summaries.0057E33A: Calculates unit defense strength considering terrain, fortification, city walls, unit type bonuses, and special combat modifiers. Core combat formula function.
summaries.0057E6E2: Finds the best defender in a stack of units at a given tile. Iterates all units in the stack, computing effective defense for each (including HP ratio if applicable), and returns the unit with the highest effective defense value. Applies bonuses for anti-air, anti-naval, SAM battery, and SDI defense
summaries.0057E9F9: Handles a unit being killed in combat. Increments the kill counter for the owning civ's unit type, records the combat event (for replay/log), increments the global combat counter, and destroys the unit.
summaries.0057EB94: Wipes out an entire stack of units. Sets DAT_006acb0c = 0, then iterates all units in the stack (following next-in-stack pointers at DAT_00656108), calling handle_unit_kill for each.
summaries.0057EBFD: Promotes a unit to veteran status. Sets the veteran flag (0x2000) on the unit's flags word, then shows a "PROMOTED" message to the owning human player or sends a multiplayer notification.
summaries.0057ED3F: Animates combat movement for up to 8 animation channels. Creates sprite animation objects, runs 8 frames of animation with 64ms timing, swapping between source and destination sprite buffers to create a shake/wobble effect. Used for the visual combat animation in the map view.
summaries.0057F628: Destructs array of 8 animation objects at EBP-0x314, each 0x48 bytes.
summaries.0057F657: Plays the nuclear explosion animation at a given map tile. Creates a large (0x5B x 0x48) sprite, centers it on the tile, and runs 11 frames of animation with 100ms timing to show the explosion effect.
summaries.0057F9E3: Handles a nuclear attack on a tile. Shows "USEWEAPONS" warning, checks for SDI defense (cancels if defended), plays nuke animation, then destroys all units in 9 adjacent tiles and poisons tile improvements. Sets war-related treaty flags.
summaries.0057FEBC: Scrambles nearby defensive units to intercept an attack on a tile. First checks for adjacent units with the "scramble" flag that can reach the tile. Then checks for allied/own combat units within 7 tiles on the same continent that can be routed to defend. Sets their orders to "goto" the threatened t
summaries.005802FD: Refreshes display tiles for two map positions after combat. Calls the tile refresh function for both the attacker's and defender's positions.
summaries.00580341: The main combat resolution function. Handles the entire combat pipeline: calculates attack/defense power with modifiers (terrain, city walls, fortification, SAM, coastal fortress, Great Wall, Sun Tzu, veteranship), runs round-by-round combat rolls, handles unit death/capture, city capture/reduction,
summaries.005866A0: Sets keyboard focus to the cosmic editor window and triggers a repaint.
summaries.005866D3: Saves the current 22 cosmic parameters to the editor display buffer at 0x6a2d80, calls a dialog update function, then saves the post-dialog cosmic values to the saved buffer at 0x6a2d28, and restores cosmic parameters from the display buffer. This implements a save/restore cycle for the cosmic param
summaries.005869D4: Populates the cosmic editor list display, formatting each cosmic parameter with its current and saved values, reading from the text file until the entry count is exhausted or a short line is encountered.
summaries.00586BB6: Handles editing a single cosmic parameter value. Shows an input dialog, clamps the entered value between min/max from lookup tables, stores it, and refreshes the display.
summaries.00586D0A: Writes the 22 cosmic parameter values to a rules file, preserving comment text from a template file.
summaries.00586DA1: Attempts to save cosmic parameters to the rules file. If the save fails, shows an error MessageBox. Resets the editor dirty flag and invalidates the object cache.
summaries.00586E24: Shows the cosmic editor effects dialog, then returns focus to the editor.
summaries.00586E88: Closes the cosmic editor by setting the active flag to 0 and invalidating the display cache.
summaries.00586EB0: Repaints the cosmic editor window content by setting up text drawing parameters and triggering a refresh.
summaries.00586F16: Creates and initializes the full cosmic parameter editor window with all controls (list, buttons for OK/Cancel/Help/Effects), then enters a message loop until the editor is closed.
summaries.005875E9: Cleanup helper for cosmic editor — calls FUN_005c656b to release resources.
summaries.005875FF: SEH frame cleanup — restores FS:[0] from saved stack value.
summaries.0058760D: Launches the cosmic parameter editor. Creates a property sheet, initializes the editor window, then cleans up on close.
summaries.00587666: Destructor helper — calls thunk_FUN_004183d0 to destroy a property sheet.
summaries.00587A90: Creates a city list panel with scrollbar and sort button. Populates the list, configures scroll range, and draws the initial content.
summaries.00587E05: Scrolls city list pane 0 to given position.
summaries.00587E23: Scrolls city list pane 1 to given position.
summaries.00587E41: Toggles city list filter ON for the current player. Sends a network message (0xa5) and redraws the list.
summaries.00587F00: Toggles city list filter OFF. Sends network notification and redraws.
summaries.00587FBF: Sends a multiplayer message (0x65) from the city list view, formatting the current player's name.
summaries.0058804F: Sets the scroll position for a city list pane and redraws it.
summaries.005880B0: Handles mouse click on city list. Performs hit testing, then implements single-click toggle, shift-click range select, or ctrl-click multi-select based on modifier key state.
summaries.0058832D: Given mouse coordinates and pane index, returns the list row index under the cursor, or a negative value indicating header/footer/left/right margin click.
summaries.0058843F: Sorts the city list using a simple selection sort. Primary key: cities under siege come first (via building check). Secondary key: alphabetical city name comparison.
summaries.0058878E: Draws the city list panel for a given pane, rendering each visible city row with its sprite, name, status text, and selection highlighting.
summaries.00588E47: Draws a small city sprite icon in the city list at the specified position.
summaries.00588F36: Populates the city list arrays for a given pane. Filters cities by ownership or visibility depending on filter mode. Counts units in each city for the unit count column. Preserves selection state across repopulation. Sorts the result.
summaries.005899F0: C runtime static initializer — constructs a port object and registers an atexit destructor.
summaries.00589A0A: Static constructor — initializes a global port object.
summaries.00589A24: Registers the port destructor as an atexit handler.
summaries.00589A41: Atexit handler — destroys the global port object.
summaries.00589A5B: Application startup — creates the main window, loads essential art resources (GIF palette), initializes the game subsystems, registers crash handler, sets up the window title from INI file.
summaries.00589C79: Application shutdown handler. Calls cleanup function and clears the initialized flag.
summaries.00589D50: Registers a crash callback by passing a window rect pointer to FUN_005bc9d3.
summaries.00589D80: Registers a crash handler callback. Stores the callback pointer in a global array (max 10 slots) and returns the slot index.
summaries.00589DC5: Reads a warning file (e.g., _warn0.dat) and outputs each line to the debug log until a "---" terminator line or EOF.
summaries.00589EF8: Formats an error report from numeric error/module codes, looks up descriptive strings from database files, and calls crash_report_and_exit.
summaries.00589FC9: Looks up a string by line number in a text database file. Returns 0 on success, 1 on failure.
summaries.0058A0EE: Formats a comprehensive crash report with error description, module name, data values, memory allocation info, file error info, DOS error code. Outputs to debug log, calls registered crash handlers, then calls DebugBreak() and _exit(3).
summaries.0058A5CA: Static constructor for sound editor object.
summaries.0058A5E4: Registers sound editor destructor as atexit handler.
summaries.0058A601: Atexit destructor for sound editor global at DAT_006acd58.
summaries.0058A61B: Creates a popup window with configurable flags for centering, titlebar, scrollbar, and optional background image/music. Used as the foundation for many in-game dialogs.
summaries.0058A80D: Copies a file from param_1 to param_2 (binary copy in 1KB blocks). Shows a MessageBox error if either file can't be opened. Skips if source and dest are the same path.
summaries.0058A905: Imports a WAV sound file into the game's SOUND directory. Shows a file open dialog, validates the selection, copies the file to the appropriate slot in the sound directory.
summaries.0058ABEE: Closes sound editor by clearing active flag and invalidating cache.
summaries.0058AC13: Identical to sound_editor_close_a — another close handler.
summaries.0058AC38: Paints the sound editor window, drawing 6 sound slot boxes arranged in a 3x2 grid with labels and a title.
summaries.0058AE20: Tests if point (param_1, param_2) is within rectangle (param_3, param_4, param_5, param_6). Returns 1 if inside, 0 if outside.
summaries.0058AE6C: Handles mouse click in sound editor. Hit-tests each of the 6 sound slot boxes; if a valid slot is clicked, plays the associated sound effect.
summaries.0058AFB6: Populates the 6 sound editor slots based on a unit type index. Maps the unit to appropriate combat/movement sounds based on its domain (land/sea/air), role, and era.
summaries.0058B47E: Creates and runs the sound editor dialog. Sets up 6 sound slot buttons and OK/Cancel buttons, populates slots, then enters a message loop.
summaries.0058B859: Destructs the array of 6 button objects.
summaries.0058BD60: Activates the selected unit by calling move_unit with direction -1 and mode 3 (activate in place).
summaries.0058BD84: Wakes all of the current player's units by refreshing their movement points.
summaries.0058BDFD: Sets the "wait" flag on the selected unit (bit 0x4000 in unit flags word) and advances to the next unit needing orders.
summaries.0058BE56: Handles the "Build City" order. Checks if unit is settler type, validates location (not ocean, not adjacent to existing city), creates city or adds population to existing city, handles city naming dialog, tutorials, and error messages.
summaries.0058C295: Disbands a unit (or city in cheat mode). If disbanded in a city, adds half the unit's shield cost to the city's production. If the unit was the last for a civ, triggers civ death.
summaries.0058C56C: Checks if any of the 5 adjacent cardinal tiles has water or irrigation-relevant features. Returns 1 if water/ocean/river found nearby, 0 otherwise.
summaries.0058C65E: Handles all settler/engineer improvement orders (road, railroad, irrigation, farmland, mining, fortress, airbase, pollution cleanup, terraform). Validates prerequisites (correct unit type, terrain compatibility, required technologies) before initiating the work order.
summaries.0058CBE1: Changes the selected unit's home city to the city at its current location. Caravans cannot be rehomed if they already have a home city. Updates both old and new home city displays.
summaries.0058CCE6: Fortifies the selected unit. Land units can only fortify on land (not ocean), and only in a city or fortress. Sets unit order to "fortified" (1) and refreshes movement.
summaries.0058CDE5: Issues the "unload" order for a transport ship or air unit carrying units. Land units need a city or ship to unload from. For sea units carrying land units, initiates the goto order for unloading.
summaries.0058CFCD: Handles the pillage order. Shows a menu to select which improvement to pillage (road, railroad, irrigation, farmland, fortress, airbase). Handles diplomatic consequences (cancels cease-fire if pillaging in contested territory). Delegates actual pillaging to thunk_FUN_004c50d0.
summaries.0058D442: Places units on sentry duty. If only one unit at location, sentries it directly. If multiple units, shows a unit list dialog to select which to sentry. Sets order to 0xff (idle/sentry) and clears the "needs orders" bit.
summaries.0058D60A: Handles the paradrop order. Validates unit hasn't moved and hasn't paradropped already (bit 0x10 in flags), checks airbase (bit 0x2 in tile improvements), then initiates paradrop targeting.
summaries.0058D6AF: Shows a dialog listing cities the unit can travel to (filtered by domain compatibility and range), then sets the unit's goto order to travel to the selected city. Handles land, sea, and air unit routing differently. For air units, checks fuel range. Loops to show allied cities if no own cities are a
summaries.0058DDCE: Unloads ground units from a transport ship. Sets the ship's wait flag and wakes carried ground units with goto orders pointing at the ship, selecting the first ready one.
summaries.0058DF14: Sets the "automate" flag on a settler/engineer unit (bit 0x8000 in unit flags).
summaries.0058DF7B: Handles the airlift unit order. Validates the unit is ground-domain, in a city with an airport that hasn't already airlifted this turn, then shows a list of destination cities with airports. Checks for enemy fighters in range and warns. Delegates actual airlift to thunk_FUN_004ca1cd.
summaries.0058F010: Shows a message popup only if the current tile/event is visible to the human player (DAT_006ad0d0 != 0).
summaries.0058F040: Processes a goody hut encounter. Randomly selects from 6 outcomes: (0) friendly tribe founding a city, (1) mercenary military units, (2) gold, (3) barbarian horde, (4) technology scroll, (5) nomad settler. Each outcome has era-dependent logic and probability weighting. Handles the full lifecycle of
summaries.0058FDA9: Claims adjacent ocean tiles for a civilization when it builds on a coast. Iterates 8 adjacent tiles; for those that are the same ocean type and have units from the claiming civ, transfers ownership.
summaries.0058FEDB: Handles a caravan/freight unit arriving at a destination city. Shows a dialog offering trade route establishment or wonder contribution. For trade routes, calls thunk_FUN_00440750. For wonder contribution, adds the unit's shield cost to the city's accumulated shields, shows appropriate messages, and
summaries.00590607: Popup dialog close thunk — calls thunk_FUN_0059df8a.
summaries.0059061D: SEH unwind — restores FS:[0] from EBP-0xC.
summaries.0059062C: THE main unit movement function — the single largest function in the binary at ~18KB. Handles all aspects of moving a unit one tile: terrain cost, combat initiation, boarding/unloading transports, diplomatic interactions (expelling, bribery, treaty checks), city capture triggering, carrier landing,
summaries.00594D42: Locks map tiles for multiplayer movement synchronization. In client mode, sends lock request to server and waits for confirmation (up to ~3.5 seconds). In host mode, checks for conflicts with other players' locked tiles. Returns 1 if lock acquired, 0 if denied.
summaries.0059511C: Unlocks map tiles after multiplayer movement completes. In client mode, sends unlock request and waits for server acknowledgment. In host mode, clears the lock table entry and notifies other players.
summaries.00596B00: Gets the maximum allowed count for a spaceship component type (param_2: 0=structural, 1-2=component, 3-5=module). The max depends on base limits in DAT_00634f64 array and the civ's current spaceship progress. For components (1-2): limit scales with (habitation_count + 1) / divisor. For modules (3-5)
summaries.00596C08: Returns the clamped count of a spaceship component — min of current count and max allowed.
summaries.00596C61: Gets the total max allowed for a spaceship category: structural (type 0), components (types 1+2 summed), or modules (types 3+4+5 summed).
summaries.00596CED: Returns the absolute maximum for a spaceship category from the global limits table. Category 0=structural, 1=sum of component limits, 2=sum of module limits.
summaries.00596D3C: Returns the raw (unclamped) total count of spaceship parts for a category. Category 0=structural count, 1=fuel+propulsion, 2=habitation+life+solar.
summaries.00596E06: Returns the clamped total for a spaceship category (sum of clamped individual component counts).
summaries.00596E92: Calculates population capacity from habitation module count. Formula: sum of (1 per module for first 4, 2 per module for 5-6, 3 per module for 7+).
summaries.00596EEC: Recalculates all spaceship statistics for a civ: mass, fuel ratio, energy ratio, life support ratio, flight time, success probability, and estimated arrival year. Updates the civ's spaceship state record.
summaries.005973FD: Launches a civ's spaceship. Sets the launch flag, records the launch turn, displays "LAUNCHED" message, shows spaceship screen. For AI civs: orders all cities to max production. For human civs: triggers AI responses — other civs may declare war or escalate to prevent victory.
summaries.0059772C: Displays the spaceship status dialog for a civ. Shows all component counts, ratios (fuel, energy, life support), mass, flight time, success probability, and launch button if ready. If launched, shows arrival year. If player clicks "Launch", calls spaceship_launch.
summaries.00597D6F: AI evaluation of which spaceship category to build next. Checks if structural/component/module sections are complete, evaluates whether to build more in the requested category or switch to a different one. Returns category index (0-5 for specific part, -1 for none needed, -2 for can't build).
summaries.00598197: Handles building a spaceship component for a human or AI player. For humans, shows a dialog to choose which component to build. For AI, picks the component with fewest built. After building, recalculates stats and checks for auto-launch conditions (AI only).
summaries.00598A05: Checks if a spaceship section is complete. param_2: 0x23=structural, 0x24=components, 0x25=modules. Returns 1 if all components in the section meet their maximum limits, 0 otherwise.
summaries.00598B4E: Shows the "View Spaceships" menu. Lists all civs with active space programs, lets the player select one to view, then opens the spaceship dialog for that civ.
summaries.00598CEB: Returns whether the spaceship victory condition is enabled. Disabled if scenario flag 0x80 is set, or if no space tech (DAT_00655c18 == -1) and no civ has the required tech.
summaries.00598D45: Determines if an AI civ should start building spaceship parts. Returns 1 if: (a) space race is enabled, (b) any human civ has already launched, OR (c) another AI with the required tech is ahead in the race. Compares weighted component scores.
summaries.005998B0: Returns true if a civ's raw component count for a category meets or exceeds the global maximum for that category.
summaries.00599910: Checks if a civ can build in a spaceship category. Returns 1 if: the category max isn't reached, or the raw count is 0 and the section isn't complete, and the civ has the required technology.
summaries.005999C0: Returns true if raw count >= max allowed for a spaceship category.
summaries.00599A20: Initializes the Civilopedia item list. Validates and adjusts the current selection index, copies item IDs from the source array to the display array, sets up the scrollbar, and refreshes the display.
summaries.00599B8D: Draws the detail view for a selected Civilopedia item. Renders the item name, icon, cost, attack/defense values, and full description text. Uses the sprite drawing system for icons and the text layout system for descriptions.
summaries.0059A15D: Loads a Civilopedia description from the PEDIA section of the game text file. Reads lines until an '@' delimiter, skipping comments (';'), processing formatting markers ('^', '*'), and building the full description string in DAT_00679640.
summaries.0059A2E6: Navigates the Civilopedia to a specific item by index. If index < 0x27, finds the item in the list, updates the selection, redraws the detail view, and refreshes the display. If index >= 0x27, delegates to thunk_FUN_00526913 (likely a different encyclopedia section).
summaries.0059A6F0: Sets the random number generator seed. If param_1 is 0, returns 0 (and leaves seed unchanged). Otherwise, sets DAT_00635094 = param_1 and returns the previous seed value.
summaries.0059A733: Generates the next random number using a linear congruential generator: seed = seed * 0x19660D + 0x3C6EF35F. Returns seed / 0xFFFFFFFF as a float10 (0.0 to 1.0).
summaries.0059A791: Returns a random integer in the range [param_1, param_2]. If param_1 == param_2, just advances the RNG and returns param_1. Otherwise, generates a float, scales to range, and truncates.
summaries.0059A850: C runtime static initializer — calls netmgr constructor and registers atexit handler.
summaries.0059A884: Registers FUN_0059a8a1 (netmgr_dtor thunk) as an atexit handler.
summaries.0059A8BB: Initializes the network manager. Calls popup message init and base network init, sets up XDaemon callback function pointers for: broadcast receive, secure receive, client connection, new client, connection lost, and oversized message handling (max 50000 bytes).
summaries.0059A998: Resets all network manager state to defaults. Zeros player slots, connection status, message queues, timeout values, and all per-player stat arrays. Sets default timeout to 60, message queue size to 8. Calls disconnect and counter reset.
summaries.0059AD40: Network manager destructor. Frees the network buffer, disconnects, resets counters, and destroys the base object.
summaries.0059ADEF: Establishes a network connection. Supports 4 connection types (0=TCP, 1=IPX, 2=Modem, 3=Serial). Reads/writes timeout settings from CIV.INI. Initializes the appropriate XDaemon transport, activates server if hosting, sets up player name/game name, and marks connection as active.
summaries.0059B293: Disconnects from the network. Flushes send buffers, closes connection, shuts down the appropriate transport (sockets/modem/serial), logs max queue depths, resets library, and optionally waits 2 seconds for graceful disconnect.
summaries.0059B571: Updates the network player list by inserting/updating a player entry. Removes any existing entry with the same name, then inserts the new entry in alphabetical order by game name. Each entry is a 0x124-byte struct containing player name, game info, and timestamps.
summaries.0059B7FC: Adds a new client to the network game. Finds the first empty player slot (0-6), copies the client's connection ID, name, and game name into the slot, marks it active, and decrements the available slots counter.
summaries.0059B96A: Removes a client from the network game by connection ID. Finds the matching player slot, clears the human player bit in DAT_00655b0b if the player had a civ assigned, zeros out the slot data, and decrements the player count.
summaries.0059BAF0: Frees all entries in the network player linked list. Iterates from head, deleting each 0x124-byte node.
summaries.0059BB54: Callback for receiving broadcast network messages. Validates message has the magic header 0x66606660, filters by game name, and dispatches valid messages to the message handler.
summaries.0059BC41: Callback for receiving secure (connection-based) network messages. Validates the magic header, dispatches to message handler, and records message stats. If message is corrupt (wrong header), logs extensive debug info including hex dump of the first 32 bytes and attempts to find a valid message withi
summaries.0059BFB5: Callback for when a new client connects. Simply logs the connection with the client ID.
summaries.0059BFDB: Callback for when this client connects to a server. Records the server's player slot ID and sets the connected flag. Sends a handshake message (type 0x2F) to the server.
summaries.0059C04B: Callback for connection lost events. Increments the total lost connection counter and the per-player lost connection counter for the disconnected player.
summaries.0059C0A4: Callback for oversized message events. Logs the message size.
summaries.0059C0E1: Builds a network packet by prepending a 0x2C-byte header to the payload data. Handles both initial packet creation (param_2 == NULL) and appending to existing packets. The header contains magic number, message type, size, and 7 parameter fields.
summaries.0059C276: Clears all 169 message type counters to zero.
summaries.0059C2B8: Clears the total connection loss counter and all 7 per-player loss counters.
summaries.0059C31F: Fills a game info structure (0x124 bytes) with the current game settings for network advertisement. Includes: version string, player name, game name, scenario name, difficulty, barbarians, player count, map size, turn number, year, and various game option flags.
summaries.0059C575: Records a combat kill event in the per-civ combat history ring buffer (300 entries per civ). Stores: killed unit type, attacker type ID, turn number, attacker civ name. Each entry is 0x22 bytes. Advances the write pointer (wrapping at 300) and increments total count.
summaries.0059D080: Initializes a popup message structure (0x118 bytes). Zeros out title, text fields, radio button slots (7 players x 24 bytes), and sets the structure size field to 0x118.
summaries.0059D190: Static initializer pair — constructs object and registers atexit.
summaries.0059D1AA: Constructs a static object with params (0, 0x10, 1).
summaries.0059D363: Sets DAT_006cec84 = DAT_00635a58 (copies a default timer value).
summaries.0059D37D: Both set _DAT_006cec80 to the current time (thunk_FUN_00421bb0).
summaries.0059D3E1: Sets popup dialog position: DAT_006359cc = param_1, DAT_006359d0 = param_2.
summaries.0059D401: Loads 3 popup label strings from the POPUPS/LABELS section of the text file. Stores string IDs in DAT_006cec98 and resolved pointers in PTR_DAT_00635a48.
summaries.0059D527: Each sets one of three popup background bitmap pointers.
summaries.0059D56F: Sets all three popup bitmap pointers to default values (DAT_006ceca8, DAT_006cec78, DAT_006cec88).
summaries.0059D5B5: Resets and initializes a popup list control with 9 slots and param_1 items.
summaries.0059D5F5: Resets all fields of a popup dialog structure to default values. Initializes counters, positions, colors, margins, button states, and all 100+ fields of the popup object. This is the main initialization for the popup dialog system.
summaries.0059DB08: Creates a new popup dialog object. Initializes base class, resets all fields, and sets up the list control with param_1 as the style flags.
summaries.0059DB65: Destroys a popup dialog, freeing all associated GDI resources (bitmaps, fonts, etc.) and popping it from the popup stack. Validates stack index is in range [1, 16].
summaries.0059DF8A: Closes a popup dialog by destroying it and clearing its list control.
summaries.0059DFB9: Opens a popup dialog with specified parameters (title, position, dimensions, flags). Initializes or resets the dialog fields, sets the bitmap pointers, and configures the layout dimensions.
summaries.0059E0EB: Sets the text of an edit field in a popup dialog, identified by field ID. Validates the text fits within the field's allocated buffer size.
summaries.0059E18B: Adds a text edit field to a popup dialog. Allocates a 0x20-byte node, sets field ID, label, default text, and dimensions. Appends to the linked list of edit fields.
summaries.0059E327: Returns whether the popup dialog is a "special" type by testing bit 7 (0x80) of the flags byte at this+0x3C.
summaries.0059E356: Returns the constant 0x20 (32), which is the standard button icon width for popup dialogs.
summaries.0059E376: Computes the maximum popup text area height from the bitmap. Gets button width (0x20), gets bitmap height, computes height * 5 / 4 as upper bound. Returns the lesser of button width and scaled bitmap height, clamped to the actual bitmap height.
summaries.0059E3FA: Gets the line height for the current page. If the page has a custom line height (this+0x200 + page*4), returns it. Otherwise returns default font height + 1.
summaries.0059E448: Returns the popup bitmap height plus 10 pixels of padding.
summaries.0059E472: Sets the popup dialog's bitmap pointer (this+8 = param_1) and recalculates the layout height (this+0xB4) from the bitmap dimensions.
summaries.0059E4A4: MFC library function CArchive::SetObjectSchema. Sets the object schema version field (this+0xC) to param_1.
summaries.0059E4C5: Sets popup dialog field at this+0x10 to param_1. Used for popup configuration (likely a display mode or option flags field).
summaries.0059E4E6: Sets popup dialog field at this+0x38 to param_1. Used for popup configuration (likely a style or behavior field).
summaries.0059E507: Sets the radio button column count for the current page and calculates the total radio section height. Sets flag 0x1000 in the popup flags. If flag bit 0x400 is clear, uses line height * columns + 2; otherwise uses font height * columns + 2.
summaries.0059E585: Sets the current page index for the popup dialog. Validates param_1 is 0 or 1; defaults to 0 for any other value.
summaries.0059E5C9: Sets page layout parameters for the current page: column offset (this+0x4C[page*4]) and secondary parameter (this+0x1E0[page*4]). Sets flag 0x1000. If param_1 is non-zero, also configures the radio column count.
summaries.0059E624: MFC library method. Sets field at this+0xC8 to param_1. Used as a popup dialog object setter for field 0xC8.
summaries.0059E648: Calculates the height of the button area in a popup dialog. Returns bitmap_height + 4 + (button_count * 2).
summaries.0059E676: Calculates the width needed for a text string in the popup, including padding. Returns text_width + 4 + (checkbox_size * 4).
summaries.0059E6A9: Sets the popup dialog title string. Allocates a buffer from the popup's memory pool large enough for the string, copies param_1 into it, and stores the pointer at this+0x134.
summaries.0059E6FF: Sets the popup dialog width (this+0x11C) with optional resolution scaling. If the popup's source and target resolutions differ (this+0x80 != this+0x84) and flag bit 0x800 is clear, scales param_1 proportionally. Otherwise stores param_1 directly.
summaries.0059E762: MFC/CRT library function ios::delbuf. Sets the delete-buffer flag at this+0x1C to param_1. Used by the popup dialog system as a generic field setter.
summaries.0059E783: Sets two popup dialog position fields: this+0x14 = param_1, this+0x18 = param_2. Used to position content within the popup.
summaries.0059E7AD: Searches the popup's radio option linked list (head at this+0x228) for a node whose ID field (node+4) matches param_1. Returns the node pointer or 0 if not found.
summaries.0059E812: Searches the popup's edit field linked list (head at this+0x238) for a node whose ID field (node+0xC) matches param_1. Returns the node pointer or 0 if not found.
summaries.0059E877: Searches the popup's button linked list (head at this+0x234) for a node whose first field (node[0]) matches param_1. Returns the node pointer or NULL if not found.
summaries.0059E8DB: Sets or clears the "selected" flag (bit 0) on a radio option identified by param_1. If param_2 is non-zero, sets bit 0; otherwise clears it.
summaries.0059E927: Sets or clears the "disabled" flag (bit 1) on a radio option identified by param_1. If param_2 is non-zero, sets bit 1; otherwise clears it.
summaries.0059E973: Clears the "selected" flag (bit 0) on all radio options in the popup's linked list.
summaries.0059E9B3: Clears the "disabled" flag (bit 1) on all radio options in the popup's linked list.
summaries.0059E9F3: Returns whether a radio option (identified by param_1) has the "grayed" flag (bit 2) set. Returns 1 if grayed, 0 otherwise.
summaries.0059EA4D: Sets or clears the "grayed" flag (bit 2) on a radio option identified by param_1. If param_2 is non-zero, sets bit 2; otherwise clears it.
summaries.0059EA99: Sets the default selected item in the popup by ID. If this+0x28 is 0 (button mode), finds the button and stores it at this+0x224. If non-zero (radio mode), finds the radio option and stores it at this+0x220.
summaries.0059EB0D: Wrapper that calls thunk_FUN_00418a70 with param_2, ignoring param_1. Likely a sound play wrapper for popup events.
summaries.0059EB42: Wrapper that calls FUN_0059eb0d with first argument 0, forwarding param_1 as the second argument. Plays a default sound on popup events.
summaries.0059EB68: MFC library method. Sets field at this+0xD8 to param_1. Used as a popup dialog object setter for field 0xD8.
summaries.0059EB8C: MFC library method. Sets field at this+0x23C to param_1. Used as a popup dialog object setter for field 0x23C.
summaries.0059EBB0: MFC library method. Sets field at this+0x240 to param_1. Used as a popup dialog object setter for field 0x240.
summaries.0059EBD4: MFC library method. Sets field at this+0x244 to param_1. Used as a popup dialog object setter for field 0x244.
summaries.0059EBF8: MFC library method. Sets field at this+0x248 to param_1. Used as a popup dialog object setter for field 0x248.
summaries.0059EC1C: MFC library method. Sets field at this+0x24C to param_1. Used as a popup dialog object setter for field 0x24C.
summaries.0059EC40: MFC library method. Sets field at this+0x250 to param_1. Used as a popup dialog object setter for field 0x250.
summaries.0059EC64: MFC library method. Sets field at this+0x130 to param_1. Used as a popup dialog object setter for field 0x130.
summaries.0059EC88: Adds a button to the popup dialog. Allocates a 0x14-byte node from the popup memory pool, links it to the tail of the button list (this+0x234). Sets the button's ID (param_2), icon resource (param_1), and optional label text (param_3). Tracks the maximum button width at this+0x120 for layout calcula
summaries.0059EDF0: Adds a radio button option to the popup dialog. Allocates a 0x18-byte doubly-linked node. If flag 0x80 is set, inserts in alphabetical order (case-insensitive); otherwise appends to tail. Maintains head (this+0x228) and tail (this+0x22C) pointers. If label is empty, auto-selects the option. Tracks m
summaries.0059F026: Adds a radio option with the "initially checked" (bit 2) flag. Sets flag 0x05 on the popup (bits 0 and 2), calls popup_add_radio_option with param_3=0, then if param_3 was non-zero, sets bit 2 (grayed) on the returned node.
summaries.0059F06D: Adds a text input/edit field to the popup dialog. Allocates a 0x1C-byte node and a 0x100-byte text buffer. Computes the input field width based on character width * max_length, adjusting for scrollbar overflow if the total width exceeds the popup width. Initializes the text buffer with param_2 (defa
summaries.0059F2A3: Adds an action button label string to the popup dialog. Supports up to 6 labels (this+0x294 array, index tracked at this+0x34). Allocates a string buffer from the popup memory pool and copies the label text.
summaries.0059F31A: Sets the text rendering style for popup labels based on three boolean parameters: param_1 (bold), param_2 (shadow), param_3 (underline). Selects one of four font objects stored at this+0x64, this+0x68, this+0x6C, this+0x70 and activates it via FUN_005c19ad.
summaries.0059F3D7: Renders a popup label at a given position. First replaces all underscores with spaces. If the dialog has a shadow font (this+0x68 != this+0x64), draws the shadow version offset by (+1,+1), then draws the foreground version. Returns the x-position after the text.
summaries.0059F5BA: Renders text at a position offset by the popup's vertical scroll offset (this+0xF8). Calls the text rendering function with adjusted y-coordinate.
summaries.0059F5F7: Returns whether any text node in the popup's text list has a negative line count (node+0x10 < 0), indicating a special formatting marker. Iterates the text list starting at this+0x230.
summaries.0059F64A: Lays out and optionally renders all text elements in a popup dialog with word wrapping. Processes the linked list of text nodes (this+0x230), handling line breaks, centered text (flag bit 1), inline images (positive node+0x10), and standard text formatting. Returns the total height consumed.
summaries.0059FB78: Returns the index of a radio option within its page group. Iterates all radio options in the list (this+0x228), counting only those with the same page ID (node+0xC) as param_1. Returns the count when it reaches param_1.
summaries.0059FC19: Returns the radio option node at a given index within the current page group. Iterates radio options, counting only those matching the current page (this+0x48). Returns the node at the param_1-th position.
summaries.0059FCBA: Returns the page number (0-based) that a radio option falls on, based on the column count per page. Divides the option's index by the columns-per-page value.
summaries.0059FCF2: Returns the radio option at the start of page param_1. Multiplies param_1 by columns-per-page and retrieves that indexed option.
summaries.0059FD2A: The main popup dialog layout function (4785 bytes, the largest popup system function). Computes positions and sizes for all elements: title, text area, radio buttons, edit fields, action buttons, and scrollbar. Handles multi-column radio layouts with page navigation, text word-wrapping via popup_lay
summaries.005A0FEA: Draws text in a popup item, truncating it if it exceeds the available column width. Applies shadow text if the text/highlight color differs from the normal color.
summaries.005A1148: Draws a popup item that may contain a '|' separator for two-column display. Splits the string at '|', draws left part left-aligned and right part right-aligned within the column.
summaries.005A120B: Draws a single popup list item. Computes item position from its index, draws selection highlight if selected, fills background, draws text with optional highlight colors, and invalidates the item rect if requested.
summaries.005A14D2: Redraws all visible items in both panes (0 and 1 if dual-pane) of a popup list. Iterates through the linked list of items, drawing each visible one, and handling empty-space background fill.
summaries.005A1766: Converts an item count to a page count by dividing by the items-per-column for the current pane. If columns==1, returns the count unchanged.
summaries.005A17A4: Aligns an index to a column boundary by subtracting the remainder when divided by items-per-column.
summaries.005A17E9: Selects an item in the popup list. Updates the scroll position if the new selection is not visible, redraws affected items, and calls the selection callback if defined.
summaries.005A1A44: Clamps param_3 to range [param_1, param_2]. If value < min, returns min; if value > max, returns max.
summaries.005A1A7D: Hit-tests a mouse coordinate against the popup list to determine which item index is under the cursor. Returns special negative values for areas outside the list (above=-1, below=-2, left=-3, right=-4).
summaries.005A1C52: Counts items in the linked list that belong to a given pane (param_1).
summaries.005A1CAF: Determines which pane (0 or 1) a mouse coordinate falls in. Sets `this->current_pane` accordingly. If dual-pane mode and coord is outside both panes, restores original pane.
summaries.005A1DA3: Sets the scroll position of the active popup to a given item index and redraws if position changed.
summaries.005A1E28: Creates the popup window (surface + dialog). Allocates the drawing surface, sets up palette/parent, creates the window at specified position/size, configures message handlers.
summaries.005A20F4: Paint callback — if active popup exists, calls popup_paint.
summaries.005A211C: Massive function that initializes all controls for the popup dialog system. Creates grid controls, scrollbars, icon lists, tab buttons, text buttons, and checkbox lists based on the popup configuration flags. Handles single-pane and dual-pane modes.
summaries.005A3BAE: Handles grid item selection in the popup. Finds the selected item by index, stores its ID, sets the "selection changed" flag, and triggers redraw.
summaries.005A3C58: Marks the popup for redraw by setting the 0x400 flag and invalidating the window.
summaries.005A3C83: Handles icon selection if not in tab mode (flag 0x4 not set on byte 0x3d). Sets flag 0x2000 and invalidates.
summaries.005A3CCA: Handles tab button clicks in the popup. Navigates to the clicked tab, updates current tab pointer, calls optional tab callback, and redraws.
summaries.005A3DF3: Calls the tab help callback (offset 0x240) if defined. If returns 0, just redraws; if non-zero, also sets 0x2000 flag.
summaries.005A3E56: Handles button clicks in the popup (OK, Cancel, Help, custom buttons). Updates timestamp, processes button type (cancel=set -1, ok=set selected item, help=re-select current tab). For list mode, reads current list selection.
summaries.005A407F: Handles keyboard input in the popup. Supports navigation keys (arrows, page up/down, home/end) for both list and tab modes. For dual-pane mode, handles tab traversal with up/down arrows. For grid mode, handles multi-column navigation with left/right arrows and page scrolling.
summaries.005A49C1: Handles alphabetic key input for quick-jump in popup lists. Searches for the first item starting with the typed letter and scrolls to it. Supports list mode, tab mode, and button/shortcut modes.
summaries.005A4DF3: Handles left mouse button down in the popup. If click is on a valid item, starts mouse tracking (capture) and selects the item.
summaries.005A4EDF: Handles mouse move during drag in the popup. If dragging outside the list area, auto-scrolls in the appropriate direction (up/down/left/right) and adjusts the selection.
summaries.005A535E: Handles left mouse button up. Finalizes the mouse move, clears drag flag, releases mouse capture.
summaries.005A53B8: Handles double-click on a popup item. If valid click on an item in grid mode (not tab mode), simulates button-up, sets the "confirmed selection" flag (0x2000), and invalidates.
summaries.005A5494: Handles vertical scrollbar page scroll for pane 0. Sets pane to 0, computes new scroll position, and redraws if changed.
summaries.005A552C: Handles vertical scrollbar position change for pane 1. Sets pane to 1, updates scroll position, redraws if changed.
summaries.005A55B1: Handles vertical scrollbar page scroll for pane 1. Same as pane0_page but for pane 1.
summaries.005A5649: Draws the popup background. Supports solid fill (default), tiled bitmap (DAT_00635aa0/DAT_00635aa4), and custom bitmap backgrounds.
summaries.005A577E: Master paint function for the popup system. Creates window/controls if needed, draws borders, title, background, tab bar, item list, buttons. Handles both initial paint and refresh paint.
summaries.005A5F34: Shows the popup as a modal dialog and runs the message loop until the user makes a selection or cancels. Returns the selection result from this->selection_id (offset 0xd8).
summaries.005A632A: Parses a game text file section to configure and populate a popup dialog. Handles @-directives (OPTIONS, PROMPT, TITLE, BUTTON, COLUMNS, HEIGHT, SMALLFONT, WIDTH, LENGTH, CHECKBOX, LISTBOX, SYSTEM, DEFAULT) and text/option lines. This is the core text file parser for all Civ2 popup dialogs.
summaries.005A6C23: Pushes the current parent surface onto a one-deep stack and sets a new one.
summaries.005A6C45: Pops the parent surface from the one-deep stack.
summaries.005A9320: MFC library vector deleting destructors for various types (CString 0x3c, button 0x48, dialog 0x3c, tab 0x40). Standard MFC patterns: if flag & 2, vector destroy; if flag & 1, scalar delete. Plus two scalar deleting destructors (FUN_005a94d0, FUN_005a95b0).
summaries.005A9600: Sends a message via the popup's internal handle at ECX+8.
summaries.005A9640: Clears the checkbox state at ECX+0x34 to 0.
summaries.005A9670: Gets item text from a list control via a Windows message.
summaries.005A96B0: Gets text from an edit control via a Windows message.
summaries.005A96F0: Returns whether checkbox mode is active (flag 0x1 at byte 0x3d).
summaries.005A9730: Draws an icon in the popup at the given position.
summaries.005A9780: Sets the global drawing surface to param_1.
summaries.005A9798: Draws a colored rectangle. Sets the draw color then fills the rect.
summaries.005A97CC: Draws a horizontal line from (param_2, param_4) to (param_3+1, param_4+1) with the given color.
summaries.005A9811: Draws a horizontal line of given width: (param_2, param_3) to (param_4+param_2, param_3+1).
summaries.005A9858: Draws a vertical line from (param_2, param_3) to (param_2+1, param_4+1).
summaries.005A989D: Draws a vertical line of given height: (param_2, param_3) to (param_2+1, param_4+param_3).
summaries.005A98E4: Draws a rectangle outline (4 lines: top, bottom, left, right).
summaries.005A9964: Draws a rectangle outline from (x, y) with given width and height.
summaries.005A99FC: Draws a 3D border around a RECT. Top/left in light color (param_3), bottom/right in dark color (param_4).
summaries.005A9AA3: Clears a surface region by calling FUN_005c041f.
summaries.005A9ABF: Fills a rectangle specified by (x, y, w, h) with a solid color.
summaries.005A9AFE: Blits a rectangle from one position to another, both specified by (x, y, w, h).
summaries.005A9B5D: Tiles a source bitmap to fill a destination rectangle. Handles partial tiles at the edges and wrapping.
summaries.005A9CE9: Draws an inset 3D border (reversed light/dark from draw_3d_border), then shrinks the rect by 1 pixel.
summaries.005A9F30: Checks if all human players are ready for production phase in multiplayer. If all ready (or single player), invalidates the UI to proceed.
summaries.005AA004: Same as above but for the movement phase. Also triggers if DAT_006ad308 < 2 (single player/quit).
summaries.005AA0E5: Server-side multiplayer turn processing loop (production + moves). This is the main game loop for the server in multiplayer. Handles production phase: runs AI players, waits for all humans to submit production. Then handles movement phase: processes each player's moves, waits for all humans to finis
summaries.005AB0A3: Client-side check for production phase readiness. Invalidates UI if production ready signal received, network lost, game ended, or data received.
summaries.005AB120: Client-side check for movement phase readiness. Same pattern as waitprod but checks DAT_006c9218.
summaries.005AB19D: Client-side check for human player ready state. Triggers on ready signal, AI flag, network loss, disconnect, game end, or data received.
summaries.005AB23A: Alternative client-side ready check. Same conditions but with OR instead of AND for some flags.
summaries.005AB2D5: Client-side multiplayer turn processing loop. Waits for server signals through each phase: production, AI moves, human moves. Displays maps, handles turn timer, shows casualties, and manages PBEM auto-play. Nearly identical structure to the server version but operates as a client.
summaries.005AC840: Initializes the unit list in the Civilopedia. Copies unit IDs to display array, sets scrollbar range and position aligned to 9-item pages. Nearly identical to init_scrollbar_list (FUN_00518f00) but uses offset 0xb9c/0xc98 instead of 0xd90/0xdb0.
summaries.005AC9AD: Draws the full unit detail page in the Civilopedia. Shows unit sprite, name, stats (attack, defense, hit points, firepower, movement, cost), prerequisite tech, and ability flags parsed from PEDIAUNITFACTS.
summaries.005AD998: Selects and displays a unit type in the Civilopedia. Finds the unit in the list, updates the selection, draws the detail page, and runs the message loop.
summaries.005ADFA0: Clamps a value to [min, max] range. Identical logic to FUN_005a1a44 (clamp_value).
summaries.005ADFD9: Swaps two 4-byte values at the given pointers.
summaries.005AE006: Counts the number of set bits in the low 8 bits of param_1 (population count).
summaries.005AE052: Wraps an X coordinate for a cylindrical (non-flat) map. If flat earth flag (0x8000) is set, returns unchanged. Otherwise wraps negative values by adding map width, and values >= width by subtracting.
summaries.005AE0B0: Wraps a Y coordinate similarly to wrap_x but using DAT_006d116a (map height). Only active on round maps.
summaries.005AE10E: Computes the minimum X distance between two points, accounting for map wrapping on cylindrical maps.
summaries.005AE17D: Computes the tile distance in X between two points: `(distance_x_wrapped(p1, p2) + 1) >> 1`.
summaries.005AE1B0: Computes the tile distance between two (x,y) tile coordinates: `(abs_dx_wrapped + abs_dy) >> 1`. This is the Civ2 "tile distance" metric (Chebyshev-like on the staggered grid).
summaries.005AE24D: Computes tile distance from dx/dy deltas: `(abs(dx) + abs(dy)) >> 1`.
summaries.005AE296: Computes a diagonal movement cost from dx/dy. Returns a value that accounts for the longer axis being more expensive than simple tile distance. Formula: `longer - ((distance - shorter + 1) >> 1)` where distance = `(abs_dx + abs_dy) >> 1`.
summaries.005AE31D: Computes movement cost between two map coordinates, combining wrapped X distance with Y distance, then applying diagonal_movement_cost.
summaries.005AE37B: Returns 1 if two directions (0-7) are adjacent (differ by 1, with wrapping), 0 otherwise.
summaries.005AE3BF: Converts a bit index to byte offset and bit mask. `*param_2 = param_1 >> 3` (byte), `*param_3 = 1 << (param_1 & 7)` (mask).
summaries.005AE3EC: Shifts param_1 by param_2 bits. If param_2 > 0, left shift. If param_2 < 0, right shift by abs(param_2). If param_2 == 0, returns unchanged.
summaries.005AE580: PBEM (Play-By-Email) game setup flow. Shows initial menu (new game, load, scenario load, continue). For new game: randomizes map params, runs new_game_setup. For load: loads save file. Handles scenario loading and PBEM game continuation. Manages player selection for each human player in sequence.
summaries.005AEC14: Prompts for a PBEM email address and stores it in the per-player email array.
summaries.005AEF20: Copies unit type data from the game's unit type tables into the editor buffer arrays for editing. Copies name, domain, prerequisite, stats (attack, defense, cost, hitpoints, firepower, movement, range), and ability flags for all 62 unit types.
summaries.005AF140: Copies unit type data from the editor buffer back to the game's unit type tables. Reverse of editor_save_unit_types_to_buffer. Multiplies cost by DAT_0064bcc8 and movement by 10 when saving back.
summaries.005A93B0: MFC vector deleting destructor for button-sized objects (0x48 bytes). If flag & 2, vector-destroys via thunk_FUN_00418870; if flag & 1, scalar-deletes.
summaries.005A9440: MFC vector deleting destructor for dialog-sized objects (0x3c bytes). If flag & 2, vector-destroys via thunk_FUN_0040f570; if flag & 1, scalar-deletes.
summaries.005A94D0: Scalar deleting destructor. Calls thunk_FUN_00418ea0 to destroy the object, then conditionally frees memory if flag & 1.
summaries.005A9520: MFC vector deleting destructor for tab-sized objects (0x40 bytes). If flag & 2, vector-destroys via thunk_FUN_00453ba0; if flag & 1, scalar-deletes.
summaries.005A95B0: Scalar deleting destructor. Calls thunk_FUN_0044cba0 to destroy the object, then conditionally frees memory if flag & 1.
summaries.005AB07F: Cleanup helper for wait_production_005aa0e5. Calls thunk_FUN_0059df8a to close/destroy the dialog.
summaries.005AB095: SEH frame restore for wait_production_005aa0e5. Restores the previous exception handler from the stack frame.
summaries.005ABFDB: Cleanup helper for wait_production_005ab2d5. Calls thunk_FUN_0059df8a to close/destroy the dialog.
summaries.005ABFF1: SEH frame restore for wait_production_005ab2d5. Restores the previous exception handler from the stack frame.
summaries.005AEBEF: Cleanup helper for PBEM email dialog (FUN_005ae580). Calls thunk_FUN_0059df8a to close/destroy the dialog.
summaries.005AEC05: SEH frame restore for PBEM email dialog. Restores the previous exception handler from the stack frame.
summaries.005AED0D: Cleanup helper for pbem_get_email_address (FUN_005aec14). Calls thunk_FUN_0059df8a to close/destroy the dialog.
summaries.005AED23: SEH frame restore for pbem_get_email_address. Restores the previous exception handler from the stack frame.
summaries.005B02A5: Toggles enable/disable state of two editor buttons based on a combobox selection index (0, 1, or 2).
summaries.005B0373: Handles editor control commands. Command 0xc9: reads spinner values and either accepts or shows a notice dialog. Command 0xcd: refreshes button states.
summaries.005B0473: Creates and populates a listbox control for the unit editor. Depending on param_1 (0-4), fills with unit type names, advance names, or ability flag names.
summaries.005B08E8: Creates a numeric spinner control at a position determined by a layout table, with range 0-3.
summaries.005B09DC: Paints the unit type editor window. Draws the unit sprite preview, 3D borders, and 13 labeled parameter fields (attack, defense, movement, cost, range, etc.).
summaries.005B1037: Creates and runs the full unit type editor window. Loads editor artwork, creates 13 spinner controls and 5 listbox controls, sets up 7 buttons, enters message loop until closed.
summaries.005B2590: Validates the integrity of a unit's linked list stack. Checks for infinite loops, dead units in stack, and crossed locations (units in stack at different map positions). Repairs broken links by severing them. Returns 1 if valid, 0 if repairs were needed.
summaries.005B29AA: Returns the maximum hit points for a unit based on its type.
summaries.005B29D7: Returns the remaining HP of a unit (max_hp - damage). If hitpoint combat is disabled (flag 0x10 not set), resets damage to 0 first. Minimum return is 0.
summaries.005B2A39: Calculates total movement points for a unit, including bonuses from techs (Nuclear Power +1 for sea, Lighthouse +2 for sea, Magellan +1 for sea if no sub flag) and HP-based reduction when hitpoint combat is active.
summaries.005B2C3D: Returns remaining movement points (total - spent). Minimum 0.
summaries.005B2C82: Returns the next unit in the stack linked list, or -1 if at end. Validates stack first.
summaries.005B2CC3: Follows next pointers to find the last unit in the stack.
summaries.005B2D39: Follows prev pointers to find the first unit in the stack.
summaries.005B2DAF: Finds the first unit at position (param_2, param_3) belonging to civ param_1. Scans all units linearly, then returns the first in that unit's stack.
summaries.005B2E69: Finds the first unit of any civ at map position (param_1, param_2). If DAT_00636058 is set, requires a unit to be visible at the tile first. Returns first unit in stack, or -1.
summaries.005B2F50: Sets a unit's order to "goto" (3). If the unit wasn't already on goto, resets the goto target.
summaries.005B2F92: Returns the Nth unit in a stack (0-indexed from first). Returns -1 if N exceeds stack size.
summaries.005B3007: Counts how many units precede the given unit in the stack (following prev pointers). Returns -1 if unit is invalid.
summaries.005B3046: Finds the Nth unit with a specific role (param_3) in a stack. Returns unit index or -1.
summaries.005B30E9: Counts total units in a stack.
summaries.005B3136: Counts how many units of a specific type are in a stack.
summaries.005B319E: Removes a unit from its map tile stack. Unlinks it from the prev/next chain, sets its position to offscreen coordinates, and clears the tile's unit-present bit if this was the last unit. In multiplayer, synchronizes with server.
summaries.005B345F: Places a unit on the map at a given position. Links it into the existing unit stack at that tile, sets the tile's unit-present bit and owner nibble.
summaries.005B36DF: Moves a unit from its current position to a new position by picking it up and putting it down. Handles MP synchronization.
summaries.005B3863: Relocates a unit to its own current position (used to refresh stack linkage).
summaries.005B389F: Moves a unit to the bottom of its stack (last position). Picks it up, finds the current last unit, links it after the last.
summaries.005B3AE0: Relocates all units in a stack to a new position.
summaries.005B3B78: Ejects all sea-domain units from a stack to offscreen holding coordinates, then relocates them back to the original position. Used when a carrier is sunk to handle its cargo.
summaries.005B3CD4: Ejects air units and returns the first unit remaining in the stack.
summaries.005B3D06: Creates a new unit of the specified type for a given civilization at a map position. Finds a free unit slot, initializes all fields (type, owner, position, orders, movement, home city, etc.), places it on the map, and handles multiplayer synchronization. Triggers tutorial messages for first units of
summaries.005B4391: Deletes a unit. Picks it up from the map, decrements all counters, marks the slot as dead, clears goto orders of other units targeting this one, updates city displays, and triggers civ death check if this was the last settler with no cities.
summaries.005B47FA: Deletes every unit in a stack by iterating from first to last.
summaries.005B488A: Clears a unit's visibility mask (which civs can see it).
summaries.005B48B1: Clears visibility for all units in a stack.
summaries.005B490E: Marks a unit as seen by a specific civilization (sets the civ's bit in the visibility mask). No-op if the civ is the unit's own owner.
summaries.005B496E: Sets visibility for all units in a stack to be seen by a specific civ.
summaries.005B49CF: Checks if a unit at (param_1, param_2) moving for civ param_3 would violate zone-of-control rules. Examines 8 adjacent tiles for enemy units/cities that are not at peace. Returns true if ZOC violation found.
summaries.005B4B66: Simple check for adjacent enemy units — no ocean/continent checks. Returns true if any adjacent tile has units from a civ not at peace with param_3.
summaries.005B4C63: Like check_adjacent_enemy_simple but also checks that the enemy is on the same landmass (ocean type match).
summaries.005B4D8C: Checks ZOC only if there's no city at the location. Returns 0 if city present (cities negate ZOC).
summaries.005B4DE2: Returns true if civ param_3 has any unit or city adjacent to tile (param_1, param_2).
summaries.005B4EE2: OR's a visibility bitmask into every unit in a stack.
summaries.005B4F3C: Returns a bitmask of all civilizations with presence on or adjacent to a tile.
summaries.005B4FCA: Updates a unit's visibility by checking all 8 adjacent civs and marking the stack as seen by each.
summaries.005B503B: Returns 1 if the stack contains a unit of the specified type, 0 otherwise.
summaries.005B50AD: Sums a property across all units in a stack. param_2 selects which property: 0=shield cost, 1=defense, 2/11=count, 3=attack, 4=carried air units, 5=ship count, 6=transport capacity (ships add capacity, ground units subtract), 7=bomber count, 8=nuclear count, 9=can-build-city count, 10=can-found-city
summaries.005B53B6: Counts units in a stack that have a specific role.
summaries.005B542E: Loads ground/air units onto a transport ship. In mode param_2=1 (physical loading), ejects air units first, then iterates the remaining stack looking for units that should board: first pass loads units with matching goto orders or those the transport is supposed to carry; second pass loads overflow.
summaries.005B5BAB: Stacks a unit (puts it into storage). If the unit is a ship, calls load_unit_onto_ship to load carried units. Otherwise, relocates the unit to offscreen holding coordinates.
summaries.005B5D93: Safely deletes a unit, handling the case where it's a ship carrying units. If the ship is on ocean and sinks, unloads and deletes all carried units first. If on land, just unloads them. Then deletes the unit itself.
summaries.005B6042: Deletes a unit and refreshes the map display at its former position. Sends MP tile-refresh notification.
summaries.005B620A: For all ships in a stack, attempts to auto-load nearby ground units onto them.
summaries.005B62EE: Sets the orders byte for all units in a stack.
summaries.005B633F: Returns 1 if a unit is ready to receive movement orders: must be alive, on valid map tile, owned by current active civ, not on goto/fortify, has movement remaining, and not marked as "done this turn" (bit 2 in flags).
summaries.005B6458: Returns 1 if a unit is "active" — alive, has valid position, not on goto, and has remaining movement.
summaries.005B6512: Finds the next unit needing orders, prioritizing by distance from the current cursor position. Nearest ready units come first; sea units are slightly deprioritized. Runs up to 3 passes, clearing "wait" flags on later passes to find more units.
summaries.005B6787: Refreshes a unit's movement by setting movement_spent to total_movement (i.e., the unit has already spent all its movement — this is used to mark "movement refreshed" in a way where remaining = total - spent would be 0; however, looking at the logic, it sets the byte to the return of calc_unit_movem
summaries.005B67AF: Finds the nearest unit to a position, optionally filtered by owner civ. Returns unit index or -1.
summaries.005B6898: Returns the name string of a unit's home city, or a "NONE" string if homeless.
summaries.005B68F6: Checks if a settler/engineer unit can perform a specific terrain improvement at a location. Returns 0 if no improvement possible, 1 for irrigation, 2 for mining. Checks terrain compatibility, existing improvements, and tech prerequisites.
summaries.005B6A58: Clears a unit's orders and removes the automation flag.
summaries.005B6AB5: Draws a unit sprite in a dialog at the specified position.
summaries.005B6AEA: Shows a scrolling dialog listing all units in a stack with their details (civ name, veteran flag, type name, home city, trade good for caravans). Returns the index of the selected unit or -1.
summaries.005B7FE0: Allocates all map data arrays: tile data (6 bytes per tile), per-civ visibility (1 byte per tile x 7 civs), and 4 auxiliary maps (fertility, continent, owner, city-radius-owner). Initializes tile terrain to ocean (10). Calls crash handler on allocation failure.
summaries.005B8416: Frees all map data arrays. Unlocks and deallocates each map buffer.
summaries.005B85FE: Initializes the map resource seed from the current time.
summaries.005B8635: Saves map data to a file. Writes 14-byte map header, per-civ visibility arrays (or seed coordinate data in scenario mode), and 6-byte-per-tile map data.
summaries.005B8783: Loads map data from a save file. Reads header, allocates new map arrays, reads per-civ visibility and tile data.
summaries.005B8931: Returns pointer to 6-byte tile data for map position (param_1, param_2). Returns pointer to dummy tile if coordinates are off-map. Formula: base + (width/2 * y + x/2) * 6.
summaries.005B898B: Returns pointer to a civ's visibility byte for a tile. Formula: civ_vis_base[civ] + (width/2) * y + x/2.
summaries.005B89BB: Returns the raw terrain byte (byte 0 of tile data, masked to lower 4 bits = terrain type without special resource bit).
summaries.005B8A1D: Returns the owner civ index for a tile (upper nibble of byte 5, >> 4). Returns -1 if off-map or value is 0xf (no owner).
summaries.005B8A81: Returns byte 3 of tile data (continent/landmass ID).
summaries.005B8AA8: Returns continent ID only if tile is not ocean, otherwise -1.
summaries.005B8AF0: Returns upper 3 bits of byte 2 (>> 5) = city radius owner.
summaries.005B8B1A: Updates a civ's visibility data for a tile by copying byte 1 of tile data to the civ's visibility map.
summaries.005B8B65: Returns whether a tile has been explored by a specific civ (checks bit in byte 4 corresponding to civ index).
summaries.005B8BAC: Sets or clears the explored bit for a specific civ on a tile.
summaries.005B8C18: Returns lower 4 bits of byte 5 (fertility value 0-15).
summaries.005B8C42: Returns city_radius_owner if nonzero; otherwise returns fertility (clamped: if 0 < fertility < 9, returns 8).
summaries.005B8CA6: Returns the city-owning civ at a tile, or -1. Checks tile improvement bits: if (byte1 & 0x42) == 0x02, it's a city tile, and returns the owner.
summaries.005B8D15: Returns the fortress-owning civ at a tile, or -1. Checks if (byte1 & 0x42) == 0x42 (fortress).
summaries.005B8D62: Returns the civ with units at a tile, or -1. Checks if byte1 bit 0 is set (unit present).
summaries.005B8DA4: Returns the controlling civ at a tile — city owner first, then unit owner.
summaries.005B8DEC: Checks if civ param_3 is trespassing at tile (param_1, param_2). Returns -1 if at war with owner, owner index if treaty violation (peace but not alliance), or -1 for no trespass.
summaries.005B8EE1: Checks if a tile has a special resource. Uses a deterministic hash of tile coordinates and map seed to determine resource placement. Returns 0 (none), 1 (resource type 1), or 2 (resource type 2). Returns 0 for ocean or tiles with resource-suppression flag (bit 0x40 in byte 0).
summaries.005B8FFA: Checks if a tile has a goody hut (village). Similar hash-based formula to resource placement, but different constants and requires tile to be land with no owner.
summaries.005B90DF: Reveals pollution on a tile by setting the pollution bit (0x80) in tile improvements. Updates visibility for all 7 civs. Increments global pollution counter.
summaries.005B9179: Generates/randomizes terrain around a nuclear detonation site. For each of 9 tiles (center + 8 adjacent): if no city, randomizes improvements (removes fortress, randomly removes roads/irrigation/mining, adds pollution 2/3 of the time). If city present, halves its population.
summaries.005B9431: Checks if a specific technology has been discovered by a civ. Returns nonzero if the tech bit is set.
summaries.005B94D5: Returns byte 1 of tile data (improvement flags: bit 0=unit present, bit 1=city, bit 2=irrigation, bit 3=mining, bit 4=road, bit 5=railroad, bit 6=fortress, bit 7=pollution).
summaries.005B94FC: Sets or clears improvement bits on a tile. If the value actually changed and in multiplayer, queues or sends a map update packet (0x90).
summaries.005B9646: Sets the terrain type for a tile (lower 4 bits of byte 0). Sends MP notification (0x91) if changed.
summaries.005B976D: Sets or clears visibility bits (byte 4) on a tile. Sends MP notification (0x92) if changed.
summaries.005B98B7: Sets the fertility value (lower 4 bits of byte 5). MP notification 0x93.
summaries.005B99E8: Sets the tile owner (upper nibble of byte 5). Values 0-7 for civs, 0xf for no owner. MP notification 0x94.
summaries.005B9B35: Sets the continent/landmass ID (byte 3). MP notification 0x95.
summaries.005B9C49: Sets the city-radius owner for a tile (top 3 bits of byte 2). MP notification 0x96.
summaries.005B9D81: Sets a civ's tile visibility byte. Can either overwrite or OR with existing value. MP notification 0x97.
summaries.005B9EC6: Begins a batched map update session for multiplayer. Disables immediate sending and enables queuing.
summaries.005B9F1C: Ends a batched map update. If queued updates exist (DAT_006365f4 > 1), sends them as a batch packet (0x59) and flushes. Otherwise just flushes pending messages.
summaries.005B9FDE: Queues a single map update operation into the batch buffer. If buffer would overflow (> 0x100 entries), flushes the current batch first. Each update type (0-7) has a different number of parameters.
summaries.005BA206: Applies a batch of queued map updates received from the server. Iterates the batch buffer, dispatching each update to the appropriate tile-setter function based on type (0=improvements, 1=terrain, 2=visibility, 3=fertility, 4=owner, 5=continent, 6=city_radius, 7=civ_vis_data).
summaries.005BAD40: Parses a binary string ("0"/"1" characters) into an integer value. Non-binary characters terminate parsing.
summaries.005BADF0: Builds a file path by concatenating a directory (param_2) with a filename (param_3), adding backslash separator if needed. Uppercases the result.
summaries.005BAEB0: Sets the target surface for text drawing.
summaries.005BAEC8: Sets the source font surface for text drawing.
summaries.005BAEE0: Configures text rendering style: foreground color, shadow color, and optional shadow offsets.
summaries.005BAF24: Enables or disables text outline mode.
summaries.005BAF57: Draws text with optional shadow and outline effects. Returns x position after rendering.
summaries.005BB024: Draws text centered horizontally within a given width.
summaries.005BB0AF: Draws text right-aligned within a given width.
summaries.005BB3F0: Creates an offscreen rendering surface with specified dimensions and palette.
summaries.005BB463: Creates offscreen surface then applies an additional setting.
summaries.005BB4AE: Creates an offscreen surface variant with 8 parameters (includes parent window).
summaries.005BB525: Creates offscreen surface B then applies setting.
summaries.005BB574: Sets the active rendering surface (from the object's member at ECX+0x48) and triggers a refresh callback.
summaries.005BB621: Resizes a window/surface object. If the new size matches the current active view, returns early. Otherwise sets the new rect, updates the active surface pointer, calls the refresh callback, and ends paint.
summaries.005BB6C7: Scrolls a surface to position (param_1, param_2), clamping to the allowed scroll range.
summaries.005BB760: Creates a child surface window with 6 parameters, then initializes its size and links it to the parent.
summaries.005BB7C3: Creates a child surface via FUN_005bb760 (6 params), then applies an additional configuration via param_7.
summaries.005BB80A: Creates a child surface with 7 parameters using an alternate creation function FUN_005c592b, then sets size and parent link.
summaries.005BB871: Creates a child surface via FUN_005bb80a (7 params), then applies additional configuration via param_8.
summaries.005BB8C0: Returns the HWND stored at offset +4 of the object (ECX).
summaries.005BB8E0: Sets the surface display size by delegating to resize_window_client (FUN_005bc505) using the HWND at ECX+8.
summaries.005BB910: Returns the minimum scroll position (x, y) from the surface object.
summaries.005BB950: Returns the maximum scroll position (x, y) from the surface object.
summaries.005BB990: Invokes the refresh callback function pointer stored at ECX+0x110, if non-null.
summaries.005BB9C0: Drains all pending mouse messages (0x200-0x209) and keyboard messages (0x100-0x108) from the message queue.
summaries.005BBA1D: Drains all pending messages from the message queue.
summaries.005BBA4F: Peeks and dispatches a single message. If no message is pending, calls idle processing. Returns 1 if a message was dispatched, 0 otherwise.
summaries.005BBAB8: Peeks and dispatches a single WM_PAINT message (0x0F). Returns true if a paint message was dispatched.
summaries.005BBB0A: Processes all pending messages by calling gdi_BA4F in a loop until no messages remain, then flushes GDI.
summaries.005BBB32: Processes all pending WM_PAINT messages by calling gdi_BAB8 in a loop, then flushes GDI.
summaries.005BBB5A: Launches an external program using WinExec with SW_SHOWNORMAL display mode.
summaries.005BBB76: Peeks and dispatches a single WM_PALETTECHANGED message (0x3BD). Returns true if one was found and dispatched.
summaries.005BBBCE: Processes all pending palette change messages, then optionally runs refresh logic.
summaries.005BBC10: Creates a Win32 window with extensive style configuration based on bitflags in param_2, allocates a window object struct, and returns it.
summaries.005BBFEE: Enables or disables a window. Null-safe wrapper around Win32 EnableWindow.
summaries.005BC019: Sets a user data value at offset +0x40 of the window object.
summaries.005BC032: Returns 1 if the window is visible, 0 if null or hidden.
summaries.005BC07E: Sends WM_CLOSE (0x10) to a window. Null-safe.
summaries.005BC0AB: Destroys a window object: pops from window stack, hides and destroys the Win32 window, deletes GDI objects, frees memory.
summaries.005BC173: Iterates over the window stack (empty loop body). Appears to be a placeholder or stripped function.
summaries.005BC1B5: Pushes a window handle onto the global window stack array.
summaries.005BC1DB: Removes a specific window handle from the window stack array by finding and shifting elements.
summaries.005BC280: Creates a palette-aware background brush for a window. Uses either direct RGB or palette-indexed color depending on DAT_00638b48 palette mode flag.
summaries.005BC35E: Creates an RGB background brush from explicit R/G/B parameters and invalidates the window.
summaries.005BC3BF: Sets a callback function pointer at offset +0x24 of the window object.
summaries.005BC3D8: Sets a callback function pointer at offset +0x28 of the window object.
summaries.005BC3F1: Sets a callback function pointer at offset +0x2c of the window object.
summaries.005BC40A: Shows a window (SW_SHOW=5) and optionally brings it to the top of the Z-order.
summaries.005BC476: Sets the title/text of a window. Null-safe wrapper around SetWindowTextA.
summaries.005BC4A1: Moves a window to position (param_2, param_3) while keeping its current width and height.
summaries.005BC505: Resizes a window's client area to (param_2 x param_3) pixels, accounting for border/frame sizes.
summaries.005BC603: Returns whether a window is maximized. Returns 0 if null.
summaries.005BC65F: Returns whether a window is minimized (iconic). Returns 0 if null.
summaries.005BC692: Restores a window from minimized/maximized state (SW_RESTORE=9). Null-safe.
summaries.005BC6BB: Validates (marks as not needing repaint) a rectangle of the window. Null-safe.
summaries.005BC6E6: Invalidates a rectangle of the window (no erase). Null-safe.
summaries.005BC713: Moves and resizes a window to match a RECT structure. Null-safe.
summaries.005BC763: Centers a window relative to its parent (or screen if no parent), with optional offset (param_2, param_3).
summaries.005BC933: Returns the client area width of a window. Returns 0 if null.
summaries.005BC96B: Returns the client area height of a window. Returns 0 if null.
summaries.005BC9A3: Gets the screen-coordinates bounding rectangle of a window. Null-safe.
summaries.005BC9D3: Returns the difference between window rect width and client rect width (i.e., the total horizontal non-client area / borders). Returns 0 if null.
summaries.005BCA3D: Returns the difference between window rect height and client rect height (i.e., total vertical non-client area / title + borders). Returns 0 if null.
summaries.005BCAA7: Fills a RECT with the full screen dimensions (0, 0, screen_width, screen_height).
summaries.005BCAD7: Converts client coordinates (param_2, param_3) to screen coordinates, returning results via param_4 and param_5. Null-safe.
summaries.005BCB26: Maps a point from window coordinates to its parent window's coordinates. Null-safe.
summaries.005BCB85: Gets a window's rectangle in its parent's coordinate space. Null-safe.
summaries.005BCC11: Performs a BitBlt from a source surface to a destination window DC, with palette realization if in palette mode.
summaries.005BCC8D: Performs a BitBlt from source to destination using the alternate DC slot (field +4 instead of +8). No palette realization.
summaries.005BCCE2: Performs a StretchBlt from source to destination window DC, with palette realization if in palette mode.
summaries.005BCD66: Performs a StretchBlt from source to destination using the alternate DC slot (field +4). No palette realization.
summaries.005BCDC3: Selects a palette into a window's DC, but only if the system is in palette mode (DAT_00638b48 == 1).
summaries.005BCDFC: Sets a menu on a window and adjusts the window size to account for the menu bar height.
