summaries.005BCE5F: Loads an icon resource by ID and sets it on the window. If the window is minimized (iconic), invalidates it to redraw with the new icon.
summaries.005BCEEE: Loads a cursor resource by ID from the application instance.
summaries.005BCF1C: Destroys a cursor handle if non-null.
summaries.005BCF40: Sets the current mouse cursor. Simple wrapper around Win32 SetCursor.
summaries.005BCF5A: Stores a cursor handle on the window object and optionally activates it immediately.
summaries.005BCFA0: Shows the mouse cursor by calling ShowCursor(TRUE).
summaries.005BCFB8: Hides the mouse cursor by repeatedly calling ShowCursor(FALSE) until the display counter goes negative.
summaries.005BCFDD: Loads a cursor by resource ID onto a window and optionally activates it.
summaries.005BD023: Stores a cursor handle on the window object and immediately activates it. Null-safe for both window and cursor.
summaries.005BD05F: Loads a cursor by ID or special value and stores it on the window object. Supports standard system cursors and application-defined cursors.
summaries.005BD0E7: Gives keyboard focus to a window and brings it to the top of the Z-order. Null-safe.
summaries.005BD120: Gives keyboard focus to a window. Null-safe.
summaries.005BD14C: Disables the parent (or specified owner) window to create a modal-like effect.
summaries.005BD1C5: Re-enables the parent (or specified owner) window, reversing the modal effect.
summaries.005BD248: Sets a callback function pointer at offset +0x38 of the window object. Null-safe.
summaries.005BD270: Sets a callback function pointer at offset +0x3c of the window object. Null-safe.
summaries.005BD298: Waits for a mouse button release by polling the message queue. Returns 1 when the specified button is released.
summaries.005BD39E: Checks keyboard/mouse modifier state or processes queued system messages. The check mode is selected by param_1.
summaries.005BD48F: Gets the current mouse cursor position in client coordinates of the specified window.
summaries.005BD4CD: Returns true if the left mouse button is currently held down.
summaries.005BD500: Returns true if the right mouse button is currently held down.
summaries.005BD533: Captures mouse input to the specified window.
summaries.005BD566: Sets a child window as the topmost child within its parent. Validates parent-child relationship and SWS_ATTACHED style flag.
summaries.005BD610: Returns the first DWORD of the ECX object (typically the vtable pointer or style flags).
summaries.005BD630: Constructs a port object by setting its vtable pointer and initializing fields via FUN_005bd813.
summaries.005BD65C: Allocates a port surface from width and height dimensions by creating a RECT and delegating to port_alloc.
summaries.005BD696: Full port allocation: frees any existing DIB, creates a new DIB section of the specified size, and initializes pixel stride.
summaries.005BD7DB: Loads an image into a port by first calling a setup function, then loading via load_cvpc_resource.
summaries.005BD813: Initializes all fields of a port object, either to zero (if param_1 is null) or from the dimensions of the provided RECT.
summaries.005BD915: Destroys a port object: unlocks the surface if locked, frees the DIB, resets all fields, and clears the singleton pointer if this was it.
summaries.005BD987: Loads an IFF/LBM (Interleaved Bitmap) image from a resource. Parses FORM/ILBM/PBM chunks, extracts BMHD, CMAP, and BODY data, and decodes pixels into the port buffer.
summaries.005BDF7F: Decodes a single LBM scanline, handling both planar (ILBM) and chunky (PBM) pixel formats, with optional RLE decompression.
summaries.005BE1B3: Decodes a single row of RLE-compressed data. Supports uncompressed (type 0) and PackBits RLE (type 1).
summaries.005BE2C4: Loads a TGA (Targa) image from a resource. Supports uncompressed 8-bit images with optional color palette and vertical flip.
summaries.005BE595: Loads a TGA image from a file. Same logic as load_tga_resource but uses file I/O instead of resource lookup.
summaries.005BE940: SEH cleanup helper for the TGA file loader. Calls the common cleanup function FUN_005d7c6e.
summaries.005BE956: SEH frame restoration for the TGA file loader. Restores the FS:[0] exception handler chain.
summaries.005BE967: Loads a PCX image from a resource. Supports 256-color 8-bit PCX with RLE compression and embedded palette.
summaries.005BEC8C: Loads a PCX image from a file. Same logic as load_pcx_resource but uses file I/O.
summaries.005BF04A: SEH cleanup helper for the PCX file loader. Calls the common cleanup function.
summaries.005BF060: SEH frame restoration for the PCX file loader.
summaries.005BF071: Loads a GIF image from a file. Validates GIF signature, reads global color table, finds image block, and decompresses LZW data into the port buffer.
summaries.005BF5BA: SEH cleanup helper for the GIF file loader.
summaries.005BF5D0: SEH frame restoration for the GIF file loader.
summaries.005BF5E1: Loads a GIF image from a resource. Same GIF parsing and LZW decompression as load_gif_file but reads from resource data.
summaries.005BF930: Loads a CvPc (Civilization custom compressed picture) image from a resource. Reads header with byte-swapped width/height, palette, and LZW-compressed pixel data.
summaries.005BFAD9: Loads a BMP (Windows Bitmap) image from a resource. Supports uncompressed 8-bit bitmaps with 256-color palette.
summaries.005BFCFF: Loads a BMP image from a file. Same logic as load_bmp_resource but reads from file via Realloc/FUN_005c5470.
summaries.005C0023: Restores FS:[0] from saved SEH chain pointer at EBP-0xC.
summaries.005C0034: Sets the port's clip rect (this+0x14) from its own bounds rect (this+0x24..0x30).
summaries.005C0073: Sets the port's clip rect from param_1 (4 ints), then clips it against the port's bounds rect.
summaries.005C00CE: Copies the port's clip rect (this+0x14..0x20) into the output parameter.
summaries.005C0105: Dispatches picture loading by format type stored at this+0x44. Switch on 1=BMP resource, 2=GIF resource, 3=GIF LZW, 4=picture file.
summaries.005C019D: Wrapper that calls FUN_005bd696 with param_1.
summaries.005C01C1: Locks the port's surface buffer. Builds a row-pointer table at this+0x38 so each row of the bitmap can be accessed by index.
summaries.005C02E0: Unlocks the port's surface, freeing the row pointer table.
summaries.005C0333: Fills a rectangle in the port with a given color index. Auto-locks if not locked.
summaries.005C041F: Fills the entire clip rect with a given color. Calls port_fill_rect with this+0x14 as the rect.
summaries.005C044A: Wrapper calling port_copy_rect with the clip rect and two color params.
summaries.005C0479: Replaces all pixels of color param_2 with param_3 within the given rectangle.
summaries.005C0593: Copies/blits a rectangle from the source port (this) to a destination port (param_1), with clipping on both sides.
summaries.005C0753: Copies from the port to the screen surface. Has a fast path for 320px width (direct copy) and a general path (scaled blit).
summaries.005C0979: Copies from the port to the screen with palette selection and clipping.
summaries.005C0A55: Copies from port to screen rect with source and destination rect clipping.
summaries.005C0B2C: Stretched blit from port to screen with both source and dest rect params.
summaries.005C0BF2: Gets a single pixel value at (param_1, param_2). Returns 0 if out of clip rect bounds.
summaries.005C0C5D: Sets a single pixel value at (param_1, param_2) to param_3. No-op if out of clip rect.
summaries.005C0CC5: Selects a palette on the port's surface if it differs from the current one.
summaries.005C0D12: Same as FUN_005c0cc5 but with null check on param_1.
summaries.005C0D69: Draws text at (param_2, param_3) with optional shadow (bit 0x10 of param_4). Uses the current font.
summaries.005C0E57: Draws text into a rect with optional shadow. Similar to port_draw_text_styled but uses a rect for positioning.
summaries.005C0F57: Draws text with a specific font (param_1 points to font handle). Optional shadow via bit 0x10 of param_5.
summaries.005C1020: Draws text into a rect with a specific font and optional shadow.
summaries.005C10FB: Measures text string width/height using the current font.
summaries.005C1167: Measures text with a specific font (handle from param_1[0]).
summaries.005C11B2: Draws a line from (param_1,param_2) to (param_3,param_4). Handles horizontal, vertical, and diagonal lines.
summaries.005C145D: Draws a rectangle outline using 4 line-drawing calls.
summaries.005C1513: Draws a 3D raised/beveled rectangle (button-up appearance). In 8-bit mode uses palette indices; in true color uses RGB values.
summaries.005C1742: Draws a 3D sunken/beveled rectangle (button-down appearance). Reverses the highlight/shadow colors from port_load_tga.
summaries.005C1972: Sets the global port context pointer DAT_00637e58 to param_1, returns old value.
summaries.005C1998: Returns the current global port context pointer DAT_00637e58.
summaries.005C19AD: Sets the global draw color byte DAT_00637e78 to param_1, returns old value.
summaries.005C19D3: Returns a pointer to the pixel at (param_1, param_2) in the locked port buffer.
summaries.005C1A00: Checks if (param_1, param_2) is within the port's clip rect. Returns 1 if in bounds, 0 otherwise.
summaries.005C1A62: Remaps pixel colors in the port's locked buffer using a lookup table. For each pixel, if (pixel - param_2) >= 0, replaces it with table[pixel - param_2].
summaries.005C1B0D: Allocates a GIF-format port buffer of given width and height.
summaries.005C1B47: Allocates a DIB surface for the port from a given rect. Sets format type to 2 (GIF).
summaries.005C1CBD: Allocates a port buffer of given width/height (GIF LZW format type 3).
summaries.005C1CF7: Allocates a DIB for format type 3 (GIF LZW). Same structure as port_load_gif_resource but uses create_dib_43C5.
summaries.005C1E6D: Allocates a port buffer of given width/height (format type 4).
summaries.005C1EA7: Allocates a DIB for format type 4. Same structure as port_load_gif_resource.
summaries.005C201D: Converts RGB888 to RGB555 (16-bit pixel format).
summaries.005C2048: Loads a TGA image from a "TARG" resource chunk into the port. Supports 16/24/32 bpp uncompressed TGA.
summaries.005C2360: Loads a TGA image from a file (opened by Realloc). Same pixel conversion as tga_decode_rle but file-based.
summaries.005C27AD: Loads a BMP bitmap from a resource into the port (16-bit surface). Supports 8/24bpp source BMPs.
summaries.005C2A77: Loads a BMP bitmap from a file into the port. Same as bmp_load_from_resource but file-based.
summaries.005C2E5E: Loads a GIF image from a "GIFS" resource into the port. Decodes LZW-compressed GIF data.
summaries.005C3313: Loads a GIF image from a file into the port. File-based version of gif_decode_resource.
summaries.005C3874: Loads a "CvPc" (Civ Picture) resource into the port. Custom compressed picture format with embedded palette.
summaries.005C3EFC: Loads a BMP from resource into a 24-bit port surface. Swaps BGR to RGB byte order.
summaries.005C4375: Loads a TGA from "TARG" resource into a 24-bit port. Swaps BGR to RGB.
summaries.005C4A27: Fills a rect with an RGB color. In 16-bit mode uses FUN_005e4ef8; in true color uses FUN_005e4c3f.
summaries.005C4B4C: Fills entire clip rect with RGB color. Calls port_fill_rect_clipped with this+0x14.
summaries.005C4B7F: Copies from source port to dest port (8-bit blit), clipping both rects.
summaries.005C4C46: Copies from a screen-type source to port, clipping both rects.
summaries.005C4D0D: Stretched blit from screen source to port, with auto-lock.
summaries.005C4DD3: Draws text with shadow using RGB color params. Similar to port_draw_text_styled but with explicit shadow color params.
summaries.005C4EB6: Draws text in rect with shadow. Rect-based version of text_draw_with_shadow.
summaries.005C4F9F: Like text_draw_with_shadow but with explicit font handle from param_1.
summaries.005C505D: Like text_draw_in_rect_with_shadow but with explicit font handle.
summaries.005C512D: Measures a text string using the current font. Outputs dimensions via params.
summaries.005C51A5: Measures text with explicit font handle from param_1.
summaries.005C51FC: Draws an RGB-colored line from (param_1,param_2) to (param_3,param_4) with clip rect checking.
summaries.005C52DD: Draws a rectangle outline using 4 RGB line-drawing calls.
summaries.005C53C3: Applies a color effect to the port's pixel data. Computes bits-per-pixel from format type.
summaries.005C5410: Byte-swaps a 16-bit value. Returns CONCAT11(low_byte, high_byte).
summaries.005C5470: Gets the data pointer for the currently-open file. Wrapper for FUN_005d89e8.
summaries.005C54A0: Releases the file data pointer. Wrapper for FUN_005d8ab8.
summaries.005C54F0: Returns true if the port's surface buffer (this+0x34) is non-null (i.e., locked).
summaries.005C5520: Releases a resource handle. Wrapper for FUN_005db1e0.
summaries.005C5540: Finds a named chunk in a resource. Wrapper for FUN_005db1fa.
summaries.005C5560: Locks a resource and returns data pointer. Wrapper for FUN_005db531.
summaries.005C5580: Unlocks a resource. Wrapper for FUN_005db54b.
summaries.005C55A0: Aligns a value up to the next multiple of 4. Used for BMP row alignment.
summaries.005C55F0: Returns this+0x40 (the surface/DIB handle). MFC library function misidentified by Ghidra.
summaries.005C5610: Advances a pixel pointer to the next row. Returns param_1 + this+0x10 (row stride).
summaries.005C56C0: Destructor for _Timevec. Calls FUN_005e92c9 to release the surface.
summaries.005C56F0: Returns this+0x408. In SMEDS32 context, returns the palette handle.
summaries.005C5710: Moves a pixel pointer to the previous row. Returns param_1 - this+0x10.
summaries.005C5740: Returns *this (first dword of object). Used to get surface pointer for screen blit.
summaries.005C59C4: Appends a surface node (param_1) to the linked list at this+0xB8. Walks to the end of the list via +0x20 next pointers.
summaries.005C5A27: Removes a surface node from the linked list at this+0xB8 by matching param_1 to node IDs via thunk_FUN_00418740.
summaries.005C5AEB: Iterates the surface list and removes each node.
summaries.005C5B36: Iterates the surface list calling invalidate_8B00 on each node's HWND at +0x1C.
summaries.005C5B7F: Walks the surface list looking for a dirty surface (via FUN_005c5ea0). If found and this+0xC4 is set, calls FUN_005eabcc to restore it.
summaries.005C5C2D: Walks the surface list, returns the first active (dirty) surface node, or 0 if none.
summaries.005C5C86: Searches the surface list for a control matching a lowercase name. Handles type 6 (button activate), type 2 (checkbox toggle), and type 3 (multi-control) nodes.
summaries.005C61B0: Runs a modal dialog loop. Pushes current dialog onto a stack (max 16 deep), enters message pump, exits when this+0x8C is cleared.
summaries.005C62CB: Sets this+0x8C to 0, which causes the modal_dialog_run loop to exit.
summaries.005C6303: Sets DAT_00637ea4 to param_1, returns old value.
summaries.005C63AF: If this+0xA0 is non-null, calls it as a function pointer with param_1. Returns whether callback existed.
summaries.005C8200: Creates a GDI font with specified family (0=Times New Roman, 1=Arial, 2=System, 3=Courier), size, and style (bold/italic/underline/strikeout bits).
summaries.005C8CAF: WndProc for basic custom controls. Handles WM_MOUSEMOVE (0x200), WM_LBUTTONDOWN (0x201), WM_LBUTTONUP (0x202), WM_LBUTTONDBLCLK (0x203). Manages mouse tracking state, captures, and dispatches to callbacks at offsets +0x2C through +0x3C.
summaries.005C90CA: WM_PAINT handler for controls. Gets parent window's surface, maps coordinates, selects palette if 8-bit mode, blits parent background.
summaries.005C9222: Parses a '~' character in the label string to determine the hotkey position. Stores hotkey index at param_1+0x28, lowercase hotkey char at param_1+0x25. Removes the '~' from the string.
summaries.005C9307: Default WndProc for MSControlClass windows. Handles WM_PAINT (calls update_palette_90CA), WM_DESTROY (frees instance data), and keyboard messages 0x100-0x102 (forwards to parent).
summaries.005C944B: Destroys the window for a control node unless it's type 3.
summaries.005C9499: Allocates a 0x30-byte instance data block for a control window. Stores the memory handle, zeros out callback slots, and sets it as the window's GWL_USERDATA (offset 0) via SetWindowLongA.
summaries.005C9563: Gets control instance data from window via GetWindowLongA(hwnd, 0). Returns 0 for null hwnd.
summaries.005C9595: Frees control instance data. Deletes GDI bitmaps at offsets +0xC, +0x10, +0x14 if owned. Frees allocated text at +0x24 if type 2. Frees the memory handle. Resets WndProc to send_msg_9307.
summaries.005C96CC: Gets client rect, invalidates it, and calls UpdateWindow for immediate repaint.
summaries.005C9710: Sets this+0x1C to param_1. MFC library function.
summaries.005C9740: Creates a 3-state button control with pre-rendered normal, hover, and disabled bitmaps. This is the main button creation function. Draws text centered with underline hotkey indicator. Creates 3 compatible bitmaps: normal (raised), pressed (sunken), disabled (grayed). Sets WndProc to blit_ACD4.
summaries.005CABF6: Logs error if param_1 is null (attempted disposal of null button).
summaries.005CAC22: Activates a button by keyboard. Sets focus, shows pressed state, waits for key release, then invokes callback.
summaries.005CACD4: WndProc for standard 3-state buttons. Handles WM_PAINT (blits appropriate bitmap), WM_SETFOCUS/WM_KILLFOCUS (focus rect), WM_KEYDOWN (space activates), WM_LBUTTONDOWN/UP (mouse click with tracking). Manages pressed/hover states.
summaries.005CB319: Creates a sprite-based button control. Pre-renders from two port surfaces (normal and pressed sprites). Sets WndProc to blit_B6EB.
summaries.005CB601: Creates a positioned sprite button with explicit x,y,w,h. Same as create_window_B319 but simpler.

summaries.005CBDB0: Returns this+0x404. Duplicate of the one at 0x005C56F0.
summaries.005CBEB0: Wrapper for FUN_005d4204 (likely a timer/delay function).
summaries.005CBED0: CRT initialization function. Calls FUN_005cbeea then FUN_005cbf04. Part of static initializer chain.
summaries.005CBF04: Registers FUN_005cbf21 as an atexit handler.
summaries.005CBF40: Measures text extent using GDI. Creates a compatible DC, selects the current font, calls GetTextExtentPoint and GetTextMetrics. Adds 0x25 to width, enforces minimum height of 0x14. Returns rect via param_4.
summaries.005CC035: Measures a control rect by font height only (no text measurement). Minimum height 0x14. Sets rect via param_4.
summaries.005CC0F0: Creates a checkbox control. Loads 3 bitmap resources (0x130/0x131/0x132 for unchecked/checked/disabled states). Stores label text in allocated memory. Sets WndProc to draw_text_C320.
summaries.005CC248: Logs error if param_1 is null (attempted disposal of null checkbox).
summaries.005CC274: Toggles a checkbox and invokes its callback. Sets focus, toggles state, captures mouse, invalidates for repaint, dispatches callback.
summaries.005CC320: WndProc for checkbox controls. Handles WM_PAINT (draws checkbox bitmap + label text with hotkey underline), WM_KEYDOWN (space toggles), WM_LBUTTONDOWN/UP (mouse toggle), WM_SETFOCUS/KILLFOCUS (focus rect). Supports 8-bit palette mode and true-color. Handles enabled/disabled text color and shadow tex
summaries.005CCDDF: Sets the enabled control text color (R, G, B) and enables the custom color flag.
summaries.005CCE0E: Sets the disabled control text color (R, G, B) and enables the custom color flag.
summaries.005CCE40: If this+0x2C is non-null, calls it as a function pointer with (param_1, param_2).
summaries.005CCE80: Subclassed WndProc for native scrollbar controls. On WM_DESTROY, clears the control data. Forwards all messages to the original WndProc.
summaries.005CCF17: Creates a custom scrollbar control. On first call, creates a temporary SCROLLBAR, captures its WndProc and class extra bytes, then registers "MSScrollBarClass" with those params. Creates the custom scrollbar window with subclassed WndProc.
summaries.005CD149: Scrollbar message handler. Processes WM_COMMAND (0x111) to set position, and WM_HSCROLL/WM_VSCROLL (0x114/0x115) with sub-messages: SB_LINEUP(0), SB_LINEDOWN(1), SB_PAGEUP(2), SB_PAGEDOWN(3), SB_THUMBPOSITION(4), SB_THUMBTRACK(5). Dispatches to change/track callbacks.
summaries.005CD6E0: Initializes all 16 scale table slots to zero, then builds default tables with ratio 1:1.

summaries.005CDCDB: Fills a single scale table slot with Bresenham mapping for ratio param_2:param_3.
summaries.005CDE2C: Default CString constructor. Calls FUN_005d1b38 to initialize empty string.

summaries.005CF20A: Same as above but calls FUN_005d080d.

summaries.005CF439: Gets sprite scale factors from this+0x28 and this+0x2C.

summaries.005CF64C: Core sprite extraction function. Extracts sprite data from a locked port surface within a given rect. Scans for non-transparent pixels to find tight bounds. Builds compressed scan-line format (offset, length, pixel data per row). Handles three transparency modes: -1 (use first pixel as transparent),
summaries.005CFDEB: Same as FUN_005cf64c but extracts from the screen surface instead of a port. Gets screen buffer via FUN_005e6188/FUN_005e7028. Uses FUN_005d1ef0/FUN_005d1f20 for row navigation instead of port row pointers. Releases screen surface on completion.

summaries.00601CF0: Allocates a CRT file handle from the OS file handle table. Grows the table if needed (max 64 blocks of 32 handles each).
summaries.00601E40: Associates a Win32 handle with a CRT file handle. Updates std handles (stdin/stdout/stderr) if applicable.
summaries.00601F40: Frees a CRT file handle, setting its OS handle to -1.
summaries.00602060: Returns the Win32 handle for a CRT file handle.
summaries.006020E0: Creates a CRT file handle from an existing Win32 handle. Detects file type and sets appropriate flags.
summaries.006021E0: Flushes a file's buffers to disk via Win32 FlushFileBuffers.
summaries.006022C0: Returns nonzero if file handle refers to a character device (terminal).
summaries.00602310: Converts a wide character to multibyte. Uses WideCharToMultiByte for non-C locale.
summaries.006023E0: 64-bit unsigned integer division. Compiler helper function.
summaries.00602450: 64-bit unsigned integer remainder. Compiler helper function.
summaries.006024D0: Closes all open file streams except stdin/stdout/stderr.
summaries.00602590: Returns the floating-point status word in abstract format.
summaries.006025C0: Clears and returns the floating-point status word.
summaries.00602640: Wrapper for __control87 masking off denormal control bit.
summaries.00602670: Resets the floating-point processor to default state.
summaries.006026D0: Converts hardware FPU control word to abstract format (CRT internal representation).
summaries.00602820: Converts abstract FPU control word to hardware format and loads it.
summaries.006029F0: Converts hardware FPU status word to abstract format.
summaries.00602A90: Checks if all bits after a given position in a 96-bit mantissa are zero. Used in floating-point rounding.
summaries.00602B30: Increments a 96-bit mantissa at a specific bit position, propagating carry.
summaries.00602BF0: Rounds a 96-bit mantissa to a specified precision using banker's rounding (round-half-to-even).
summaries.00602D60: Returns 1 if 96-bit mantissa is all zeros.
summaries.00602DB0: Right-shifts a 96-bit mantissa by a specified number of bits.
summaries.00602EA0: Converts a 12-byte long double to either double (64-bit) or float (32-bit) format based on conversion parameters.
summaries.00603110: Converts 12-byte long double to double using DAT_0063b280 conversion parameters.
summaries.00603140: Converts 12-byte long double to float using DAT_0063b298 conversion parameters.
summaries.00603170: Converts 12-byte long double to 10-byte long double format.
summaries.00603300: Converts floating-point mantissa digits to a string representation. Handles rounding of the last digit.
summaries.006033E0: Float-to-string output helper. Converts double to 12-byte long double then to decimal string representation.
summaries.00603450: Converts a double-precision float to 12-byte long double internal format.
summaries.006035D0: Returns the length of a wide character string.
summaries.00603650: Initializes timezone settings from TZ environment variable or Win32 GetTimeZoneInformation. Parses TZ string for timezone name, offset, and DST rules.
summaries.006039B0: Determines if a given local time is within daylight saving time. Uses cached DST transition dates, recomputes if year changed.
summaries.00603D20: Converts DST transition specification (month, week, day-of-week or absolute day) to day-of-year and time-of-day values. Handles both relative (nth weekday) and absolute day formats.
summaries.00603F30: Changes file size. Can extend (by writing zero bytes) or truncate (via SetEndOfFile). Preserves file position.
summaries.006041E0: Adds two 32-bit unsigned integers, stores result, returns carry flag (0 or 1).
summaries.00604230: Adds two 96-bit integers (3 dwords each) with carry propagation.
summaries.006043E0: Converts a decimal digit string to 12-byte long double format. Processes digits via multiply-by-10-and-add loop, then normalizes.
summaries.00604520: Converts a string to 12-byte long double. Full parser: handles sign, integer part, decimal point, fractional part, exponent (E/e/D/d notation), and overflow/underflow. State machine with 12 states.
summaries.00605140: String to long double conversion wrapper.
summaries.006051A0: Converts a floating-point number to decimal string for printf-family functions. Handles special values (zero, INF, QNAN, SNAN, IND). Uses 12-byte long double arithmetic for precision.
summaries.006056E0: Converts wide character string to multibyte. Handles C locale (direct cast), single-byte codepages, and multibyte codepages with buffer overflow handling.
summaries.00605A40: Counts wide characters up to a maximum, stopping at null terminator.
summaries.00605AB0: Gets an environment variable value. Converts wide environment to multibyte on first call if needed.
summaries.00605BA0: Sets file translation mode (text 0x4000 or binary 0x8000). Returns previous mode.
summaries.00605CE0: Multiplies two 12-byte long doubles. Handles special cases (zero, overflow). Uses schoolbook multiplication with 16-bit limbs.
summaries.00606110: Multiplies a 12-byte long double by 10^n. Uses precomputed power-of-10 tables (positive at DAT_0063b380, negative at DAT_0063b4e0) with base-8 decomposition of the exponent.
summaries.006061F0: Case-insensitive multibyte string comparison using locale-aware collation.
summaries.00606260: Converts wide-character environment to multibyte, creating the narrow environment variable table.
summaries.00606330: Unicode string comparison with ANSI fallback. Auto-detects API support and caches result.
summaries.00606620: Counts wide characters up to max, stopping at null. (Duplicate of 0x00605A40 — different variant.)
summaries.00606690: ANSI string comparison with Unicode fallback. Handles edge cases with lead bytes in multibyte character sets. Auto-detects API support.
summaries.00606AF0: Counts characters in a string up to a maximum, stopping at null terminator.
summaries.00606B60: Sets or removes an environment variable. Handles both the CRT environment table and Win32 environment. Copies the environment on first write (copy-on-write).
summaries.00606ED0: Finds an environment variable by name in the CRT environment table. Returns index or negative count.
summaries.00606F70: Deep-copies the entire CRT environment table (array of string pointers).
summaries.00607070: Multibyte-aware character search. Handles lead bytes in DBCS character sets.
summaries.006075E0: Returns the length of a file in bytes using seek-to-end then restore.
summaries.006076A0: Returns the value at offset 0x10 of the object pointed to by param_1. Simple accessor.
summaries.006076C0: Converts a string to uppercase. Uses locale-aware LCMapStringA for non-C locales, direct ASCII conversion for C locale.
summaries.006077F0: Converts a string to lowercase. Uses locale-aware LCMapStringA for non-C locales, direct ASCII conversion for C locale.
summaries.00607920: Creates a directory. Wraps Win32 CreateDirectoryA with CRT error mapping.
summaries.0061A000: 16-bit segmented-mode function that performs a 2D tile/sprite copy or blitting operation. It reads pixel values from a source buffer, applies a modular transformation, then conditionally writes them to a destination buffer. Uses nested loops iterating over rows and columns.
summaries.0061A759: 16-bit segmented-mode function that decodes and blits an RLE (run-length encoded) sprite onto a destination buffer. It processes compressed sprite data row by row, handling transparency (skip) and pixel copy with a color key filter.
